gmw_00000496.name: Mrk 279
gmw_00000496.overview: Mrk 279 is a nearby Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1001.2712, arXiv:2109.03619] hosted by an S0/Sa galaxy [arXiv:2109.03619] with a moderately inclined disk [arXiv:1308.4129, arXiv:1809.05113]. The active nucleus harbors a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2405.08478] and exhibits X-ray flux and spectral variability [arXiv:2405.08478], alongside a complex, low-velocity outflow [arXiv:1804.05652].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes a long X-ray observation of Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 279, finding broad and narrow iron-region emission, small BLR contribution, and likely torus or accretion-disk origins.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a 160 ks-long \ observation of the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 279 . The spectrum shows evidence of both broad and narrow emission features. The \ line may be equally well explained by a single broad Gaussian (FWHM 10 000 ) or by two components: an unresolved core plus a very broad profile (FWHM 14 000 ).

For the first time we quantified, via the ``locally optimally emitting cloud" model, the contribution of the broad line region (BLR) to the absolute luminosity of the broad component of the \ at 6.4 keV. We find that the contribution of the BLR is only 3 . In the two-line component scenario, we also evaluated the contribution of the highly ionized gas component, which produces the \ line in the iron K region. This contribution to the narrow core of the \ line is marginal < 0.1 . Most of the luminosity of the unresolved, component of \ may come from the obscuring torus, while the very-broad associated component may come from the accretion disk.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 2018-2020 X-ray observations of Mrk 279, a nearby NLS1 galaxy, finding large flux and luminosity changes while spectral shape varies mainly over longer timescales.

quotes.quote: Markarian 279 (Mrk 279) is a nearby NLS1 galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.0305 scott2009variable harboring a supermassive black hole of mass M = 3.49 10^ 7 M_ at its centre peterson2004central . It has been observed extensively in the X-ray energies by various missions. Mrk 279 has also been observed multiple times by Chandra and XMM-Newton , independently yaqoob2004cores, jiang2019high, igo2020searching, ursini2020estimating . These observations have revealed that the X-ray flux of the source show significant variations over time scotttrinsic, costantini2010xmm, ebrero2010xmm .

In conclusion we find that, in agreement with the previous studies, Mrk 279 shows significant variations in its flux state and hence, its luminosity. This pattern is closely followed by the spectral components as well. We also notice that the photon index and hence the spectral shape follow the flux variations over longer periods of time. However, neither the index or the spectral shape is seen to exhibit the short term changes seen in the X-ray flux.

sources.summary: The paper studies the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 279 with Chandra and HST, using possible O v metastable absorption to constrain its warm absorber density and distance.

quotes.quote: Mrk 279 is a moderately distant ( z=0.0306 ) Seyfert 1 galaxy that we observed for 340 ks with the Chandra LETGS. This instrument has a spectral resolution of 0.05  \ FWHM. The full data analysis of the LETGS spectrum of Mrk 279 will be presented elsewhere (Costantini et al. \ ). Here we only summarize the results relevant for the study of the O v absorption lines. The LETGS spectrum was fit by a simple power law plus modified blackbody with Galactic absorption. This smooth continuum spectrum produces an excellent fit to the data except for the few regions where Mrk 279 has absorption lines.

Although not completely impossible, such a small radius is not very plausible, because Balmer line time-delay measurements suggest a broad emission line region size of 6--17 light days for Mrk 279 (Scott et al. \ and references therein), and UV spectra show that the broad lines are covered by the warm absorber.

Simultaneous HST observations of Mrk 279 (Gabel et al. ) yield an upper limit to the column density for level 5 deduced from the non-detection of this line of 5 10^ 16  m ^ 2 . Combined with the allowed range for the ground state column density ( 10^ 19.82 0.44  m ^ 2 as quoted before) this leads to an upper limit of 10^ 3 for the population of level 5 with respect to the ground.

sources.summary: The comment argues that Chandra data cannot directly determine Mrk 279 metal abundances, criticizes UV support for supersolar values, and reports UV-derived outflow abundance estimates.

quotes.quote: A recent manuscript posted on astro-ph (0611578) by Fields et al. (hereafter F06) reports evidence of supersolar metal abundances in Mrk 279 by analyzing its Chandra LETGS X-ray spectrum. We point out that it is impossible in principle to obtain direct metal abundances from these X-ray data, since there is no handle on the amount of hydrogen column density.

F06 find support for their supersolar metal abundances from a cursory analysis of the UV data from the same Mrk 279 campaign. We point out that F06 included in that analysis portions of the UV trough that are known to arise from gas unrelated to the outflow, which weakens the support from the UV data. A detailed analysis of the Chandra LETGS X-ray spectrum was accepted for publication in A A on Sept 14 2006 (Costantini et al 2006; hereafter C06) and posted on astro-ph on the same date. F06 ignore most of this published analysis while duplicating the finding of two ionization components with similar parameters to the ones found by C06. Finally, we note that it is possible to derive accurate abundances from the UV data set of this object.
We find that relative to solar the abundances in the Mrk 279 outflow are (linear scaling): carbon 2.2 0.7 , nitrogen 3.5 1.1 and oxygen 1.6 0.8 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Mrk 279 as a nearby Seyfert 1, finding short-timescale X-ray variability but no statistically significant warm-absorber changes over days or years.

quotes.quote: The Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 279 was observed by in November 2005 in three consecutive orbits, showing significant short-scale variability (average soft band variation in flux 20 ). The source is known to host a two-component warm absorber with distinct ionisation states from a previous observation. To study the warm absorber in Mrk 279 and investigate any possible response to the short-term variations of the ionising flux, and to assess whether it has varied on a long-term timescale with respect to the observation. The RGS spectra of Mrk 279 were analysed in both the high- and low-flux states using the SPEX fitting package.

Mrk 279 is a nearby ( z=0.0305 , Scott et al. ) Seyfert 1 galaxy ( F_ 0.5-2  keV 2 10^ 11  erg cm ^ 2  s ^ 1 , this work). The source was previously observed in X-rays by the HEAO 1 A-2 experiment (Weaver et al. ) and (Weaver et al. ) in order to study the spectral complexity of the Fe K emission line region. More recently, Mrk 279 was observed in X-rays using HETGS simultaneously with FUSE and HST-STIS in 2002 (Scott et al. ).

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 279 as a Seyfert 1 with complex iron-band emission, including narrow and broad components, and reflection modelling of soft excess and broad Fe K emission.

quotes.quote: Mrk 279 Previous analysis of the long \ observations of the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 279 veron06 in 2005 by costantini10 shows very complex emission features in the iron band, indicating both a broad Fe K emission line from the disc and a narrow Fe xxvi line potentially from the outer layer of the torus. A ratio plot of the stacked pn spectrum of Mrk 279 fitted with an absorbed power law is shown in Fig. pic_fe . The iron band of the spectrum shows two narrow emission features and a broad line component.

Based on the indication of the iron band, we model the broad band spectrum of Mrk 279 with MODEL1 . MODEL1 offers a very good fit with C-stat/ =155.59/177 . The best-fit model is shown in Fig. pic_final2 . The relativistic disc reflection model accounts for both the soft excess and the broad Fe K emission line in Mrk 279 A high BH spin of a_*>0.95 is required for the spectral modelling and only an upper limit of the disc density (n_ e )<16.9 is achieved.

sources.summary: The paper presents first photometric reverberation-mapping verification data for Mrk 279, measuring optical continuum light curves and inter-band delays with high-accuracy nuclear flux photometry.

quotes.quote: Here we present the first science verification data obtained for the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 279.
Mrk 279 lies at redshift z = 0.0306 and the bands 4300, 5700, 6200, and 7000 \ trace mainly the AGN continuum variations (Figure 1). The light curves of Mrk 279 are shown in Figure 2.

A more detailed study of the light curves of Mrk 279, including cross-correlation analysis will be presented in a forthcoming publication. We have presented the first science verification data of a new photometric RM monitoring of AGNs, using the robotic 46 cm telescope of the Wise Observatory in Israel. The use of image subtraction and aperture photometry techniques allows us to measure the nuclear flux of the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 279 with high accuracy ( < 1 ). We are able to measure inter-band time-delays of up to 2 days across the optical range.

sources.summary: The paper treats Mrk 279 as a luminous Sy1 AGN with weak PAH emission, warm-absorber features, and the sample’s shortest PAH half-lives from high X-ray photon flux.

quotes.quote: We applied our experimental data to estimate the stability of PAHs in the circumnuclear regions of six different AGNs (Table tab:agn ): Mrk 279, Mrk 334, Mrk 3, NGC 5728, NGC 7682, and NGC 1808. These AGNs were taken from the catalog, and all of them show emission in 8.6 m, which is due to ionized PAHs Draine2001, Draine2007, Sales2010,Sales2013 . The first two entries are Seyfert 1 (Sy1) AGNs, while the others are classified as Seyfert 2 (Sy2).

In contrast to NGC 1808, the shortest half-life times ( 10 ^2 yr) are obtained for Mrk 279, which presents the highest average X-ray photon flux at 2500 eV (6.68 10^ 8 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ). The absorption features of this very luminous Sy1 galaxy, which is located at 128.6 Mpc Scott2004 , are consistent with the presence of a warm absorber, most likely a dusty torus Ebrero2010 . Moreover, weak PAH emission features have been independently observed in Mrk 279 by different groups Santos-Lleo2001,Sales2010 .

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 279 with a well-fit line profile, captured large-scale variability, poorly constrained radial BLR shape, and thick slightly inclined disk geometry.

quotes.quote: Mrk 279 The \ line profile for \ is modeled very well, with only a slight discrepancy at the red side of the core of the line. The large timescale variations of the integrated \ line flux are well captured, but the model is unable to reproduce the smaller fluctuations on the order of days. The \ emitting region has a radial profile that is poorly determined, with anything from a narrow Gaussian to a steeper than exponential profile being allowed ( = ). The minimum radius is found to be large, with r_ min = \ light days. The 2D posterior distributions of these two parameters shows that smaller values of the minimum radius ( <5 light days) are allowed when < 0.5 (narrow Gaussian), but for wider Gaussian and steep exponential profiles, the minimum radius is robustly determined.
The opening angle and inclination angle for \ are _o = \ degrees and _i = \ degrees, respectively, indicating a thick disk that is slightly inclined relative to the observer.

sources.summary: Mrk 279 is included in the XMM-Newton Seyfert sample, but its hardness-ratio curves show no time-interval changes, so it is excluded from cloud-parameter estimates.

quotes.quote: None of the studied time-intervals show changes in the HR curves in Mrk 279 (see Fig. HRmrk279 ).

We also modeled the spectra of the three time-intervals for Mrk 279 and Mrk 766 considering the above components plus the pexrav model Magdziarz95 to account for neutral reflection. We modeled the spectrum of the three time intervals of both galaxies keeping all the parameters free. The parameters obtained with this analysis are given in Table Table22 . We notice that there is very little difference between the parameters listed in Table Table2 and those obtained using the pexrav component.
We have not included Mrk 279 in Table Table5 as this galaxy does not show changes in the HR curves, which is needed for deriving occultation times and variation in the covering factor.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 279 as a Seyfert 1 in a moderately inclined host whose compact NLR kinematics fit a host-disk and NLR intersection model.

quotes.quote: Mrk 279 Mrk 279 is a Seyfert 1 residing in a moderately inclined host. The modeled kinematics provide a good fit to the data, as we are able to account for three components within a small area over the nucleus. Imaging of the NLR shows a compact area slightly more than 1 '' in diameter. The opening angle seen in the [ O III ] imaging has a similar width of 140 ^ as the intersection between the host disk and NLR in the geometric model. While the resulting wedge geometry of the intersection matches well with the imaging, our model assumes that the inner portion of each NLR cone is hollow.

sources.summary: The paper models Mrk 279 and finds constrained inclination, outflow signatures, minimum radius, and orbit angle, with marginal opening-angle and mid-plane constraints.

quotes.quote: Mrk 279 Some of the parameters can be constrained: we find = mrk279_inc degrees, \ is constrained to be > 0.5 i.e. signatures of outflowing trajectories, indicating that even though is not tightly constrained, the model is able to identify that some of the orbits have velocities close to the radial outflowing escape velocity. We also find = mrk279_rmin light-days and = mrk279_thetae degrees indicating that not all of the outflowing orbits are bound. The opening angle is somewhat constrained: = mrk279_opening degrees, indicating that very thin disc configurations are not favoured. A similar result is obtained for = mrk279_eps , indicating that the mid-plane of the BLR is not completely transparent. Both the constrained and marginally constrained parameters agree within the uncertainties with the result from the full light-curve modelling (Fig. posterior_mrk279 ).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Mrk279 as a nearby low-luminosity Seyfert 1 whose S0/Sa host morphology and galaxy fluxes are recovered more precisely by PFVG than by traditional FVG.

quotes.quote: Mrk279 Mrk279 is a nearby ( z = 0.031 ) low-luminosity ( V = 15 mag) Seyfert 1 galaxy. The FVG analysis by revealed an S0/Sa host-galaxy morphology, in agreement with the results obtained through modeling high-resolution HST images ( 2002ApJ...569..624P ). The PFVG is able to recover the galaxy fluxes with an exceptional precision of 1 compared to the precision of 30 obtained by the traditional FVG method (Figure fig:AppMrk279 ). As shown in Appendix fig:Apx3 , the average S/N of the light curves is 90 . This is considerably lower than the average S/N 150 obtained for Mrk509, but consistent with the fact that Mrk279 is about 50 less luminous.

sources.summary: The paper presents ISO mid-infrared imaging and monitoring of the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 279, finding a point-like source, a power-law nuclear spectrum, weak PAH features, and a dust-emission bump.

quotes.quote: Mid-infrared images of the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 279 obtained with the ISO satellite are presented together with the results of a one-year monitoring campaign of the 2.5--11.7 \ spectrum. Contemporaneous optical photometric and spectrophotometric observations are also presented. The galaxy appears as a point-like source at the resolution of the ISOCAM instrument (4--5 ). The 2.5--11.7 \ average spectrum of the nucleus in Mrk 279 shows a strong power law continuum with = 0.80 0.05 ( F_ ^ ) and weak emission PAH features. The Mrk 279 spectral energy distribution shows a mid-IR bump, which extends from 2 to 15--20 .

sources.summary: The paper cites Mrk 279 as a comparison outflow case with a continuous ionization distribution, modeled UV/X-ray absorption abundances, enhanced nitrogen ratios, and modest broad-line-region contribution to Fe K emission.

quotes.quote: In yet another case, Mrk 279, the distribution is continuous but more complicated than a simple power-law costantini2007 .

The first absolute abundance estimate for an outflow was derived by our group from the UV spectra of Mrk 279, where absorption lines of H, C, N, and O were carefully modelled arav2007 to find abundances relative to solar for carbon (2.2 0.7), nitrogen, (3.5 1.1) and oxygen (1.6 0.8), which fully agree with the relative abundances of C, N, and O derived from the simultaneous X-ray spectra costantini2007 . The apparently enhanced N/H and N/O ratios in the Mrk 279 outflow may hint at strong contributions from stellar winds from massive stars and AGB stars.
For classical Seyfert 1 galaxies this contribution seems modest e.g. < 20 in Mrk 279, costantini2010 , while it may account for most of the line emission in specific objects e.g.

sources.summary: The paper says prior work on MRK 279 found two warm absorbers in combined data, but this analysis detects none and derives ionization-parameter limits.

quotes.quote: MRK 279- studied the same dataset ( 60 ) but they have combined 3 other datasets to make 160 . Very poor quality of the RGS dataset. They found two WA. =0.8 0.3 , =0.7 10^ 20 , and =2.6 , =3 10^ 20 . We have not detected any WA in this source and have found strict upper and lower limits on column density and ionisation parameters.

sources.summary: The paper constructs two non-simultaneous SEDs for Mrk 279 because OM and optical fluxes differ, finding consistent fits without requiring intrinsic reddening.

quotes.quote: The OM photometric flux at the effective wavelength 3444 seems to be in a lower flux state when it is compared with the optical spectrum. This 20 of variability between both spectrum and OM photometry prevent to join both datasets. Two non-simultaneous SED were constructed: on the one hand, GALEX in conjunction with the simultaneous OM photometric band; on the other, a fit over the single optical spectrum adding another two line-free points. The fitting results of both datasets are in good agreement and no intrinsic reddening was necessary to get a satisfactory thin AD fit.

sources.summary: The monitoring paper uses Mrk279 as a Seyfert 1 verification target, presenting narrow-band AGN continuum light curves and noting that a fuller analysis will follow.

quotes.quote: In the followings we illustrate the results for the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk279. Through Mrk279 redshift of z = 0.0306 , the emission line free continuum bands 4300, 5700, 6200 and 7000 trace mainly the AGN continuum variations (Fig. fig:filters ). The light curves of Mrk279 are shown in Figure fig:lcmrk . A more detailed study of the light curves of Mrk279, including other AGNs from the campaign will be presented in a forthcoming publication.

sources.summary: The paper uses FUSE data for Mrk 279, noting its complex low-velocity UV/X-ray outflow and blended intrinsic absorption that prevents a sensitive Ly upper limit.

quotes.quote: The spectrum shown in Figure fig:1m is from the FUSE observations in the extensive UV and X-ray campaign on Mrk 279 Gabel05a,Arav07,Costantini07 . Mrk 279 has a complex, low velocity outflow, with UV and X-ray spectral lines spanning velocities from +100 to 550 km s^ 1 . Unfortunately, the broad, blended troughs of intrinsic absorption due to Ly and C 3 977 in this outflow fall within the region of interest for a Ly counterpart to the suggested X-ray UFO. This makes it difficult to establish a sensitive upper limit for any Ly absorption.

sources.summary: The review describes Mrk 279 as a Seyfert whose narrow Fe K line has core and broad components, with LOC modeling showing only part of the Fe K flux arises in the BLR.

quotes.quote: In a major departure from phenomenological Gaussian modeling, and simple comparisons of Fe K and H line widths, examined the spectrum of the Seyfert 1 Mrk 279 using the Local Optimally emitting Cloud model (LOC; ). This model does not assume a geometry for the BLR; rather, it simply assumes that each line is the result of contributions from multiple regions that follow a power law distribution in gas density and radius. Mrk 279 is particularly interesting among Seyferts, in that its ``narrow'' Fe K line appears to be composed of an unresolved core and a FWHM = 14,000 km s ^ 1 component. By first establishing the parameters of a LOC model using UV and soft X-ray lines and then extending the model to the Fe K band, show that the BLR model can account for only 3-17 of the Fe K broad line flux.

sources.summary: The paper studies Mrk 279 as a Seyfert 1 galaxy with UV and X-ray-bright intrinsic absorption, deriving covering-factor and column-density constraints for its outflow.

quotes.quote: We have undertaken an intensive multiwavelength observing campaign with HST /STIS, FUSE , and CXO to study the intrinsic absorption in the Seyfert 1 galaxy Mrk 279. Mrk 279 was selected for this study because of its UV and X-ray brightness and the rich absorption spectrum in both bands, including unblended and well-resolved UV doublets see hereafter SK04 scot04 . Additionally, it has minimal contamination by Galactic absorption and a relatively weak contribution from a narrow emission line region (NLR), both of which can complicate measurements of the absorption properties.
These measurements provide the foundation for subsequent analysis and interpretation of the mass outflow in Mrk 279, and provide novel geometric constraints.

sources.summary: The paper monitors Mrk 279 during an X-ray/UV campaign, finding gradual count-rate variation but no significant variability on timescales shorter than 10 ks.

quotes.quote: Time variability The light curve of Mrk 279 taken in the zeroth spectral order is shown in Fig. fig:lc where we mark also the epochs of the FUSE and HST pointings, during the multi-wavelength campaign. The count rate (t) of the X-ray data varies gradually, spanning the range between 0.49 /s (during the minimum in observation 4) to 0.82 /s (at the maximum of observations 5 and 7). No significant variations on time scales shorter than 10 ks are observed. Typical time scales d t/ d (t) for the more gradual variations are 120 ks.

gmw_00000497.name: NGC 3384
gmw_00000497.overview: NGC 3384 is a lenticular galaxy located in the Leo I group [arXiv:1406.6982, arXiv:1712.05833] that is nearly devoid of gas and dust [arXiv:1406.6982, arXiv:1005.4208]. It exhibits a complex structure featuring a rotating stellar disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0109409, arXiv:1005.4208], a potential double bar [arXiv:1607.00585], and outer arcs suggesting past interactions [arXiv:1406.6982].

sources.summary: The paper portrays NGC 3384 as a disturbed lenticular galaxy with peculiar structure, faint fuzzies, outer arcs, and a southwest plume suggesting accretion features.

quotes.quote: NGC 3384 is a lenticular galaxy in close proximity (in projection) to M105. Like M105, it too is nearly devoid of gas sage06, oosterloo10 and dust tomita00 , and busarello96 found a fairly flat color profile at around B-V 0.9 out to 40 , typical for S0 galaxies li11 . The galaxy also contains several ``faint fuzzies'' (Larsen et al 2001), globular cluster-like objects with large half-light radii ( R_ eff = 7 15 pc, versus 2 to 3 pc in normal GCs), which burkert05 hypothesize may have originated in a low impact parameter galaxy collision. Previous work has also shown the galaxy to be host to a number of other notable peculiarities, including strong isophote twists barbon76, busarello96 , a pseudobulge or double-bar pinkney03, erwin04, meusinger07 , and outer boxy isophotes busarello96 . NGC 3384 thus appears much less well-settled than its neighbor M105.

Unlike with M105, however, we discover very clear residuals from the smooth profile in the form of two arcs northeast of the galaxy center, as well as a diffuse plume protruding out toward the southwest. Again, all of these features are seen when subtracting a model from the V image as well. The northeast arcs appear much less sharp than arcs often seen in elliptical galaxies e.g. malin83 , which are thought to be formed via minor mergers hernquinn87 .

sources.summary: The paper reconsiders NGC 3384 as a possible double-barred galaxy, citing kinematic evidence for an inner bar, humps, h4 features, and similarity to the model.

quotes.quote: NGC 3384 is not a well-confirmed S2B candidate. fis_dro_10 identified the inner bar on the infrared image by a photometric decomposition of the bulge region. In previous studies erw_spa_03, erw_04, sar_etal_06 , NGC 3384 was classified as a single-barred galaxy, with the inner component identified as a nuclear disk by unsharp mask. In this paper, we consider NGC 3384 as a potential S2B candidate, and present some kinematic evidence for the existence of the inner bar ( fig:NGC3384 ).

As shown in fig:Atlas3D and fig:NGC3384 , the most impressive kinematic feature is enhancements, which we term `` humps'', appearing along the minor axis of the inner bar. For NGC 2859 and NGC 2950 , humps are accompanied by moderate ring/spiral-like features. NGC 3384 also exhibits humps, consistent with the existence of an inner bar that is parallel to the LON.

The nuclear disk in NGC 3384 , if it exists, seems even weaker than in NGC 2950 , thus it may not be sufficient for forming hollows (or humps) as large as 30 km/s. Although we cannot rule out the scenario that humps are caused by a nuclear disk, the S2B model does share similar kinematic features with NGC 3384 . It is reasonable to consider NGC 3384 as a potential S2B candidate.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3384 as M105’s close SB0 neighbor, noting overlapping isophotes and planetary-nebula populations that require probabilistic separation and PN.S survey coverage.

quotes.quote: NGC 3384 For completeness, we also briefly discuss the photometric properties of the closest neighbouring galaxy of M105, NGC 3384. Its isophotes overlap those of M105. As NGC 3384 is an S0 galaxy viewed edge-on, its light can be decomposed into a contribution from its bulge (spheroid) and a contribution from its disc. 2013MNRAS.432.1010C carried out a photometric disc-spheroid decomposition based on 2MASS K band images 2006AJ....131.1163S . They found that the spheroid light distribution is best described by a S e rsic profile, _ spheroid (r_ 3384 ) = _ e r_ 3384 r_ e ^ 1/n 1 , with _ e = 4.09 10^ 7 , n = 4 , = 7.67 , r_ e = 15 2 , ellipticity _ spheroid = 0.17 , and position angle PA_ spheroid = 60 51 .

As the on-sky distance between M105 and the SB0-galaxy NGC 3384 is only 435 and the difference in distance modulus between the galaxies is only  0.2 mag , their PN populations appear superimposed. An immediate separation in velocity space is not possible due to the small difference in systemic velocity ( v_ sys < 200 km s ^ 1 ). At the time of publication of the data 2007ApJ...664..257D , 2007ApJ...664..257D addressed this by dividing the sample into two components on a probabilistic basis assuming that the distributions of PNe in the two galaxies directly follow the diffuse starlight.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3384 as a Leo I SB0 galaxy with complex central isophotes, little emission, regular bulge rotation, and evidence for a cold central stellar disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 3384 is a large SB0 ^- (s) galaxy in the Leo I group ( M_B = --19.6). It forms a triple on the sky with NGC 3379 and NGC 3389 but there is only marginal evidence for interactions. The light distribution in the central 20 is complex. The inner isophotes are elongated along the major axis, suggesting an embedded disk, but beyond 10 the elongation is along the minor-axis (e.g. \ Busarello et al. \ 1996). The isophotes are off-centered at much larger radii.

NGC 3384 shows no emission lines, remains undetected in HI, CO, radio continuum, and X-ray, but has IRAS detections at 12 and 100 m (e.g. \ Roberts et al. \ 1991). Figure 2 displays the stellar kinematics of NGC 3384 and illustrates a key advantage of SAURON . Figure 2 shows that the bulge of NGC 3384 is rotating regularly.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3384 as the target of a head-on collision with M96, initially gas-rich and later converted into a fast-rotating early-type galaxy while producing the Leo ring.

quotes.quote: In our model, NGC 3384 is the target disk of a head-on collision with the massive galaxy M96. M96 appeared to be the most likely interloper, assuming that the \ bridge linking it to the ring was left over after the collision. Note that the key role played by M96 in the formation of the ring had already been envisioned by . As for the target galaxy, the S0 galaxy NGC 3384 seemed a better candidate than the other central galaxy (M105): in the collision scenario, the target should initially be a gas-rich spiral, and an interaction without merger is more likely to convert it into a disky ETG (S0/Sa) than into a spherical elliptical such as M105 Bekki98,Bournaud03a .

The proposed progenitor galaxy, NGC 3384, does not show strong morphological disturbances, even in our deep imaging data. This is however expected in our model, that produces a gaseous collisional ring, an extended and very faint (unobservable) stellar halo, but leaves little signs of collision such as classical tails and shells around the parent galaxy. SAURON observations of NGC 3384 Emsellem04,Kuntschner06 revealed a barred rotating disk, with a low gas and dust content and without very recent star formation, which is also broadly consistent with our model where the pre-existing spiral is converted into a fastly rotating ETG by the 1.2 Gyr old collision.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 3384 lacks a resolved sphere of influence, has decreased black-hole mass in the new code, and gives the strongest dark-halo constraints in the sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 3384. Besides NGC 2778, this is the only other galaxy for which the sphere of influence is not resolved. While for NGC 2778 the new code does not lead to a change of the ^2 distribution, for NGC 3384 M_ decreases. This might indicate a larger uncertainty in the determination of M_ using different modeling codes when R_ inf is not resolved.

NGC 3384 is the galaxy with the strongest constraints on the presence of a DM halo. For this galaxy, we ran a grid of models changing r_c as well as V_c , but we found no change in ^2 for different values of r_c . However, we are able to set a lower limit on V_c with V_c > 350 km s ^ 1 at 1 confidence. The no DM halo model is excluded at more than 3 confidence (see Figure fig:dmtest ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3384 as the second Leo I pair galaxy, connecting its S0 disk to the HI ring, modeling its surface-brightness structure, and subtracting it to measure intragroup light.

quotes.quote: The LEO I pair NGC 3379-NGC 3384 The galaxies NGC 3379-NGC 3384 are members of the LEO I group. This is a very loose group composed, at least, of 11 bright galaxies where seven out of the total, including NGC 3379 (BGG) and NGC 3384, are associated with the subgroup NGC 3368 (M96) centered on the BGG. The other four galaxies are members of the LEO triplet deVaucouleurs1975gaun.book..557D . In addition to both early and late morphological types of galaxies (ETGs, LTGs), a large number of dwarf galaxies have been revealed by Muller2018A&A...615A.105M . There is extensive literature on the LEO I group, in particular about the presence of a fragmented ring of neutral hydrogen surrounding the pair NGC 3379-NGC 3384, with a radius of about 100 kpc.

Sil'chenko2003ApJ...591..185S found that the ages and kinematics of both the circumnuclear stellar and gaseous disks in NGC 3384, NGC 3379 and M96 are consistent with a formation age on the Gyr scale. The primordial formation of the HI ring, however, is hindered by stability problems over large time scales, a fundamental inconsistency which suggests that a close encounter between disk galaxies is the most likely scenario for the formation of the ring. They might be M96 and NGC 3384, since the S0 NGC 3384 is the only galaxy of the group having the disk major axis aligned with the ring. NGC 3384 could have been transformed into a S0 galaxy after that encounter Corbelli2021ApJ...908L..39C . According to this hypothesis, Dansac2010ApJ...717L.143M reproduced all the most important features of the ring, such as the shape, the rotation and the lack of the optical counterpart brighter than 28 mag/arcsec ^2 .

sources.summary: The paper re-investigates NGC 3384 as a classical S0 galaxy with inner and elongated components, peculiar morphology, low dust content, and signs of a post-activity dust-settling phase.

quotes.quote: We re-investigate the lenticular galaxy NGC 3384, a member of the Leo I galaxy group, using HST and multi-colour Calar Alto observations. Various approaches are used to visualize the two known peculiar components, the so-called inner component (IC) and the elongated component (EC), on the HST images. The methods were checked in detail using synthetical images from simulated galaxies. For the first time, we make the IC as well as the inner part of the EC visible on direct images. The results confirm both the bar-like appearance of the inner EC and the quasi-elliptical shape of the IC.

NGC 3384 is an apparently unspectacular yet fascinating ``classical'' S0 galaxy in the nearby galaxy group Leo I. Its appearance on a photographic plate was described by Pease ( ) as ``an astonishing nebula consisting of a bright center 40 diameter, on which are superimposed a very bright elongated nucleus 19 10 , p \ 45 , crossed by a second bright nucleus 40 5 , p \ 130 , presenting a Saturn-like appearance". Since that time, NGC 3384 was a target of a variety of photometric, spectroscopic, and kinematic studies of samples of nearby early-type ga laxies (e.g., Fisher ; Pinkney et al. ; Emsellem et al. ; Erwin ; Lauer et al. ; Sil' chenko ).

sources.summary: NGC 3384 is tied to the Leo Ring: a spiral-like H I structure ends near it, and the authors identify a bright cloud with the galaxy.

quotes.quote: The situation in NGC 3384 is very complex. This galaxy is member of the M96 group that is famous for its large \ ring encircling several galaxies of this group the Leo Ring; Schneider83 . Subsequent to the observations reported here, we have imaged the \ over the entire region and a full study of the Leo Ring will be published elsewhere.

One result of this work is that the Leo Ring appears to form a large spiral-like structure that appears to end very close, both in space and in velocity, to NGC 3384. At the endpoint of this spiral-like structure a fairly bright \ cloud is observed and this is the cloud we identify here with NGC 3384. However, it is conceivable that most of the \ of the Leo Ring originated from NGC 3384 see Dansac10 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3384 as a Leo Group satellite S0 in a six-galaxy lenticular sample, using planetary nebulae to separate spheroid and disc kinematics out to large radii.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we therefore apply the kinematic bulge--disc decomposition technique developed in Ari to the sample of six S0s with suitable PNe data presented in Ari2 . This sample was selected to span a range of environments in order to see if the evolutionary properties depend systematically on surroundings: NGC 7457 and NGC 3115 are isolated; NGC 1023 and NGC 2768 are the dominant galaxies of two small groups (5-6 members); while NGC 3384 and NGC 3489 are satellite galaxies of the Leo Group (30 members). The Hubble types of these galaxies and the adopted distance moduli are listed in Table tab:galfitinput .

In this paper, we have analysed the kinematics of a sample of six lenticular galaxies using the planetary nebulae data presented in Ari2 to determine their dynamical properties out to large radii. This analysis has emphasised the importance of treating the spheroidal and disc components of these systems as distinct entities both photometrically and kinematically, and doing a careful job of separating them. Through this process, the rather heterogeneous properties of S0s over-all start to be resolved into more consistent sub-components, and a reasonably coherent picture begins to emerge.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 3384 is a Leo-group S0 with 54 detected globular clusters and suggests it hosts extended red faint clusters similar to those in NGC 1023.

quotes.quote: NGC 3384 Also in the Leo group, this S0 galaxy has S _N 0.9 ash98 . We detect a total of 54 GCs, of which 30 formally fall in the ``blue'' category and 24 in the red, but with no statistical evidence for bimodality.

Among the remaining galaxies in our sample, a similar population of extended red objects appears to exist in NGC 3384. Apart from the generally poorer GCS of this galaxy, the appearance of its , diagram mimics that of NGC 1023. Furthermore, as mentioned in Sect. sec:lf , the GCLF of NGC 3384 shows an excess of faint clusters very reminiscent of that in NGC 1023. We thus suggest that NGC 3384 is the second case of a galaxy which hosts a population of these, hitherto unknown, extended red faint clusters.

sources.summary: The paper records NGC 3384's adopted photometric profile or decomposition, giving bulge/scale parameters and comparing those choices with alternative literature fits for the structural analysis.

quotes.quote: NGC 3384: The decomposed SDSS i- band bulge profile comes from Beifiori2012 . We calibrated it to the I- band using the equation of Jordi2006 and the (R-I) color from Hyperleda. We get R_e=7.6'' , while Laurikainen2010 quote R_e=3.6'' from a complex fit involving 2 bars. But Erwin2004 classified the object as a barred galaxy with an inner disk, rather than double-barred. Moreover, the residuals of the fit of Laurikainen2010 are not shown, so we keep our reasonable solution.

sources.summary: The review treats NGC 3384 as an S0 galaxy candidate for intermediate-age populations, with faint extended clusters tied to disk populations and a fainter turnover magnitude.

quotes.quote: Turning to the S0's, NGC 1023 and NGC 3384 are perhaps candidates for hosting intermediate-age populations. Both galaxies show indications of star formation activity in their inner regions (Kuntschner et al. , Sil'chenko ).

A similar finding is reported for NGC 3384 by Larsen et al. . Brodie Larsen found that these faint extended clusters belong to the disk populations of their host galaxies and quote an age of at least 7 Gyr.
The S0-galaxies NGC 1023 and NGC 3384 exhibit a population of faint extended red clusters, which cause a fainter TOM, if they are included in the luminosity function.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3384 as a nearby SB0 companion whose planetary-nebula population overlaps NGC 3379, requiring probabilistic allocation that assigns 23 objects to NGC 3384.

quotes.quote: The SB0 galaxy NGC 3384 lies only 7 in projection away from NGC 3379, so their populations will appear to some extent superimposed. NGC 3384 has a systemic velocity of 704 , some 200 \ lower than NGC 3379, but inconveniently NGC 3384 is rotating quite rapidly, and the side of NGC 3384 closest to NGC 3379 comprises the receding part of its disk, which shifts the velocity of any PNe to very close to NGC 3379's velocity, so we cannot separate the two populations on the basis of their kinematics, as indeed another glance at Figure  fig:bandpass immediately shows. Instead, we adopt the simple approach of dividing the two on a probabilistic basis: we model the distributions of PNe in the two galaxies on the assumption that they follow the diffuse starlight, and assign each PN to whichever distribution makes the dominant contribution at its location. One slight subtlety is that because these galaxies lie at different distances and because their PN luminosity functions have somewhat different intrinsic specific frequencies 1989ApJ...344..715C , the cross-over between the two does not exactly coincide with the similar point in the raw photometry. However, these effects can be accounted for, and the resulting division between the two, which is shown in Figure  fig:galsep , allocates 23 of the 214 PNe to NGC 3384.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3384 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This lenticular galaxy is a member of the Leo I group 1992A&AS...93..211F . We find a rotation velocity of 160 7 at 7 , consistent with the rotation amplitude published by 2011MNRAS.414.2923K . We do not observe any twist of the kinematic position angle or any misalignment with the photometric one, and the motion is that of a regular rotator. The velocity dispersion map in 2004MNRAS.352..721E shows that the has a double peak at the center ( 155 ), and a declining profile that reaches 80 at 0.5 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3384 as a nearby lenticular hosting a smaller set of faint extended clusters; Keck spectra verify membership and compare cluster velocities with its rotation curve.

quotes.quote: In lar01 , a smaller number of faint, extended objects similar to those in NGC 1023 were noted in another nearby 11.5 Mpc, sak97 lenticular, NGC 3384, but their existence was ruled out in two other nearby early-type galaxies, NGC 3115 (a lenticular) and NGC 3379 (an elliptical). In this paper we show that the faint extended objects in NGC 1023 and NGC 3384 occupy a new region of the size/luminosity/metallicity parameter space for star clusters. We present new Keck / LRIS spectra of these clusters to verify membership with NGC 1023 and NGC 3384 and disprove the (albeit unlikely) possibility that the objects are unrelated background galaxies.

Although there are too few clusters to provide any dynamical constraints on the NGC 3384 extended cluster system, in Fig. fig:brodie.fig5 we show, for completeness, the radial velocities of the extended clusters and the NGC 3384 rotation curve from fis97 .

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 3384 has a dumbbell-shaped dispersion map, rapid central rotation, nearly cylindrical outer velocities, and positive h4 values unlike some prior measurements.

quotes.quote: The dispersion map exhibits a dumbbell-like shape, coincident with a rapidly rotating component in the central 5 \ F97, Bu96 , with a strong anticorrelation signature in h_3 . The velocities are nearly cylindrical in the outer part of this galaxy classified as SB0 (Paper II). Our h_4 map shows a slight dip at the centre, consistent with the long-slit profile of F97 . Our h_4 map is consistent with h_4 being positive everywhere, in contrast to the measurements of Pin03 .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 3384 among barred S0 galaxies with disk-like structure and luminous inner disks or bars, notes bulge overestimation in decomposition, and reports no central emission.

quotes.quote: We also included three galaxies, NGCs 2787, 3384, and 3945, which were previously found to possess disk-like structural and kinematic properties 1996A&A...314...32B, 2003ApJ...591..185S, 2003ApJ...596..903P, 2003ApJ...597..929E . All three are barred S0 galaxies with inner disks or bars that are more luminous than the surrounding bulge.
A two-component bulge/disk model, such as ours, also overestimates the bulge component of our three S0s with luminous inner disks (NGCs 2787, 3384, and 3945) as previously found by .
We detect emission in the central regions of all our galaxies except the S0s NGCs 3384 and 7457.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC3384 as a low-redshift galaxy with six associated high-redshift quasars and notes their redshift mismatch as evidence for non-cosmological quasar redshifts.

quotes.quote: In addition, it has also been observed that the galaxy NGC3384 has a redshift z=0.003 , and that the six quasars found around it have similar redshifts z [ 1.11,1.28 ] to each other. These quasars are associated with the galaxy NGC3384. However, the redshift of galaxy NGC3384 is significantly different from that of quasars, indicating that the redshift of the quasar is non-cosmological 1979ApJ...229..489A .

gmw_00000498.name: NGC 1277
gmw_00000498.overview: NGC 1277 is a compact lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1301.1800, arXiv:1405.6425] located in the core of the Perseus cluster [arXiv:1301.1800, arXiv:1612.02823]. It is a massive relic galaxy [arXiv:1803.04893, arXiv:2104.00672] hosting an extremely old stellar population [arXiv:2003.04093, arXiv:2012.04783] and a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1301.1800, arXiv:1506.08202].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 1277 as a Perseus lenticular galaxy hosting an ultramassive black hole candidate, finding weak X-ray emission and arguing its black hole is not significantly growing.

quotes.quote: Miller ^3 ^1 Institute of Astronomy, Madingley Road, Cambridge CB3 0HA ^2 Max-Planck-Institute f ur extraterrestrische Physik, 85748 Garching, Germany ^3 Department of Astronomy, University of Michigan, 500 Church St., Ann Arbor, MI48109, USA We study the X-ray emission from NGC 1277, a galaxy in the core of the Perseus cluster, for which van den Bosch et al. have recently claimed the presence of an UltraMassive Black Hole (UMBH) of mass 1.7 10^ 10 , unless the IMF of the stars in the stellar bulge is extremely bottom heavy. The X-rays originate in a power-law component of luminosity 1.3 10^ 40 embedded in a 1 keV thermal minicorona which has a half-light radius of about 0.36 kpc, typical of many early-type galaxies in rich clusters of galaxies. If Bondi accretion operated onto the UMBH from the minicorona with a radiative efficiency of 10 per cent, then the object would appear as a quasar with luminosity 10^ 46 , a factor of almost 10^6 times higher than observed. The UMBH in NGC 1277 is definitely not undergoing any significant growth at the present epoch.

The mystery has now deepened by the recent claim of a UMBH in the lenticular galaxy NGC 1277 (van den Bosch et al 2012). The estimated black hole mass of 1.7 10^ 10 corresponds to 14 per cent of the total stellar mass of that galaxy, much larger than the 0.1 per cent found in a normal massive galaxy. NGC 1277 lies in the core of the Perseus cluster where we have amassed a very deep X-ray exposure with Chandra (Fabian et al 2006; 2011).

sources.summary: The paper argues NGC 1277 is a genuine massive relic galaxy with little post-collapse accretion, unusually red globular clusters, compact structure, and little stellar halo.

quotes.quote: Here we report optical observations of the massive relic galaxy NGC 1277, a nearby unevolved example of a high redshift ``red nugget" . The g_ 475W z_ 850LP cluster colour distribution shows that the GC system of the relic is unimodal and uniquely red. This is in strong contrast to normal galaxies of similar and larger stellar mass, whose cluster systems always exhibit (and are generally dominated by) blue clusters . We argue that the cluster system of NGC 1277 indicates that the galaxy has undergone little (if any) mass accretion after its initial collapse and use analytic merger trees to show that the total stellar mass accretion is likely less than 10 . These results confirm that NGC 1277 is a genuine relic galaxy and show that the blue clusters constitute an accreted population in present day massive galaxies.

NGC 1277 is 5 times more compact than normal galaxies of the same stellar mass at z = 0 . As we have shown, NGC 1277 also lacks a significant population of blue clusters which are generally associated with a ``halo'' population in galaxies. The lack of a halo is consistent with the surface brightness profile of NGC 1277 which shows a sharp decline beyond 10 kpc corresponding to a factor of 5 less stars when compared to normal galaxies of the same mass .

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 1277 as a known compact massive galaxy and uses it as an example of a local early-type galaxy with an intermediate-sized disk.

quotes.quote: Having checked these papers, our Table Tab1 includes 20 known bulges along with the known compact massive galaxy NGC 1277 (van den Bosch et al. \ 2012), plus one additional galaxy , as opposed to a bulge, which also meets the above size and mass criteira. All but three of the 22 systems have R_ e < 2 kpc. All but 4 have M_* 0.9 10^ 11 M_ .

A third and possible fourth example is NGC 1277 ( M_* = 1.2 10^ 11 M_ ) and NGC 5845 (excluded here because M_* = 0.5 10^ 11 M_ ), as already pointed out by van den Bosch et al. \ (2012) and Jiang et al. \ (2012), respectively. However, if the disks of these galaxies were to continue to grow, until they resembled the more stereotypical S0 galaxies today, with B/D flux ratios of 1/3 and R_ e,bulge /h_ disk 0.2 ( R_ e,bulge /R_ e,disk 0.12 ), then their galaxy sizes would likely exceed 2 kpc while the spheroidal component remained compact.

As noted in Section Sec_data , NGC 1277, NGC 1332, NGC 5493 and NGC 5845 are examples of local early-type galaxies where the disk does not dominate the light at large radii, as in proto-typical lenticular galaxies. These disks are an order of magnitude larger than nuclear disks, and referred to as intermediate-sized disks.

sources.summary: The paper detects CO(1-0) in NGC 1277, links it to a nuclear dust lane, uses gas kinematics to constrain an overmassive black hole, and notes possible weak AGN emission.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The J2000 coordinates R.A. =03 h 19m 51.5s and Dec =+41^ 34 24 7 were used as pointing reference.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of CO(1-0) emission from NGC 1277, a lenticular galaxy in the Perseus Cluster. NGC 1277 has previously been proposed to host an overmassive black hole (BH) compared to the galaxy bulge luminosity (mass), based on stellar-kinematic measurements. The CO(1-0) emission, observed with the IRAM Plateau de Bure Interferometer (PdBI) using both, a more compact ( 2.9 arcsec resolution) and a more extended ( 1 arcsec resolution) configuration, is likely to originate from the dust lane encompassing the galaxy nucleus at a distance of 0.9 arcsec ( 320 pc). The double-horned CO(1-0) profile found at 2.9 arcsec resolution traces 1.5 10^8 \ M_ of molecular gas, likely orbiting in the dust lane at 550 \ km \ s^ 1 , which suggests a total enclosed mass of 2 10^ 10 \ M_ . At 1-arcsec resolution, the CO(1-0) emission appears spatially resolved along the dust lane in east-west direction, though at a low signal-to-noise ratio.

In agreement with the previous stellar-kinematic measurements, the CO(1-0) kinematics is found to be consistent with an 1.7 10^ 10 \ M_ BH for a stellar mass-to-light ratio of M/L_V=6.3 , while a less massive BH of 5 10^ 9 \ M_ is possible when assuming a larger M/L_V=10 . While the molecular gas reservoir may be associated with a low level of star formation activity, the extended 2.6-mm continuum emission is likely to originate from a weak AGN, possibly characterized by an inverted radio-to-millimetre spectral energy distribution.

sources.summary: The paper remeasures NGC 1277, finding a massive classical spheroid, an inner nearly edge-on disk, and a black hole mass far smaller and more normal than earlier claims.

quotes.quote: Here we provide our measurements for NGC 1277. Our structural decomposition reveals that NGC 1277 is dominated by a `classical' spheroid with a S ersic index n=5.3 , a half-light radius R_ e,major =2.1 kpc, and a stellar mass of 2.7 10^ 11 M_ (using M_*/L_V=11.65 , Mart in-Navarro et al.). This mass is an order of magnitude greater than originally reported. Using the latest M_ bh - n , M_ bh - M_ sph,* , and M_ bh - relations, the expected black hole mass is respectively ( 0.57^ +1.29 _ 0.40 ) 10^9 M_ , ( 1.58^ +4.04 _ 1.13 ) 10^9 M_ , and ( 2.27^ +4.04 _ 1.44 ) 10^9 M_ (using =300 km s ^ 1 ) for which the `sphere-of-influence' is 0 .31 . Our new kinematical maps obtained from laser guide star assisted, adaptive optics on the Keck I Telescope dramatically reaffirm the presence of the inner, nearly edge-on, disk seen in this galaxy's image.

Our optimal black hole mass, albeit a probable upper limit because of the disk, is 1.2 10^ 9 M_ ( M/L_V=12.3 ). This is an order of magnitude smaller than originally reported and 4 times smaller than recently reported.

sources.summary: The paper frames NGC 1277 as an extreme relic compact galaxy with little dark matter, using Euclid data to seek interaction signatures, baryonic mass loss, and sharp truncations.

quotes.quote: The mysterious origin of NGC 1277 NGC1277 NGC 1277 is the most extreme case of relic galaxy, i.e., a local counterpart of the massive ultra-compact galaxy population also known as `red nuggets' common at z > 2 Ferre-Mateu17,Spiniello24 . How this object remains similar to those in the primeval Universe is a mystery. In addition, it has also been found that this galaxy is consistent with being dark matter deficient f_ dm (5r_ e ) < 0.05 at the 2 confidence level, see Comeron23 , being the first such galaxy found with a high stellar mass ( M 1.6 10^ 11 M_ ). Our project aims at shedding light into these conundrums by looking for interactions of this galaxy, combining both HST and photometry, in the optical but crucially also in the NIR that better tracks stellar mass.

Finding asymmetries in the galaxy surface brightness profiles favours a scenario where it has lost some of its baryonic mass (and thus the lower-bound dark matter) via tidal interactions with the cluster or its neighbour giant elliptical NGC 1278. Conversely, the dearth of these features allows us to detect well-defined galaxy edges or truncations, a novel physically-motivated galaxy size indicator related with the gas density threshold for enabling efficient star formation e.g., Buitrago24,Fernandez-Iglesias24 . In fact, because of NGC 1277's very high formation redshift Trujillo14,Buitrago18 , our ERO data are in a position to prove that relic galaxies are those for which these truncations appear the sharpest.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC1277 as the compact lenticular reference for ultramassive black hole merger simulations, scaling model mass and length to its bulge and influence radius.

quotes.quote: NGC1277, a compact lenticular galaxy, may host the most massive SMBH to date, at 1.7 10^ 10 M_ ( van12 , though see ems13 , which advocates for a more modest mass of 5 10^9 M_ ). Current efforts are underway to determine if these represent an extreme class of SMBH or if they are statistical outliers.

Physical Scaling We use NGC1277 as a reference to scale our primary galaxy models. We set our mass to the bulge mass of NGC1277, 3 10^ 10 M_ van12 . To obtain the length scaling, we compare the SMBH influence radius in NGC1277 (565 pc) to that of our UM model ( 11 model units); this sets one model unit to 50 pc. Note that since the SMBH is so prominent, the radius of influence encloses 95 percent of the stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1277 with SWIFT spectroscopy, measuring radial IMF-sensitive absorption features, strong NaI gradients, flat FeH profiles, a steep global IMF, and FeH-derived IMF values consistent with spectral fitting.

quotes.quote: We make radial measurements of stellar initial mass function (IMF) sensitive absorption features in the two massive early-type galaxies NGC 1277 and IC 843. Using the Oxford Short Wavelength Integral Field SpecTrogaph (SWIFT), we obtain resolved measurements of the NaI0.82 and FeH0.99 indices, among others, finding both galaxies show strong gradients in NaI absorption combined with flat FeH profiles at 0.4 . We find these measurements may be explained by radial gradients in the IMF, appropriate abundance gradients in [ Na/Fe ] and [ Fe/H ] , or a combination of the two, and our data is unable to break this degeneracy.

We also use full spectral fitting to infer global properties from an integrated spectrum of each object, deriving a unimodal IMF slope consistent with Salpeter in IC 843 ( x=2.27 0.17 ) but steeper than Salpeter in NGC 1277 ( x=2.69 0.11 ), despite their similar FeH equivalent widths. Independently, we fit the strength of the FeH feature and compare to the E-MILES and CvD12 stellar population libraries, finding agreement between the models. The IMF values derived in this way are in close agreement with those from spectral fitting in NGC 1277 ( x_ CvD =2.59^ +0.25 _ 0.48 , x_ E-MILES =2.77 0.31 ), but are less consistent in IC 843, with the IMF derived from FeH alone leading to steeper slopes than when fitting the full spectrum ( x_ CvD =2.57^ +0.30 _ 0.41 , x_ E-MILES =2.72 0.25 ).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1277 as the only firm massive relic-galaxy candidate and emphasizes its location in the central Perseus cluster environment.

quotes.quote: Nevertheless, and despite the community efforts, up to now only one firm candidate to be considered one of these relics is known: NGC 1277. Curiously, this galaxy is located at the centre of one of the most rich near galaxy clusters: Perseus. Is its location a matter of chance?

reported that NGC 1277 is a firm candidate to be a relic galaxy. Interestingly, NGC 1277 inhabits the central region of the Perseus galaxy cluster. Using the BOLSOI simulation, reported that the probability of finding dark matter haloes like that of NGC 1277 increases significantly with the mass of the host structure.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1277 as the role-model massive relic, emphasizing its compactness, old formation history, extreme black hole scaling offset, and cluster environment.

quotes.quote: For galaxies in rich environments, such as the previously reported relic galaxy NGC 1277, the most extreme properties (e.g. sizes, short formation timescales, larger super-massive black holes) are expected, while lower density environments will have galaxies with delayed and/or extended star formations, slightly larger sizes and not that extreme black hole masses. The confirmation of 3 relic galaxies up to a distance of 106 Mpc implies a lower limit in the number density of these red nuggets in the local universe of 6 10 ^ 7 Mpc ^ 3 , which is within the theoretical expectations.

Throughout this paper we will use NGC 1277 and its already published properties (e.g. Trujillo2014 , MartinNavarro2015b ) as our role model for massive relic galaxy, comparing our two new candidates with it. We will specify, nevertheless, if new values have been measured in order to have a consistent analysis.

NGC 1277 is usually the most extreme case: it is the most compact, it shows the less variation in its SFHs, has the shortest formation timescales and thus seems to be the most relic-like of the three. At the same time, it is the most extreme outlier in the SMBHs scaling relations.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1277 as the benchmark relic compact galaxy, describing its old, massive, compact, metal-rich, fast-rotating properties and exclusion from the new HST sample.

quotes.quote: First, NGC 1277, a peculiar S0 galaxy at a distance of 73 Mpc in the Perseus cluster, has been confirmed as a relic galaxy tru14 . From the analysis of imaging and spectral data of the galaxy light, tru14 found that NGC 1277 is old ( >10 Gyr), massive ( M_*=1.2 0.4 10^ 11 M_ ), and compact ( R_e = 1.2 kpc). They also estimated the metallicity of the central region in NGC 1277 to be above solar ( [ Fe/H ] =0.20 0.04 and [ /Fe ] =0.4 0.1 ). NGC 1277 is rotating very quickly with a rotation velocity of v_ rot 300 , and it also shows a high central velocity dispersion of _v=355 yil17 .
NGC 1277 , MRK 1216 and PGC 32873 are also included in the list of yil17 . There are no F160W images available for NGC 1277 so we exclude it as well.

sources.summary: The paper considers NGC1277 an optimized lenticular-galaxy target for sneutrino-line searches, noting its Perseus location, possible very large SMBH, M87-like distance, and dark-matter spike behavior.

quotes.quote: Similar reasoning holds for NGC1277 and CenA. We consider NGC1277, which is a lenticular galaxy in the constellation Perseus, as it may host one of the largest SMBH ever measured and is at a distance similar to M87 with respect to us. Finally, CenA is in the constellation Centaurus and despite of hosting a smaller SMBH compared to M87 and NGC1277, it has the advantage of being one of the closest radio galaxies to the Sun. All relevant details about these three galaxies and their SMBHs are provided in Tab. tab:BHs .
10^ 26 cm ^3 /s), the plateau region is clearly visible, while if 10^ 30 cm ^3 /s the DM interaction is not strong enough to flatten out the spike for NGC1277 and M87.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1277 as a massive elliptical overmassive-black-hole outlier, favoring a compact old stellar population and relic scenario while noting revised black-hole mass estimates.

quotes.quote: Most notable are the massive elliptical NGC 1277 VandenBosch2012, Emsellem2013, Fabian2013, Yildirim2015, Scharwachter2016, Walsh2016, Graham2016 , NGC 4486B Magorrian1998, Gultekin2009, Saglia2016 , and the compact galaxy M60-UCD1 Seth2014 . All of these observed ( ) outlier galaxies have been found to lie well above the scatter in the - bulge mass ( ) relation, with / > 5 per cent, as opposed to the expected ratio of 0.3 per cent Kormendy2013,McConnell2013 .

For example, NGC 1277 has extremely regular isophotes and a flat rotation curve out to 5 times the half light radius, each implying that it is very unlikely to have been tidally stripped VandenBosch2012 . Ferre-Mateu2015 also find that NGC 1277, along with six other ( ) outlier candidates, is compact given its stellar mass and has stellar populations older than 10 Gyr, confirming the likelihood of the `relic' scenario.

For example, using new kinematical maps from the Keck I Telescope combined with Jeans Anisotropic Modelling, Graham2016 recently computed = (1.2 0.3) 10^9 for NGC 1277, an order of magnitude lower than originally estimated by VandenBosch2012 using Schwarzchild modelling of HST data.

sources.summary: The review describes NGC 1277 as an extremely old, compact, massive candidate relic galaxy near Perseus, with an unusual globular cluster system that has few, if any, blue clusters.

quotes.quote: NGC 1277 is a particularly interesting case of an unusual GC system. NGC 1277 is an extremely old, compact and massive galaxy near the centre of the Perseus galaxy cluster. The galaxy has been identified as a candidate "relic galaxy" which is to say the remnant of the early in-situ phase in massive galaxy formation without significant subsequent merging or accretion . In this view, NGC 1277 is regarded as a near-pristine "core" of a normal massive ETG. explored the GC system with HST imaging and found that NGC 1277 has few, if any, blue GCs.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1277 as the prototypical compact relic galaxy, with old stellar populations, bottom-heavy IMF, little accretion, predominantly red globular clusters, and low specific frequency.

quotes.quote: As relic galaxies, they are expected to have uniformly old stellar populations ( >10 Gyr, y17 ), and be extremely compact, as is the case for the prototypical relic galaxy NGC 1277 ( trujillo14 ) and also Mrk 1216 and PGC 032873 ( FM17 ). In addition, stellar population studies indicate that relics such as NGC 1277 have extremely bottom-heavy stellar initial mass functions (IMF) which do not vary with radius.

Recently, beasley18 reported that the GCS of NGC 1277, in the Perseus cluster is made up of almost exclusively red GCs, with only a small (<10 ) fraction of blue GCs by studying the optical color g-z with deep HST imaging. This finding supports the idea from other studies based on the age of its stellar population ( FM18 ) and its IMF ( mn15b ) that this galaxy is a relic. Furthermore, the S_N of NGC 1277 is low (< 2), in accordance with a high star-formation efficiency as would be for a rapidly early dissipative formation and a lack of a accretion of high- S_N , low-mass satellites.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1277 is an extreme relic-galaxy candidate with negligible dark matter within five effective radii, requiring early dark-matter loss or formation deficient in dark matter.

quotes.quote: The best relic galaxy candidate discovered to date is NGC 1277, in the Perseus cluster. We used deep integral field George and Cynthia Mitchel spectrograph (GCMS) data to revisit NGC 1277 out to an unprecedented radius of 6 kpc (corresponding to 5 R_ e ). By using Jeans anisotropic models constructed with JAM we recovered the dark matter fraction of NGC 1277 within 5 R_ e , f_ DM (5 R_ e ) , and found it to be negligible ( f_ DM (5 R_ e )<0.07 ; two-sigma confidence level), which is in strong tension with the CDM expectation for a massive ETG. Since the lack of an extended envelope would reduce dynamical friction and prevent the accretion of an envelope, we propose that NGC 1277 lost its dark matter very early or that it was dark matter deficient ab initio.
Alternatively, NGC 1277 might have been born in a high-velocity collision of gas-rich proto-galactic fragments, where dark matter left behind a disc of dissipative baryons.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1277 as an observed compact massive relic benchmark, noting similar relic candidates, old stellar ages, and model kinematics comparable to compact relics.

quotes.quote: NGC 1277 was claimed to be the first fully confirmed massive relic 2014ApJ...780L..20T . Subsequently, a dozen new massive relic candidates with similar properties to NGC 1277 and hosting extreme super-massive black holes were reported 2012Natur.491..729V .

This is similar to compact massive relics ( 2014ApJ...780L..20T, 2017MNRAS.467.1929F, 2024MNRAS.527.8793S ) where NGC 1277, PGC 032873 and Mrk 1216 are found to have formed their stars around 10 Gyr ago.

Here we study the kinematical properties of one of the models from 2022MNRAS.516.1081E of comparable mass with the observed compact massive relic galaxies NGC 1277, PGC 032873 and Mrk 1216 2014ApJ...780L..20T,2017MNRAS.467.1929F listed in Table tab:values and find the model to be very similar to these observed compact massive relic galaxies. These compact massive relics are found to have their average stellar ages similar to ETGs of comparable mass in a high-density environment as found by 2005ApJ...621..673T , where a galaxy with M_ * = 10^ 11 M_ would have formed 10 Gyr ago.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1277 as a nearby relic massive galaxy, emphasizing its compact structure, old enhanced stellar population, bottom-heavy IMF, and unusually massive central black hole.

quotes.quote: The Perseus cluster galaxy NGC 1277 fulfills all the characteristics to be considered a relic massive galaxy truji14 . The galaxy exhibits an unusually high central velocity dispersion (above 400 ) and is physically compact, with an effective circularized radius of 1.2 kpc truji14 . The bulk of its stars are old (age > 10 Gyr) and enhanced ( [ Mg/Fe ] 0.3 dex), which suggests a short ( 100 Myr) and intense (SFR 10^ 3 /yr) star formation burst at z 2 , followed by subsequent quiescent evolution truji14 . In addition, NGC 1277 hosts the most massive (compared to the total galaxy mass) black hole found to date but see Emsellem remco .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1277 as a compact Perseus-cluster galaxy hosting an ultra-massive black hole and remaining an outlier in black-hole scaling relations despite mass uncertainties.

quotes.quote: The first example is NGC 1277 in the Perseus cluster, which is reported to have a central black hole of mass M_ BH 1.7 10^ 10 M_ located at the centre of a compact galaxy with a half-light radius of R_e 1.6 kpc vandenBosch_et_2012 . The corresponding black hole-to-total stellar mass ratio is of the order of 14 , much larger than the usually expected values. We note that there are some uncertainties concerning the black hole mass estimate in NGC 1277 Graham_et_2016, Scharwachter_et_2016 ; nonetheless, the system still harbours one of the most massive black holes known, remaining a major outlier in the black hole-host galaxy scaling relations Walsh_et_2016 . Recent observations confirm the existence of further sources in the local Universe, with physical properties very similar to NGC 1277 Ferre-Mateu_et_2017 .

sources.summary: The paper excludes NGC 1277 because its peculiar morphology, disk-galaxy outlier status, and bottom-heavy-IMF stellar mass measurement could bias the scaling-relation regressions.

quotes.quote: NGC 1277 (peculiar morphology) and NGC 2787 are two disk galaxies which are potential outliers at the high- and low-mass end of our relations, respectively. They have a torquing effect on our regression lines, especially for the sub-category of galaxies with a disk (ES/S0). We have therefore excluded these galaxies from our regressions to avoid biasing the slope of our scaling relations. Furthermore, the stellar mass for NGC 1277 is measured from V-band imaging Graham:Durr:Savorgnan:2016 and a stellar mass-to-light ratio based on an unusual bottom heavy IMF Martin:2015 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1277 as a confirmed relic-galaxy benchmark, emphasizing its Perseus-cluster environment, low dark-matter fraction, and similarity to the strongest TNG50 relic analogue.

quotes.quote: It also shows similarities to the confirmed relic NGC 1277, regarding environment and dark matter fraction.

This is peculiarly similar to NGC 1277, that also has low dark matter-to-stellar mass fraction (log M_ DM /M_ * = -0.91^ +3.58 _ 1.44 ) according to orbit-based dynamical models from and which is a confirmed relic residing close to the centre of the Perseus Cluster (Abell 426) - one of the closest massive clusters known. Although the constraints on the dark matter fraction are poor - as one can see by the uncertainties (see Table tab:obs_properties ) - the similarity in environment to NGC 1277 is interesting.

This is motivated by the fact that Subhalo 6 is the candidate which satisfies most of the constraints imposed by the observational sample used as reference in this work, and even has a peculiar similarity with one of the real relic galaxies (NGC 1277).

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC1277 as one of the few fully characterized local massive compact relic galaxies, with rapid high-redshift star formation and red-nugget-like structure.

quotes.quote: So far, only three relics have been found and were fully characterised in the local Universe: NGC1277 Trujillo+14, Martin-Navarro+15_IMF_relic, Beasley+18 , Mrk 1216, and PGC 032873 Ferre-Mateu+17 . These three objects all show a very quick (time-scales <1 Gyr) high- z star formation and thus are consistent with having stars with a very old mean mass-weighted age ( 13 Gyr, almost as old as the Universe). Moreover, their morphology, kinematics, and density profiles perfectly resemble those of z > 2 red nuggets Ferre-Mateu+17 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC1277 as a local extreme relic, noting residual recent star formation, DoR 0.95, and recent results that it is dark-matter deficient to about five effective radii.

quotes.quote: This is motivated by the finding of Salvador-Rusinol2021_nature and Salvador-Rusinol22 , which detected residual sub-one per cent recent star formation in massive early-type galaxies and in the local relic NGC1277.

The average of these three single addenda in the equation is allowed to vary from 0 to 1, with 1 denoting systems that have formed the totality of their stellar mass in the shortest possible time As a reference, NGC1277, the most extreme relic known so far in the local Universe has a DoR of 0.95 .

However, this seems to be in strong contrast with recent results showing that NGC1277, one of the three very well-studied extreme relics in the local Universe, is dark matter deficient up to 5 \ Comeron23 .

gmw_00000499.name: SDSS J113603.51+550430.9
gmw_00000499.overview: SDSS J113603.51+550430.9 is the brightest group galaxy of the Abell 1318 cluster [arXiv:2312.05713]. The galaxy host, MCG +09-19-131, is associated with a lobed radio source that lacks a discernible radio core [arXiv:2312.05713].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J113603.51+550430.9 as Abell 1318's brightest group galaxy and links its host MCG +09-19-131 to a lobed radio source.

quotes.quote: Large-scale environment analysis To better understand the nature of the radio source, we investigated its surrounding environment in optical wavelengths in addition to radio. We considered the center position of A1318 as reported in the Abell catalog Abell1989 indicated at a redshift of z=0.0566 and then we selected the brightest SDSS sources (brighter than m_ R < 17.8) in the R band within a circular region of 500 \ from this position identified with SDSS J113603.51+550430.9 (MCG +09-19-131), located at a redshift of z=0.05707 . Using this optical source as a proxy, we computed all environmental parameters according to the procedure Massaro2019, Massaro2020b, Massaro2020a and . We found that the number of so-called cosmological neighbors, defined as all optical sources with SDSS magnitude flags indicating a galaxy-type object and having a spectroscopic z with z = z_ src z <0.005 , lying within 500 kpc, 1 Mpc and 2 Mpc is 18, 36, and 54, respectively. SDSS J113603.51+550430.9 is actually the brightest galaxy lying at a projected distance of 34 kpc from the centroid of the positions of all cosmological neighbors within 2 Mpc, computed at its source redshift.

Most of these sources though are not associated with Abell 1318. To the south (and unrelated from the main extended radio source), there is also what appears to be a lobed radio galaxy (marked as D, 170 kpc away in projection) associated with the galaxy host MCG +09-19-131, the system BGG, which does not show any discernible radio core. Interestingly, the optical galaxy host center appears to be shifted with respect to the center-point of the two lobes by around 15 \ ( 17 kpc). Further to the south-west, there is an unrelated (tailed) radio galaxy (marked as E, 190 kpc away in projection). The total (spectral) radio luminosity of the extended radio source (components A+B+C) is 4.6 10^ 24 , and of component D, associated with the BGG 1.9 10^ 23 (Table table:par ).

gmw_00000500.name: RA 11:35:59 DEC +55:07:12 (J2000)
aliases_external: Abell 1318 member galaxy at RA 11:35:59 Dec +55:07:12
gmw_00000500.overview: The galaxy RA 11:35:59 DEC +55:07:12 (J2000) is a member of the Abell 1318 cluster [arXiv:2312.05713]. It is a potential, though unlikely, host for an extended remnant radio active galactic nucleus, as it lacks compact radio emission and is not highly massive [arXiv:2312.05713].

sources.summary: The paper considers the Abell 1318 member at RA 11:35:59 Dec +55:07:12 as a possible but unlikely host for the extended remnant radio AGN.

quotes.quote: As we discuss in Section props , we did not find any association of flatter spectrum patches (indicating a radio core) within the radio source with any optical galaxies. One possibility is that the host is the galaxy at RA 11:35:59 DEC +55:07:12 (J2000), which is the only member of Abell 1318 located within the extended radio emission (within component B). However, the galaxy is not very massive (log M_ =10.8 based on the 2MASS magnitude M_K =-23.55 and using the relation in Cappellari2013 ) making it less likely associated with such an extended radio AGN. Moreover, it does not show any compact radio emission and it is very much offset with respect to the radio source barycenter.

gmw_00000501.name: NGC 5044
gmw_00000501.overview: NGC 5044 is a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0211603] located at the center of a rich, X-ray bright galaxy group [arXiv:1703.10835, arXiv:1103.5351]. The galaxy hosts a multiphase medium [arXiv:2403.03974] featuring active galactic nucleus-inflated cavities [arXiv:2403.03974], bright H-alpha filaments [arXiv:1407.3235, arXiv:2403.03974], and numerous cold molecular gas clouds [arXiv:2405.10683, arXiv:1711.10630].

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO observations of NGC 5044, finding central molecular associations, perturbed multiphase gas, bright H filaments, and signs of sloshing in its X-ray group.

quotes.quote: An ALMA observation of the early-type galaxy NGC 5044, which resides at the center of an X-ray bright group with a moderate cooling flow, detected 24 molecular structures within the central 2.5 kpc. The masses of the molecular structures vary from 3 10^5 M _ to 10^7 M _ and the CO(2-1) linewidths vary from 15 to 65 km s ^ 1 . Given the large CO(2-1) linewidths, the observed structures are likely giant molecular associations (GMAs) and not individual molecular clouds (GMCs). Only a few of the GMAs are spatially resolved and the average density of these GMAs yields a GMC volume filling factor of about 15 .

In this paper we present the results of a cycle 0 ALMA observation of NGC 5044 in the CO(2-1) emission line. The NGC 5044 group of galaxies is the X-ray brightest group in the sky and has a very smooth and nearly spherically symmetric large scale X-ray morphology. However, the central region of NGC 5044 is highly perturbed with many AGN-inflated cavities, cool X-ray filaments, cold fronts and multiphase gas (Buote et al. 2003; Gastaldello et al. 2009; David et al. 2009; 2011). NGC 5044 also hosts a system of very bright H filaments (Caon et al. 2000; Sun et al. 2014).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5044 as the central brightest group galaxy with dust and disturbed X-ray gas, whose two-Sersic fit and downward profile inflection mark its halo structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 5044 NGC 5044 is the central and brightest galaxy of a rich and X-ray bright group Mendel08 . Dust patches near the center of the galaxy have been detected by Colbert01 . X-ray observations of NGC 5044 show two cavities and cooler filamentary X-ray emission, thought to have been caused by a dynamical perturbation induced by the accretion of a low mass satellite Buote03, Gastaldello09 .

We found that this model converges to a best-fit solution, with physically meaningful value, for only two galaxies, NGC 5044 and NGC 5846. In these cases the fit yields very small S e rsic indices ( n_ 2 0.7 and n_ 2 0.4 , respectively) for the outer components. Specifically, we can distinguish the profiles of NGC 1399, NGC 5044 and NGC 5846 which show a downward inflection, from those of NGC 3923, NGC 4365 and NGC 4472, which instead have an upward inflection. Downward inflection are, instead, the natural cut-off of a low- n component. Adopting the an empirically-motivated two-component approach most common in the literature we find that four of our six galaxies (NGC 3923, NGC 4365, NGC 4472, NGC 1399) can be modelled by an inner S e rsic component and an outer exponential, while the other two objects (NGC 5044 and NGC 5846) are best fitted with a double S e rsic model.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5044 as a central group galaxy with radial H and X-ray filaments, ALMA molecular associations, no rotating disk, red-shifted absorption clumps, and CCA-like cooling from hot gas.
