In most cases, the WA clouds have been found to thrive in a multiphase medium with a range in , and even in v , 2004ApJ...611...68K,2017A&A...601A..17B,2014ApJ...793...61H,2013MNRAS.435.3028E,2009ApJ...690..773K,2016A&A...587A.129E and very rarely detected as a single cloud see for e.g., theoretical works by 2009MNRAS.393...83C . To quantify this effect, an important parameter is the absorption measure distribution (AMD). The AMD is a measure of the column density of the cloud as a function of its ionization parameter (AMD = d / d ). The shape of the AMD depends heavily on the irradiating SED and the density of the warm absorber, thereby serving as crucial diagnostics of the plasma as well as AGN-WA relations 2019ApJ...881...78A . In most cases the AMD has been found to be multi-peaked, a testimony of the existence of multi-phase ionic absorbers along the line of sight, with unstable ionization phases in the gaps of the distribution 2007ApJ...663..799H , roughly corresponding to the ionization states of 0.5< ( / )<1.5 . Interestingly, such ionization gaps have been detected in sample studies of local AGN 2007MNRAS.379.1359M,2014MNRAS.441.2613L . The measure of AMD also gives us the density profile of the outflows ( n_ e ^ ), where is the radial gradient of the density and for WA the values are =1.236 0.034 2013MNRAS.430.1102T,2016MNRAS.457.3896L . The density profile is a crucial parameter to estimate the mass outflow rate ( ) and kinetic luminosity ( ) of the outflows. WA response to changing AGN continuum luminosity and shape holds the key to crucial information on the origin and acceleration mechanisms of WA. For example., the variations in the ionization state of the WA in response to the AGN continuum and flux changes can yield the recombination timescale t_ R , which is the time required by the free electrons to recombine to a bound energy level of an ion. t_ R is inversely proportional to the cloud electron density 1999ApJ...512..184N , and an estimate of the cloud electron density ( n_ e ) gives us the distance of the cloud ( ) from the central source from the definition of . Several works using high resolution spectra in X-rays have successfully used this technique to measure the density and hence the distance. The range of density estimated is n_ e 10^ 4 10^ 11 , indicating a distance of a few 10 s of down to 0.01 2012ApJ...746L..20K,2007ApJ...659.1022K,2012A&A...539A.117K,2014ApJ...793...61H,2003ApJ...599..933N,2013ApJ...776...99R . Long term monitoring campaigns of well known variable sources such as NGC 4051, NGC 3783, NGC 5548, Mkn 509, have been undertaken in the last two decades to use this method. For example, the soft X-ray spectra of the source Mrk 509 revealed five WA components with , and the distance of the three components could be put around 5-400 , where the slow outflow components point to an origin in the NLR or torus region 2012A&A...539A.117K . Similarly, the opacity variations in X-ray WA of NGC 3783 revealed deep absorption troughs due to Fe M-shell absorbers and the structure of the absorber is heavily clumped, disfavoring a continuous radial range of ionization structures 2005ApJ...622..842K . The recent discovery of a transient, low ionized ( 1.8 ), but high column density ( 23 ) X-ray obscurer during a monitoring campaign of the source NGC 5548 has revealed a new type of X-ray obscurer See Figure Fig:pcygni left panel, 2014Sci...345...64K . It has been suggested that a long-lasting clumpy stream of ionized gas passed across our line of sight causing simultaneous soft X-ray and UV absorption troughs. The obscurer blocks nearly 90 of the soft X-rays. The outflow velocity of the gas was noted to be around five times faster than those in the persistent outflow, and are located at a distance of only a few light-days from the central SMBH, indicative of an accretion disk wind reaching beyond BLR. These outflows are best explained by magnetic acceleration rather than by radiative acceleration, the latter leading to more radial and equatorial flow. The UV counterpart of the obscuring flow of NGC 5548 exhibited components of broad, blue-shifted absorption associated with Ly , N V, Si IV, and C IV in the COS spectra 2019ApJ...881..153K . Although we still do not know the exact cause of such an obscuration flow, it has been suggested that a collapse of the broad line region has led to the outburst and triggered the obscuring event. There has been a few other incidences of this event in other sources such as Mrk 335 2013ApJ...766..104L , NGC 985 2016A&A...586A..72E , and NGC 3783 2017A&A...607A..28M . Due to high column density, the X-ray spectral features of these obscuration events do not have any resolved features in the soft X-rays, and hence the UV (where individual absorption lines could be identified) provides measurement of the kinematics and ionization state of these absorbers. The lower ionization states of the WA clouds are likely to contain dust. The dust component greatly enhances the effective thrust due to the radiation pressure, somewhere between a factor of 1-500 depending on column density, and they may become a critical component in feedback momentum and kinematics from the central SMBH 2008MNRAS.385L..43F,2018MNRAS.476..512I . However, identifying the presence of dust using X-ray spectra is not straight forward. Earlier attempts used X-ray and IR spectra to estimate the dust embedded in WA see for e.g., 1997MNRAS.291..403R,2001ApJ...554L..13L . In several sources, the implied dust column density obtained from X-rays agreed with the reddening measurements obtained using IR studies. To date however, only a few such attempts with multi-wavelength studies have been made see for e.g., for quantifying dust in absorbers for IC 4329A using spatialy resolved IR observations 2018A&A...619A..20M . Warm absorbers are rarely found in the radio loud galaxies, much similar to the phenomenon that BAL outflows are detected mostly in radio quiet QSOs. WA outflows have been detected in radio-loud galaxies such as 3C 390.3, 3C 120, 3C 382, 3C 445 2009ApJ...702L.187R,2010ApJ...725..803R,2010MNRAS.401L..10T,2012MNRAS.419..321T,2016ApJ...830...98T . This paucity in occurrence of WA in radio loud AGN may have deep implications on when the AGN creates an outflow and when it produces a jet (different modes of feedback). However, a direct physical relationship has not been established yet. Studies with samples of radio loud Seyfert-1 AGN detected a significant inverse correlation between the column density of the WA and the radio loudness parameter (R) of the jet 2019A&A...625A..25M . The relation could be explained from the perspective of a change in the magnetic field configuration (toroidal to poloidal), powering either the jet or the wind mode of outflow. Hence, when the jet is stronger, the WA is weaker and vice-versa. Interestingly, such anti-correlations between wind and jet properties have been seen in stellar mass black hole binaries 2009Natur.458..481N,2012MNRAS.422L..11P . The ultra-fast outflows The radically improved sensitivity and spectral resolution in the 2-10 energy range of , and over previous missions have opened up the avenue for detection of highly ionized winds from the SMBH observed as Fe XXV and Fe XXVI K-shell absorption lines in the region 7-9 . These absorbers may sometimes have velocities as high as 30 of the speed of light and calculations suggest that they carry considerable kinetic power required for feedback by the AGN i.e, >0.5 2010MNRAS.401....7H,2011ApJ...742...44T,2015MNRAS.451.4169G . Perhaps as much as 40 of nearby AGN show the presence of UFO 2010A&A...521A..57T,2011ApJ...742...44T,2013MNRAS.430...60G , with the outflow velocity spanning from 0.03-0.3 , ionization parameter ( / ) 3-6 , and column density ( / ) 22-24 . Understanding how these winds are formed, in particular, the interplay between the accretion and ejection flows at small radii in the accretion disk can give us vital clues on the energetic output of these outflows and hence the feedback to galaxies. The origin of the UFOs is still debated, but radiatively driven flows may not be an efficient driver in these cases as the UFOs are higly ionized and do not have much opacity in UV or X-ray lines 2014ApJ...789...19H . Magneto hydrodynamical effects in the accretion disks (magnetic fields threading the disks) may play a vital role in generating these fast outflows 1982MNRAS.199..883B,2012ASPC..460..181K,2010ApJ...715..636F,2017NatAs...1E..62F,2018ApJ...852...35K . The radial distance of these outflows estimated using the virial velocity is 10^ 2-4 r_ s , where r_ s is the Schwarzschild radius of the SMBH, indicating proximity to the accretion disk 2015MNRAS.451.4169G . Strong correlation between the bolometric luminoisty ( ) of the AGN and the kinetic luminosity ( ) of the UFO have been detected, indicating that the faster winds are more likely to be driven in higher luminosity or high Eddington AGN, however, they may not necessarily be radiation driven 2015MNRAS.451.4169G . While its possible that Eddington limited AGN may produce fast, radiatively driven winds, more generally the correlation could arise as a result of the increasing dominance of the inner accretion disk (and its MHD effects) down to small radii in more luminous sources 2013MNRAS.430.1102T . The wind velocity v has been found to be separately significantly correlated with with a slope =0.4_ 0.2 ^ +0.3 . Similarly the momentum flux of the radiation ( ) and that of the wind ( ) are strongly corretaled with a slope of =1.2_ 0.7 ^ +0.8 . There have been suggestions that the UFOs may originate at the base of the radio jet 2004A&A...413..535G , but, the connection between radio jets and the UFOs is not solidly established yet. By comparing samples of radio loud and radio quiet AGN, it has been found that the UFO detection fraction in both these cases are similar ( 50 20 ), implying that the presence of relativistic jets does not preclude the existence of these winds 2014MNRAS.443.2154T . The opening angle of the UFOs is a vital parameter to estimate the total kinetic power carried by the winds and hence a measure of the feedback, but the detection of the absorption lines alone does not provide us this information. The PCygni profiles of FeK emission and absorption lines detected in a few bright quasars gives us a rare view of the opening angle of the UFOs 2015Sci...347..860N,2019ApJ...884...80R . The principle here is that the UFO plasma absorbs (along the line of sight) and also re-emits (isotropic) and hence if we detect both emission and absorption from the same plasma, the relative strength can give us an estimate of the opening angle. For example, the PCygni profiles detected in the and observations of the quasar PDS 456 yielded a solid angle (filled by the winds) =3.2 0.6 sr (See Figure Fig:pcygni right panel). The high z quasars are an important laboratory for studying the AGN phenomenon, as these are the periods (redshift z 1-3 ) when the quasar luminosity function peaked. It is commonly believed that the SMBH `negative' feedback may have produced quasar downsizing, and hence studying feedback through outflows in the quasars at these redshifts are important to understand the luminosity function evolution. Early studies by 2002ApJ...579..169C detected high velocity Fe XXVI absorption features in the lensed BAL quasar APM 08279+5255 at a redshift of z=3.91 . 2018A&A...610L..13D and 2015ApJ...799...82C detected similar such high velocity outflows in high redshift quasars. However, high redshift studies to date remain only a few and the next generation instruments such as and can shed more light on these interesting sources. Apart from the so called traditional UFOs detected in the 6-9 as Fe absorption features, there have been detections of high velocity outflows using high resolution grating spectra in the soft X-rays ( 0.3-2 ), such as in PG 1211+143 2003MNRAS.346.1025P,2016AN....337..518P,2018ApJ...854...28R , IRAS 17020+4544 2015ApJ...813L..39L , Mrk 590 2015ApJ...798....4G , Ark 564 2013ApJ...772...66G and PDS 456 2020ApJ...895...37R . The UFOs detected in the soft X-ray spectra have velocities in the range 10,000 0.2c , but with a much lower column density ( ( / ) 22 ) and the ionization parameter ( ( / ) 3 ) compared to that of the traditional UFOs detected in the FeK band. The response of the UFOs to varying AGN continuum flux serves as an important indicator of how and when these outflows are generated. Studies on the variable quasar PDS 456 with , and observations found that the centroid energy of the blue shifted FeK absorption profile increases with AGN luminosity, and the wind outflow velocity correlates with the hard X-ray luminosity as v/ L_ 7-30 ^ 0.22 0.04 , which indicates that the wind is predominantly radiatively driven due to the high Eddington ratio of the source 2017MNRAS.472L..15M . Such occurences have also been found in other sources accreting at high Eddington ratio indicating that the UFOs may be driven by intense radiation pressure in sources accreting at or close to the Eddington rate, such as in APM 08279+5255 2003ApJ...595...85C , PG 1211+143 2003MNRAS.345..705P , IRAS F11119+3257 2015Natur.519..436T , 1H 0707-495 2016MNRAS.461.3954H , and IRAS 13224-3809 2017MNRAS.469.1553P . Interesting anti-correlations with the 3-10 luminosity and the UFO absorption equivalent width have been detected for the source IRAS 13224-3809 2017Natur.543...83P indicating a connection between the accretion process and the outflow generation. Although rare, there has been a few tentative detections of ultra-fast ``inflows" indicative of feeding of the black hole accretion disk. These features are detected as absorption at 5.5 and under the assumption that they arise from H- or He- like Fe, implied infall velocities are estimated to be 0.15-0.2c see for e.g., detected in Mrk 509 using BeppoSAX 2005A&A...442..461D . More recent findings of transient absorption signatures of highly ionized Fe lines in the 5-6 pointed towards a short lived ultra-fast inflow with an inflow velocity of v 0.3c in nearby quasars PG 1211+143, 2005ApJ...633L..81R,2018MNRAS.481.1832P .

Similar results have been found for the WA and UFO absorbers detected in the source PG 1211+143 2015ApJ...805...17F . The MHD winds have been detected in several LMXBs, where the magnetic processes are not only involved in driving the outflows but also for mediating mass transfer within the disk itself see for e.g., and references therein 2019ApJ...886..104T,2006Natur.441..953M,2008ApJ...680.1359M,2015ApJ...814...87M,2017NatAs...1E..62F,2012ApJ...750...27N Radiative winds: Radiation pressure due to UV and X-ray absorption lines are important mechanisms for driving the outflows where the cloud opacity is high enough 2004ApJ...616..688P . For highly ionized clouds the UV and X-ray line opacities may not be sufficient enough to drive them, and Compton scattering can provide the necessary momentum to the outflow, particularly for AGN radiating near/at Eddington rate 2003MNRAS.345..657K,2010MNRAS.402.1516K . An important quantity in the radiatively driven flows is the force multiplier M( , t ) which measures the thrust by radiation on the clouds. Theoretical studies of radiatively driven outflows in variable sources see for e.g., NGC 5548 2019ApJ...882...99D found that M( , t ) starts to decrease for 1 , and the decrease is gradual and can be non-monotonic as M( , t ) can increase by a factor of a few in the range =10-1000 . This increase of M at high is for very low optical depths, so even if line driving is important for as high as 1000, the outflow may produce only very weak features that are hard to detect in absorption. Recent studies have pointed out that most of the diversity detected in the AGN accretion/ejection flows, in the context of radiatively driven winds, can be described well by varying just two fundamental parameters of the SMBH: and 2019A&A...630A..94G . It has been conjectured that physical models of line-driven disc winds can open up an avenue to estimate black hole growth 2018arXiv181101966N , because there is possibly a direct connection between the mass accretion rate of the SMBH and wind power (accretion ejection connection). Motivated by the discoveries of UFOs in PG1211+143 2003MNRAS.345..705P with velocity 0.1c and column density 10^ 24 , suggesting that the clouds could have been Compton-thick at smaller radii, it has been proposed that black holes accreting at or above the Eddington rate probably produce optically thick winds and drive them by radiative pressure, both in the cases of SMBHs and LMXBs 2003MNRAS.345..657K . For the particular case of PG1211+143 it was found that the mass outflow rates of the winds 1.6 was comparable to the Eddington accretion rate, implying a radiatively driven accretion disk wind.

sources.summary: The paper reports persistent low-redshift X-ray absorption in PG1211+143, interpreting it as inflowing matter or gravitationally redshifted absorption near the supermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: galaxies: active -- galaxies: Seyfert: quasars: general -- galaxies: individual: PG1211+143 -- X-ray: galaxies \ observations of the luminous Seyfert galaxy \ have been remarkably productive, perhaps rightly so as a target in the 'guaranteed observing time' awarded to the late Martin Turner, PI of the primary focal plane camera, EPIC (Turner \ 2001). An early observation showed a strong, blue-shifted absorption line of highly ionized Fe, interpreted as an outflow or wind, with a line-of-sight-velocity of 0.15 0.01 (Pounds \ 2003; Pounds Page 2006). Archival data from \ and the Japanese x-ray observatory \ subsequently showed that such ultra-fast, highly-ionized outflows (UFOs) are relatively common in nearby, luminous AGN (Tombesi \ 2010, 2011; Gofford \ 2013). While those archival searches typically reported a single velocity, in the few cases where an AGN was observed repeatedly, the wind velocity was often different. \ is the best example from the \ data archive, with wind velocities of 0.173c, 0.139c and 0.196c, separated by 5 years and 6 months respectively (Cappi \ 2009).

We now report a weaker (lower column) and more persistent absorption component at an observed redshift of 0.123, revealed in the combined RGS1 and RGS2 data from the full 7-orbit observation, with a high on-target exposure of 1.24Ms. If interpreted as a Doppler shift,the observed redshift would correspond to an inflow velocity of 0.038c and (free-fall) location at 1400 R _ g , beyond the putative tearing radius but still inside the gravitational 'sphere of influence' of the SMBH in .

sources.summary: The paper finds variable 4.5--5.8 keV emission and significant line features in PG 1211+143, interpreting the delayed response as reverberated redshifted Fe K emission from the inner accretion disk.

quotes.quote: In particular, the 3 sources, namely PG 1211+143, NGC 4051 and NGC 3783, showed the presence of a variable emission feature in the 4.5--5.8 keV band, characterized by an increase of its intensity after the flare peak. Because of the observed variability pattern, this feature seems to be ascribable to a reverbered redshifted relativistic component of the Fe K line.

Only 3 (PG 1211+143, NGC 4051 and NGC 3783) out of the 7 studied sources show highly significant emission and absorption features. Their light curves are showed in Fig. fig:lightcurvesPG - fig:lightcurvesNG37 . Moreover, these 3 sources display variable emission features in the 4.5--5.8 keV band, the intensity of this component rising after the flare peak (see Fig. fig:contourplotsPG - fig:contourplotsNG37 ). This suggests a delayed response with respect to the continuum emission.
The obtained values are: 32 _ S for PG 1211+143, 800 _ S for NGC 4051, 32 _ S and 76 _ S from the first and the second flare of NGC 3783 respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1211+143 as a high-Eddington NLS1 observed twice, with soft-state-like X-ray spectra and possible partial covering or reflection affecting bolometric corrections.

quotes.quote: Mrk 335 and PG 1211+143 These objects are both NLS1 AGN. We present two observations for each of these objects, separated by almost seven years in the case of Mrk 335 and three years for PG 1211+143. The analysis of the observations used in this study and the previous analysis in VF07 both place them well into the `high' Eddington regime ( _ Edd > 0.1 ), with X-ray spectra which resemble the `soft' states of GBHs.

For high Eddington objects, it is likely that complex outflows may play a role in blurring our view of the nuclear emission. It is possible to model the spectrum with partial covering or strong reflection due to light bending 2004MNRAS.349.1435M for both of these objects, again making the intrinsic X-ray luminosity difficult to determine. This would naturally explain the large spread seen in the bolometric corrections (a factor 2.9 for Mrk 335; a factor 3.7 for PG 1211+143).

sources.summary: The paper uses PG 1211+143 as the prototype, noting its known Ly counterpart for one UFO but no broad Ly absorption for a higher-velocity UFO in COS or FUSE spectra.

quotes.quote: PG 1211+143 The prototype for this survey, PG 1211+143 has one UFO at a velocity of v_ out = -17 300 km s^ 1 Danehkar18 which is associated with H 1 Ly absorption at v_ out = -16 980 km s^ 1 Kriss18 . In the sample, PG 1211+143, also has another UFO at v_ out = -0.128 , which has been extensively studied Pounds14,Pounds16 . We show two spectra of the region of the predicted Ly counterpart to this higher velocity UFO in Figures fig:1i and fig:1j .

The illustrated region is spectrally complex, containing weak emission lines of S 4 and He 2 intrinsic to PG 1211+143, as well as foreground interstellar absorption features, which are marked in red in the COS spectrum in Figure fig:1i . The COS spectrum was taken with grating G140L, which has significantly lower resolution (resolving power 2000 ) than the FUSE spectrum in Figure fig:1j (resolving power 18 000 ), and it is the sum of the three exposures obtained in the combined Chandra and HST campaign Danehkar18,Kriss18 . Neither the FUSE nor the COS spectrum (or the individual exposures in the COS spectrum) shows evidence for broad Ly absorption.

sources.summary: The paper models PG 1211+143 as a canonical powerful UFO Seyfert, fitting its X-ray absorption and emission with a UV line-driven disc wind simulation.

quotes.quote: We fit these simulated wind spectra to data from the archetypal UFO source PG 1211+143 and show that they broadly reproduce the depth and velocity of the iron absorption lines seen. This directly demonstrates that UV line driving is a viable mechanism to launch even the fastest UFOs.

We apply our disc wind model to the observational data. We choose PG 1211+143 ( z=0.081 ) as a `canonical' powerful UFO target pou03,ree03,ree09 . We analyse the European Photon Imaging Camera (EPIC)-pn epic archival data (ID: 0112610101, 49.0 ks exposure time) in the XMM-Newton satellite xmm .

We conclude that the X-ray spectrum of PG 1211+143 can be fairly well described by the wind model, showing that both the two sets of absorption lines and the strong emission line can be explained as arising in a single UV line driven disc wind structure.

sources.summary: The paper studies PG1211+143 as a luminous Seyfert galaxy with variable ultra-fast ionized winds, multiple outflow velocities, and a hot Comptonised continuum component.

quotes.quote: Here we identify a variable continuum component in the same data set, well-modelled by a hot thermal Comptonised spectrum that could represent cooling radiation from the shocked gas. galaxies: active -- galaxies: Seyfert: quasars: general -- galaxies: individual: PG1211+143 -- X-ray: galaxies X-ray spectra from an \ observation of the luminous Seyfert galaxy \ in 2001 identified strongly blue-shifted absorption lines of highly ionized gas, corresponding to a sub-relativistic outflow velocity of 0.15 0.01 (Pounds \ 2003; Pounds Page 2006).

\ is the best example from the \ data archive, with wind velocities of 0.173c, 0.139c and 0.196c, separated by 5 years and 6 months respectively (Cappi \ 2009). An extended 5-weeks \ observation of \ in 2014 showed a more complex velocity structure in the highly ionized wind, with three primary (high column density) outflow velocities of v 0.066c , v 0.13c and v 0.19c (Pounds \ 2016), none being consistent with the wind velocity observed in 2001.

Extending the difference spectrum fit over the wider 0.4--10 keV band revealed a 'soft excess' and unacceptable overall fit ( ^ 2 of 186 for 156 d.o.f.). Adding a second Comptonised component, with the input photon temperature and scattering electron temperature tied across both components recovered the excellent fit ( ^ 2 of 152 for 154 d.o.f.), for a common electron temperature of 43 keV and electron scattering optical depths of 0.29 and 0.12, respectively for the 'hard' and 'soft' spectral components, perhaps indicative of spatial structure in the high temperature shock.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1211+143 as compact in radio images, dominated by AGN radio emission, and possibly showing a self-absorbed base of a low-power or frustrated jet.

quotes.quote: PG 1211+143 appears compact in both uGMRT and VLA images. We had concluded from our uGMRT study that the AGN rather than the stellar activity was the primary contributor of radio emission in this source Silpa20 . The 2-frequency uGMRT-VLA spectral index image reveals a marginally steep spectrum ( _ 2-freq = -0.59 0.07 ) uGMRT core, which could suggest either jet or lobe emission on sub-arcsec scales or optically thin synchrotron emission from an AGN-driven wind. Interestingly, the in-band VLA spectral index for the core is 0.5 0.1 which is at the cusp of flat-steep division. This could suggest that the steeper spectrum component associated with the radio outflow may have been missed out by the higher-resolution VLA observations and what is detected here is the synchrotron self-absorbed base of a low-power or `frustrated' jet.
The radio emission in PG 0049+171, PG 1211+143, PG 1244+026 and PG 1426+015 is likely to be arising from the synchrotron self-absorbed base of a low-power or ‘frustrated' jet.

sources.summary: PG1211+143 is presented as a bright narrow-line quasar whose XMM spectra show blue-shifted absorption, high-velocity ionized outflow, and enough mechanical power to contribute to its X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: PG1211+143 (at z=0.0809) is a prototypical `Big Blue Bump' quasar, and one of the brightest AGN in the soft X-ray band (Elvis \ 1991). It is also classified (Kaspi \ 2000) as a Narrow Line Seyfert 1 galaxy (FWHM H 1800 km s ^ 1 ), with a small central black hole mass ( 4 10^ 7 M _ ) for an object of bolometric luminosity 4 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 , implying that the accretion rate is close to the Eddington limit. The initial 50 ks observation of PG1211+143 with XMM-Newton in 2001 revealed several blue-shifted absorption lines, interpreted by Pounds \ (2003) as evidence for a highly ionised outflow with a velocity v 0.09c, a conclusion primarily based on identifying the strongest 7 keV feature with FeXXVI Ly .

Modelling of the broad-band spectrum of PG1211+143 (Pounds and Reeves 2006) has quantified both the absorbed and re-emitted fluxes for the ionised outflow, yielding a covering factor CF 0.1 for the high velocity, highly ionised outflow, and M 3.5 yr ^ 1 . This outflow rate compares with M_ Edd = 1.6 yr ^ 1 for a non-rotating SMBH of mass 4 10 ^7 (Kaspi \ 2000) accreting at an efficiency of 10 . The mechanical energy of the highly ionised outflow is then 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 , easily sufficient to power a substantial component of the X-ray emission of PG1211+143.

sources.summary: The paper finds variable Fe K absorption in PG 1211+143, ruling out local hot gas and identifying the absorber as a mildly relativistic AGN outflow.

quotes.quote: We present a comparison between the 2001 XMM-Newton and 2005 Suzaku observations of the quasar, PG 1211+143 at z=0.0809 . Variability is observed in the 7 keV iron K-shell absorption line (at 7.6 keV in the quasar frame), which is significantly weaker in 2005 than during the 2001 XMM-Newton observation. From a recombination timescale of <4 years, this implies an absorber density n>4 10^ 3 cm ^ 3 , while the absorber column is 5 10^ 22 <N_ H <1 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 . Thus the sizescale of the absorber is too compact (pc scale) and the surface brightness of the dense gas too high (by 9-10 orders of magnitude) to arise from local hot gas, such as the local bubble, group or Warm/Hot Intergalactic Medium (WHIM), as suggested by McKernan et al. (2004, 2005). Instead the iron K-shell absorption must be associated with an AGN outflow with mildly relativistic velocities.

sources.summary: PG 1211+143 is analyzed as a spectrally complex nearby quasar whose XMM-Newton observations show soft-band lags, flux-dependent lag-energy behavior, and possible disk/corona or wind reprocessing.

quotes.quote: We present the timing analysis of the four archived XMM- Newton observations of PG 1211+143. The source is well-known for its spectral complexity, comprising a strong soft-excess and different absorption systems. Soft energy band (0.3-0.7 keV) lags are detected over all the four observations, in the frequency range 6 10^ 4 Hz, where hard lags, similar to those observed in black hole X-ray binaries, are usually detected in smaller mass AGN. The lag magnitude is energy-dependent, showing two distinct trends apparently connectable to the two flux levels at which the source is observed. The results are discussed in the context of disk- and/or corona-reprocessing scenarios, and of disk wind models.

sources.summary: The paper treats PG1211+143 as the archetypal ultra-fast-outflow Seyfert, where X-ray observations reveal persistent, variable, massive winds and a complex multi-velocity absorption structure.

quotes.quote: X-ray spectra from an \ observation of the luminous Seyfert galaxy \ in 2001 provided the first detection of strongly blue-shifted absorption lines of highly ionized gas in a non-BAL Active Galactic Nucleus (AGN), corresponding to a sub-relativistic outflow velocity of 0.1 (Pounds \ 2003), later adjusted to 0.15 0.01 with the identification of additional absorption lines and broad-band spectral modelling (Pounds Page 2006). Further observations of \ over several years with , \ and \ found the high velocity outflow to be persistent but of variable strength (Reeves \ 2008). Evidence that the outflow in \ was both massive and energetic - with potential importance for galaxy feedback (King 2003, 2005) - came from the detection of a P-Cygni line profile and other broad emission features by combining the 2001, 2004 and 2007 \ EPIC spectra (Pounds Reeves 2009).

An extended \ observation of \ in 2014 has now provided uniquely high quality X-ray spectra of , revealing previously unseen velocity structure in the 6--10 keV energy band. Spectral modelling of data from the high-energy pn camera (Strueder 2001) identified the observed absorption lines with resonance and higher order transitions of highly-ionized Fe, consistent with two distinct outflow velocities v 0.066c and v 0.13c (Pounds \ 2016a: hereafter P16).

sources.summary: The review identifies PG 1211+143 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 quasar with ultrafast-outflow absorption features, successful wind and disc-absorption fits, and an XRISM simulation test.

quotes.quote: Early observations revealed absorption-like features above 7 keV (rest-frame) in the distant ( z=3.91 ) lensed quasar APM 08279+5255 , the nearby ( z=0.184 ) luminous quasar PDS 456 , and the narrow-line Seyfert 1 quasar, PG 1211+143 . Attributed to K-shell absorption lines from Fe xxv (He-like, 6.70 keV) and Fe xxvi (H-like, 6.97 keV) implies relativistic velocities between 0.1-0.3 .

As shown in Figure fig:pdspg , the MHD- and radiative-driven winds describe XMM-Newton data of PDS 456 and PG 1211+143 relatively well. The model was shown to fit the spectra of PG 1211+143 (Figure fig:refabs ) and IRAS 13224-3809 . A 100 ks XRISM-Resolve simulation of PG 1211+143 is shown in Figure fig:refabs .

sources.summary: The paper treats PG 1211+143 as a luminous NLS1/quasar with complex, multi-phase, highly variable UFOs spanning soft and hard X-ray bands.

quotes.quote: PG 1211+143 is a well-studied and luminous NLS1/quasar at a redshift of z=0.0809 , showing a typical X-ray luminosity of 10^ 44 erg/s and a bright optical flux with a strong `Big Blue Bump'. This source is well-known for its spectral complexity and the presence of highly variable and multi-phase UFOs e.g., 2003Pounds,2007Pounds,2009Pounds,2014Pounds,2016Pounds,2016Pounds2UFO,2018Reeves . The velocity range of these UFOs spans from -18000 km/s to -81000 km/s 2016Pounds,2018Danehkar , revealing absorption lines across both soft and hard X-ray bands.

Moreover, a corresponding UV counterpart to the UFO, outflowing at a comparable velocity, was documented by 2018Kriss . In addition, a soft time lag of 500 s at 10^ 4 Hz 2011deMarco and a hard lag up to 3 ks at 10^ 5 Hz 2018Lobban were reported in this source.

sources.summary: The paper lists PG 1211+143 among outliers to the proposed dust composition, with residuals near the 10 micron feature and requiring CDEOl1 instead of MieOl01 in the acceptable fit.

quotes.quote: We can see that five objects (Mrk 1210, NGC 7213, PG 0804+761, PG 1211+143, and PG 1351+640) are outliers in this distribution with ^2_ >2 . The middle panel of Figure fig:all_residuals shows the residuals of the spectral fits for our 49 objects using this combination, and it highlights these five objects. Comparing these residuals with the ones obtained in the best fits (top panel), we can see that the main difference is that the latter have larger ones ( >10 ) at 9 m < < 12 m for NGC 7213, PG 0804+761, PG 1211+143 and PG 1351+640.

For NGC 7213, PG 0804+761, PG 1211+143, and PG 1351+640, the residuals of the particular combination differ from the ones of the best fit mainly at 8 m < < 13 m , meaning that the former can not reproduce well the 10 \ m silicate feature in the spectra of these objects. To obtain an acceptable fit model, NGC 7213 needs MieOl2, CDEOl1, CDEOl3, and DL2 instead of MieOl01 and MieOl3; PG 0804+761 needs CDEOl1, DL01, and Min3 instead of MieOl01 and MieOl3; PG 1211+143 needs CDEOl1 instead of MieOl01; and PG 1351+640 needs CDEOl1, DL01, and Min3 instead of MieOl3.

sources.summary: The paper treats PG1211+143 as a bright narrow-line quasar with strong soft X-ray emission, absorption lines from highly ionized gas, and a fast ionized outflow from the inner disc.

quotes.quote: We report on the analysis of a 60 ksec \ observation of the bright, narrow emission line quasar . Absorption lines are seen in both EPIC and RGS spectra corresponding to H- and He-like ions of Fe, S, Mg, Ne, O, N and C. The observed line energies indicate an ionised outflow velocity of 24000 km s ^ 1 . The highest energy lines require a column density of N_ H 5 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 , at an ionisation parameter of log 3.4. If the origin of this high velocity outflow lies in matter being driven from the inner disc, then the flow is likely to be optically thick within a radius 130 Schwarzschild radii, providing a natural explanation for the Big Blue Bump (and strong soft X-ray) emission in .

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes PG 1211+143's X-ray spectrum, finding a slower about 3000 outflow with absorption and broad emission lines, while not fully excluding a high-velocity component.

quotes.quote: We analyze the X-ray spectrum of the quasar PG 1211+143 observed with the CCD and grating spectrometers on board . Using an ion by ion fitting model we find an outflow component of about 3000 \ that includes absorption lines of K-shell and L-shell ions of the astrophysically abundant elements. We also identify and include in our model broad (FWHM = 6000 ) emission lines from H-like ions of C, N, O, and Ne, and He-like ions of O, Ne, and Mg. The outflow velocity we find is an alternative interpretation of the data and is in contrast with the ultra high velocity of 24000 \ reported previously for this object. Nevertheless, we can not completely rule out the presence of a high velocity component due to the poor signal-to-noise ratio of the data.

sources.summary: The review identifies PG1211+143 as one of the strongest redshifted absorption-line detections, with highly ionized iron and both red- and blueshifted structures suggesting linked inflow and outflow.

quotes.quote: The two most significant detections to date, PG1211+143 and Mrk509, are shown in Figures 4 and 5, respectively. As for the blueshifted absorption lines illustrated above (Section 2.1), the most natural explanation for such absorption components is in terms of resonant absorption by highly ionized (H-like or He-like) iron. In this case however the measured energy shifts correspond to receding velocities v 0.1-0.4 .

Also PG1211+143 has exhibited both type of structures, although not simultaneously, suggesting maybe a fundamental link between the two phenomena. Models producing strong redshift? Two possible causes for those (mildly) relativistic redshifted lines have been proposed: either gravitational redshift of photons near the massive black hole or infall of matter onto the black hole. In the former scenario, the absorbing matter would have to be located very near the black hole, at a few R _g . It could be in the form of a rotating absorbing corona, located on top of the source-plus-disk system, as proposed by Ruszkowski Fabian (2000), or a kind of ``self-absorption'' effect at the inner edge of a nuclear outflow/jet (Yaqoob Serlemitsos 2005). Alternatively, the matter may be infalling onto the black hole with a diffuse (wind) or clumpy (blobs) structure. The fact that both Mrk509 and PG1211+143 do also possess fast outflows (Section 2.1) suggests that inflows may be linked to outflows.

sources.summary: The paper discusses PG 1211+143 as a large-black-hole-mass AGN with a soft/negative lag whose longer timescale is consistent with mass scaling from lower-mass soft-lag sources.

quotes.quote: One of the largest-mass sources ( M _ BH 10^ 7-8 M _ ) showing a soft/negative lag is PG 1211+143 (De Marco et al 2011), whose lag spectrum appears ``shifted'' by about one order of magnitude (with the lag frequency, , and amplitude, , found at 1-6 10^ 4 Hz and 10^ 2 s, respectively) with respect to those measured in other low mass sources (i.e. 10^ 3 Hz and 20-30 s, Emmanoulopoulos et al 2011). In De Marco et al (2011) we speculated that this difference could be simply explained if the time-scales in PG 1211+143 are scaled-up by the BH mass, estimated to be about 10-100 times the mass of the other AGN for which X-ray lags have been recorded so far.

The soft/negative lags detected in our analysis are perfectly consistent (in both amplitude and frequency) with those already reported in the literature for eight of the sources of our sample (Ark 564, Ar e valo et al 2006; 1H0707-495, Zoghbi et al 2010; Mrk 1040, Tripathi et al 2011; Mrk 766, MCG-6-30-15, Emmanoulopoulos et al 2011; RE J1034+396, Zoghbi Fabian 2011; NGC 3516, Turner et al 2011; PG 1211+143, De Marco et al 2011), even when slightly different energy bands are adopted.

This result confirms our previous inference (De Marco et al 2011), whereby the negative lag detected in PG 1211+143 was suggested to represent the large mass counterpart of negative lags detected in lower mass sources (such as 1H0707-495).

sources.summary: The paper highlights PG 1211+143 as a polarized, time-variable broad HI absorption system, with X-ray variability suggesting a compact variable AGN continuum source.

quotes.quote: Time Variability The most dramatic examples of polarized spectra are seen in PG 1211+143 and PG 1440+356. Not only are these spectra polarized, but they are also time variable on timescales of 30 minutes (Figures fig:pg1211var and fig:pg1440var ).
As the X-ray light curves of PG 1211+143 and PG 1440+356 show (Figure fig:xrayvar ), the X-ray emitting central region can be highly variable on a timescale of about 30 minutes.
PG 1211+143 This broad absorption line system was undetected by condon85 ; when it was observed by ho08 with Arecibo, standing waves were present in the data due to the strong continuum.

sources.summary: The review identifies PG1211+143 as the Palomar Green AGN used to explore soft X-ray excess, describing it as a bright soft-X-ray Narrow Line Seyfert 1 accreting near Eddington.

quotes.quote: The fast outflow in PG1211+143 Exploring the nature of the 'soft excess' in a sample of luminous Palomar Green AGN was a primary target in the Guaranteed Time programme awarded to Martin Turner, Project Scientist for the EPIC Camera on \ (Turner 2001). At that time the X-ray spectrum in AGN above 1 keV was expected to be a rather featureless power law apart from a fluorescent emission line at 6.4 keV from near-neutral Fe. One source, , showed a surprisingly 'noisy' X-ray spectrum which one of us (KP) volunteered to explore. , at a redshift of 0.0809 (Marziani \ 1996), is one of the brightest AGN at soft X-ray energies. It was classified (Kaspi et al 2000) as a Narrow Line Seyfert 1 galaxy (FWHM H 1800 km s ^ 1 ), with black hole mass 4 10^ 7 M_ and bolometric luminosity 4 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 , indicating a mean accretion rate close to Eddington.

sources.summary: The paper reports transient 0.3c redshifted X-ray absorption in the luminous Seyfert PG1211+143, interpreting it as evidence for chaotic accretion and disc tearing.

quotes.quote: The archetypal UFO is found in the luminous Seyfert galaxy PG1211+143 (Pounds \ 2003). To further explore the properties of this powerful UFO, the XMM-Newton X-ray Observatory carried out seven full-orbit ( 100 ks) observations over 5 weeks in 2014, with a total on-target exposure of 630 ks. The stacked data revealed a surprisingly complex spectrum, with the hard X-ray pn camera (Strueder \ 2001) finding multiple blue-shifted absorption lines, identified with highly ionized Fe between 6.6keV and 8.8 keV and outflow velocities of 0.06c , 0.13c and 0.18c (Pounds \ 2016a).

Motivated by these theoretical ideas and the paucity of relevant observations, we have carried out a thorough search for rapidly inflowing matter, on a range of timescales, as part of an on-going orbit-by-orbit analysis of the hard X-ray data from the 2014 XMM-Newton observation of PG1211+143.

sources.summary: The appendix calls PG 1211+143 a bright NLSy1 with mixed UFO literature; this study finds weak evidence from Fe variability but little low-energy alignment.

quotes.quote: PG 1211+143 is a very bright NLSy1 galaxy at soft X-ray energies and has varied results about the presence of UFO. For example, Zoghbi15 argue for no UFO, albeit the \ observations were acquired at a time when the line at 7.1 keV was weaker in strength, whilst Tombesi10 find an outflow at 0.128 and Pounds03 at 0.095 0.005c. This study only finds weak evidence for a UFO as no prominent peaks feature in the low-energy part of the spectrum, nor is there alignment to a high enough significance (bearing in mind that the significances are overestimated). Nevertheless, the Fe xxv is clearly visible as a peak and trough in the variability and count spectra, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper groups PG 1211+143 with Sy1 sources lacking absorption variations, preferring smooth gas and dust in a thin dust disk plus thicker gas torus despite possible line-of-sight interception.

quotes.quote: Panel 2 of Figure fig:cartoon_Sy1 (e.g. PG 0804+761 and PG 1211+143): Sy1 sources without absorption variations, small covering factors, small half opening angles of the dust, and low-to-intermediate half opening angles of the gas distribution. A smooth distribution of both dust and gas is preferred for these objects. All the above signatures could be explained with a geometrically-thin and smooth disk of dust and a geometrically-thick and smooth torus of gas, coexisting within the same radii Interestingly, inclination angles might intercept this thin disk along the LOS, despite its optical classification as Sy1.

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1211+143 as an AGN with mildly relativistic ultra-fast outflow, unresolved VLA radio structure, no VLBA detection, and likely extended outflow or diffuse-wind radio emission.

quotes.quote: PG 1211+143 PG 1211+143 is one of the AGNs with potentially mildly relativistic ultra-fast outflow 2003MNRAS.345..705P,2006MNRAS.372.1275P . The radio structure is unresolved in the VLA-A image 1994AJ....108.1163K . PG 1211+143 is not detected in our 5-GHz VLBA images, suggesting the lack of compact component on pc scales. The radio emission may be in the form of extended outflows or diffuse winds distributed on the scales of tens to hundreds of parsecs.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes PG1211+143 as a luminous Seyfert with UFO outflows and rare red-shifted inflow components reaching 0.3c and 0.1c near its SMBH.

quotes.quote: We discuss here why ultrafast ionized winds are relatively common in luminous Seyfert galaxies, while detection of the 0.3c inflow in \ remains a rare example. galaxies: active -- galaxies: Seyfert: quasars: general -- galaxies: individual: PG1211+143 -- X-ray: galaxies X-ray spectra from an \ observation of the luminous Seyfert galaxy \ in 2001 first identified strongly blue-shifted absorption lines of highly ionized gas, corresponding to a sub-relativistic outflow with a velocity of 0.15 0.01 (Pounds \ 2003; Pounds Page 2006).

The detection of red-shifted X-ray absorption spectra during part of the same 2014 \ observation of , corresponding to a substantial inflow of matter approaching the black hole at velocities up to 0.3c (Pounds \ 2018; hereafter Po18), offered the first observational support for the above accretion scenario.

In addition to the relatively weak high redshift absorber (at z=0.43 ), we confirm a stronger soft x-ray absorber at a redshift z=0.189 , corresponding to an inflow velocity 0.1c, and (free-fall) location at 200 R _ g .

sources.summary: The paper includes PG 1211+143 as a UFO-host NLSy1 with prior CO observations; Herschel data strongly change its SFR estimate, and it acts as a gas-luminosity outlier.

quotes.quote: PG 1211+143 and PG 1448+273 (hereafter PG 1211 and PG 1448) which were observed in CO(2-1) with ACA Shangguan_2020a .
Nevertheless, we observed that in the case of PG 1211, the SFR derived from the stellar emission and the dust emission vary by a factor of 10, highlighting the importance of the Herschel data.

We observe that the CO luminosity and the molecular gas mass correlate well with the AGN bolometric luminosities (Pearson =0.78-0.87 , p-value 0.01 ; Figure gas-SFR-AGN top left) when excluding PG 1211 which appears as an outlier. Note that the correlation is less clear when PG 1211 is considered ( =0.33-0.55 , p-value of 0.1-0.4 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports that PG1211+143 shows complex high-velocity ionized winds, including soft X-ray emission and absorption counterparts to two Fe K wind components.

quotes.quote: An extended \ observation of the luminous narrow line Seyfert galaxy \ in 2014 has revealed a more complex high velocity wind, with components distinguished in velocity, ionization level, and column density. Here we report soft x-ray emission and absorption features from the ionized outflow, finding counterparts of both high velocity components, v 0.129c and v 0.066c, recently identified in the highly ionized Fe K absorption spectrum. The lower ionization of the co-moving soft x-ray absorbers imply a distribution of higher density clouds embedded in the main outflow, while much higher column densities for the same flow component in the hard x-ray spectra suggest differing sight lines to the continuum x-ray source. galaxies: active -- galaxies: Seyfert: quasars: general -- galaxies: individual: PG1211+143 -- X-ray: galaxies

sources.summary: The paper describes PG 1211+143 as a compact quasar host showing some rotation, northern star-forming clumps, and metal-poor ionized zones.

quotes.quote: PG 1211+143 : The MUSE observation shows a compact system in which some evidence of rotation can be appreciated from the LOS velocity map. The data suggest two major clumps toward the north of the galaxy; they are consistent with star formation once the [ N ii ] upper limits are considered. The lack of [ N ii ] but the detection of H , H , and [ O iii ] suggests that these two zones are metal-poor.

sources.summary: The paper uses XMM-Newton data for the low-redshift quasar PG 1211+143 to test soft-excess models, including warm absorbers and comparable fits among three interpretations.

quotes.quote: We use the XMM-Newton data of the low redshift, optically bright quasar, PG 1211+143, and we compare it with the Narrow Line Seyfert 1 galaxy, 1H 0707-495, which has one of the strongest soft excesses seen.

The models are additionally modified by galactic absorption, cold absorption at the redshift of the source, and one (in 1H 0707-495) or two (in PG 1211+143) warm absorbers modeling the narrow absorption features. Thus, the only thing that differentiates the three models is the description of the origin of the soft excess. In PG 1211+143 the three models give virtually the same quality fits in terms of the reduced ^2 = 1.04 -1.06.

gmw_00000509.name: SDSS J001130.40 + 005751.7
gmw_00000509.overview: SDSS J001130.40 + 005751.7 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1101.2258, arXiv:2308.14444] at a redshift of z=1.4934 [arXiv:1101.2258]. It is classified as a low-synchrotron-peaked blazar [arXiv:1101.2258, arXiv:2308.14444] and uniquely exhibits a redder-when-brighter optical spectral behavior [arXiv:1101.2258].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J001130.40 + 005751.7 as the only FSRQ in its sample with redder-when-brighter behavior, a strong negative spectral-index correlation, and low-synchrotron-peaked FSRQ properties.

quotes.quote: We found that 15 of 29 FSRQs show a significant correlation at a confidence level of >99 , of which 14 FSRQs show positive correlations, and only one FSRQ (SDSS J001130.40 + 005751.7) shows a negative correlation. The negative correlation shows the source becomes steeper when source is brighter, i.e. RWB, which is shown in Fig. 1 with total 43 datapoints. The Spearman correlation analysis shows a significant negative correlation with a correlation coefficient of r_ s =-0.606 at confidence level of >99.99 . This source is included in the first Fermi Large Area Telescope AGNs catalogue with photon index of 2.51 0.15 and it is classified as a low-synchrotron-peaked FSRQs with _ peak <10^ 14 Hz (Abdo et al. 2010).

With the source redshift z=1.4934 , SDSS ugriz wavebands corresponds to the wavelength range of 1424 - 3582 in the source rest frame, which therefore is likely at the falling part of synchrotron SED. If this spectral break also exists in SDSS J001130.40 + 005751.7, we would expect to observe the rising part of accretion disk thermal emission when it dominates over the nonthermal emission. Indeed, Fig. 1 qualitatively shows that the optical spectral is rising ( _ <1.0 ) when the source is at low flux state, while it becomes falling ( _ >1.0 ) when the source is at high flux state implying a nonthermal low peak frequency synchrotron emission starts to be dominated.

sources.summary: The paper treats J0011.4+0057 as a Fermi-LAT blazar/flat-spectrum radio quasar, analyzes 2022 gamma-ray photons, derives a high-energy spectral slope, and compares it with catalog power-law parameters.

quotes.quote: To this aim, we used datat of the blazar, J0011.4+0057, observed with Fermi LAT instrument FERMILAT . This source appears in the paper accompanying the Fermi LAT fourth data release FSC4 , where it is classified as a flat-spectrum radio quasar. We have downloaded from the archive https://fermi.gsfc.nasa.gov/cgi-bin/ssc/LAT/LATDataQuery.cgi data collected in the period starting on 13/3/2022 at 3:5:18 and ending on 9/9/2022 at 00:14:04. They consist of 85,185 photons with energies E in the range 100 Mev E 97.8 Gev .

The smallest _ red ^2 , of the order of 1, is found for the solution s_ 3410 using 538 photons in the range 4.15 - 26.3 GeV with parameters =2.8871 0.0081 , E_m=4,163.4 6.4 MeV and E_M=28.68 0.23 GeV.

For J0011.4+0057 the parameters are K=(4.05 0.40) 10^ 13 ph cm ^ 2 Mev ^ 1 s ^ 1 , E_0=1,208 Mev and =2.319 0.085 . A direct comparison of our with is not easy because we selected photons with energy E>1,484.21 Mev >E_0 , so that the two fits were done over two different portion of the spectrum.

gmw_00000510.overview: The submillimeter galaxy 850.36 is a low-redshift source located at a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.224 [arXiv:1703.10168]. Its optical morphology reveals an extended, asymmetrical disk with double nuclei consistent with a major merger, while its low X-ray luminosity indicates a star-formation origin [arXiv:1703.10168].

sources.summary: The paper describes 850.36 as an unambiguous low-redshift DSFG counterpart with z=0.224, merger-like double nuclei, an extended asymmetric disk, and X-ray luminosity consistent with star formation.

quotes.quote: 850.36 has a measured spectroscopic redshift of z=0.224 from the zCOSMOS survey lilly07a ; its identification as the origin of the submillimeter emission is unambiguous. The optical morphology is clearly consistent with a major merger with double nuclei and an extended, asymmetrical disk. While we did target this source with MOSFIRE on the same mask as other higher priority DSFGs, the wavelength coverage failed to overlap with expected prominent features like Pa and Pa .

Only four of the 31 spectroscopically-confirmed sources are X-ray detected: 450.58, 850.36, 850.89 and m450.133. Their integrated rest-frame 0.5-8 keV luminosities are 8.0 10 ^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , 3.1 10 ^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , 2.0 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 and 1.1 10 ^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. The low luminosity for 850.36 (at z=0.224 ) is consistent with a star-formation origin, while the other sources are consistent with luminous AGN.

gmw_00000511.overview: The galaxy 450.24 is a spectroscopic source with a revised redshift of z=0.1657 [arXiv:1703.10168]. Near-infrared observations of the galaxy detect Pa-beta, Pa-gamma, and H2 features, with a high Paschen decrement that suggests dust obscuration [arXiv:1703.10168].

sources.summary: The paper reports a prior and revised spectroscopic redshift for 450.24, detects near-infrared Pa and H2 features, and finds a high Paschen decrement suggesting significant dust obscuration.

quotes.quote: Source 450.24 also has a prior spectroscopic confirmation at z=0.1661 lilly07a , and here we obtained a near-infrared spectrum which is more consistent with a redshift of z=0.1657 based on detection of Pa , Pa and H _2 features. We measure a Paschen decrement between Pa and Pa of 8.7 2.5, with the significant uncertainty caused by flux calibration between bands, and the Pa detection near the edge of H band close to telluric features. While highly uncertain, this ratio is still significantly higher than the expected theoretical intensity ratio of 2.16 for case B recombination temperature of 10 ^4 K, suggesting significant dust obscuration.

gmw_00000512.overview: The submillimeter source 450.28 is part of a complex blend of sources that was initially identified at a redshift of 2.472 [arXiv:1703.10168]. However, high-resolution ALMA observations reveal that this source is misidentified, with three distinct sources instead dominating the emission [arXiv:1703.10168].

sources.summary: The paper describes 450.28 as part of a blended submillimeter complex whose targeted counterpart has z=2.472, but later ALMA data reveal a misidentification within an associated protocluster structure.

quotes.quote: Source 450.28 (Figure fig:misids , middle left) is a member of a complex blend of submm sources; the prior measurements of this area at 850 \ and 1.2 mm only reveal a single source see bertoldi07a,casey13a , while 450 \ imaging breaks the emission into two distinct peaks. Our MOSFIRE follow-up targeted the most likely counterpart for the western source, 450.28, and confirms a redshift for that source of 2.472 a member of the protocluster discussed in casey15a . However, more recent ALMA follow-up reveals this source is mis-identified, and instead, three sources dominate the ALMA emission to the north and east.

While formally resulting in a mis-identification, this complex is physically associated with the structure identified at z 2.47 , confirmed through multiple transitions of CO at z=2.494 . This region is discussed at length in wang16a as a potential virialized protocluster core at z=2.51 , where they present an extended X-ray detection and eleven possible CO(1-0) detections in the broader redshift range of the protocluster. Further CO(1-0) observations of this region is being analyzed to determine redshifts and further map this complex region (Champagne , in preparation).

gmw_00000513.overview: The source 450.55 is likely a blend of an exceedingly faint background galaxy and a foreground galaxy at a redshift of z=0.36 [arXiv:1703.10168]. Its submillimeter emission is more consistent with the background source, and a secure redshift has not been obtained for this dusty star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1703.10168].

sources.summary: The paper describes 450.55 as a likely blend of a faint background galaxy and z=0.36 foreground galaxy, with the submillimeter emission favoring the background source and no secure DSFG redshift.

quotes.quote: Source 450.55 (Figure fig:misids , middle right) is likely a blend of a background galaxy which is exceedingly faint, even at near-infrared wavelengths, and a foreground galaxy, confirmed at z=0.36 . The submillimeter source is more consistent with the background source to the west of the bright foreground source, yet the foreground source is 24 bright, and so was earlier identified as the likely submillimeter counterpart. Deblending of this source was unfortunately not attempted before the spectroscopic campaign was underway, so we have not obtained a secure redshift for this DSFG.

gmw_00000514.overview: The submillimeter source 850.77 is located north of a bright foreground spiral galaxy [arXiv:1703.10168]. Because of the spiral galaxy's dominant 24-micron emission, 850.77 was previously misidentified, and its true redshift remains unknown [arXiv:1703.10168].

sources.summary: The paper describes 850.77 as an ambiguous submillimeter source offset from a bright foreground spiral, whose 24 micron emission caused a mistaken counterpart assignment and whose true redshift remains unidentified.

quotes.quote: The last case of a mis-identification is 850.77. This submillimeter source is offset to the north of a very bright foreground spiral galaxy at z=0.1088 . The spiral is clearly the dominant source of 24 \ emission in the region, and so was mistaken as a potential counterpart to the 850 \ source. Deep imaging does not reveal any obvious optical/near-infrared counterpart, and so this source remains ambiguous and without a redshift identification; it is also not detected at 450 .

gmw_00000515.name: GraL J181730853+272940139
gmw_00000515.overview: GraL J181730853+272940139 is a quadruply lensed broad-absorption line quasar at a redshift of approximately 3.07 [arXiv:1810.00479, arXiv:2012.10051]. The system is gravitationally lensed by a galaxy modeled as a highly elongated, edge-on disk [arXiv:1810.00479, arXiv:1810.04480].

sources.summary: The paper presents GraL J181730853+272940139 as a z=3.07 quadruply lensed quasar and models its lensing galaxy, finding a highly elongated, edge-on disk dominating the lens quadrupole.

quotes.quote: One of these newly discovered systems is GraL J181730853+272940139, hereafter J1817+2729. This system was reported in delchambre18 , and was identified as a quadruply lensed quasar candidate via a machine learning search inside Gaia Data Release 2, where it consists of three sources with maximum separation 1.8 . Lemon et al. in prep (private communications) confirmed it spectroscopically as a lensed quasar with source redshift z=3.07 .

The lensing galaxy has a large ellipticity of 0.67 0.02 , a major axis oriented at 54 1 deg E of N, and an effective radius along the major axis of 0.71 0.03 . Our mass modeling produces a statistically good fit with reduced ^2=1.6 for 2 degrees of freedom (dof). However, the model is unusual, as it requires a very large shear 0.28 , orthogonal to the major axis of the lensing galaxy, and high ellipticity e 0.83 . This suggests that the lens is more complex, e.g. with a dominant edge-on disk. Indeed, remodeling the lensing galaxy light with a concentric exponential disk and a De Vaucouleurs bulge reveals an edge-on disk with e 0.92 , and effective radius 0.3 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J1817+2729 as a spectroscopically confirmed quad at z=3.07, with a highly flattened mass model, elongated lens light, microlensing-demagnified image B, and edge-on disk lens model.

quotes.quote: Resolved spectra verify images A, C, and D are at a redshift of z=3.07 .

Our SIE+shear model predicts a highly flattened mass distribution with q=0.21 orientated 54 degrees East of North, and a strong shear of 0.27, -34 degrees East of North. The light from the galaxy is also highly elongated, q=0.1 , and in a remarkably similar direction to the mass (56 degrees East of North). The main contribution to ^2 is the flux ratio, with image B being 4 times fainter than predicted by our SIE+shear model. This is a saddle point and such images can be strongly demagnified by microlensing schechter2002 .

We note that such a strong shear, orthogonal to the mass distribution likely suggests a limitation in the choice of lensing model. rusulemon2018 discuss a more intricate mass model, based on deeper Subaru imaging, and suggest that the lensing galaxy is an edge-on disk. The lensing nature has been independently spectroscopically confirmed by Delchambre et al. (private communication).

sources.summary: The paper describes J1817+2729 as a z=3.07 quadruply imaged source detected by VLASS and C-band work but not by the authors' shallower X-band observations.

quotes.quote: J1817+2729, a quadruply imaged source at z=3.07 Lemon19 , was discovered by 1817 using a blind catalog search in Gaia DR2. Despite a strong detection in VLASS, our X-band observations report no significant emission at 10 GHz, and a manual re-reduction of the data showed the same. Fortunately, the target was also observed by dobie23 in C band (6 GHz), and was confirmed as a lensed radio source therein. J1817+2729 shows no variability between epochs 1 (May 2019) and 2 (Sept 2021) of VLASS, so we assume no significant variability for the source.

From VLASS epoch 1 and the summed flux densities of all images in the C-band by dobie23 , we estimate a spectral index between 3 GHz and 6 GHz of _ 3 GHz ^ 6 GHz = -1.6 0.2 , substantially steeper than the relatively flat spectrum estimated from NVSS and VLASS. This may be the result of genuine spectral curvature for instance the spectral index between 1.4 GHz and 3 GHz might be capturing the spectral turnover of a peaked spectrum radio source e.g., ODea2021 .

sources.summary: The paper confirms GraL J181730.8+272940 as a lensed BAL quasar at z = 3.074 and identifies a nearby bright point source as a Galactic star rather than part of the lens.

quotes.quote: GraL J181730.8+272940 --- This source was presented in as a candidate lensed quasar with three detected lensed images, an ERT probability of P_ ERT = 0.91 , and a maximum separation of 1 8. Our Keck spectrum at PA=135 \ confirmed that the fainter SE component and the brighter NW component of the compact configuration are a lensed quasar at z = 3.074 with strong \ BAL features and weak Ly . The bright point source 2 \ NW of the lens was also observed in that slit configuration and shown to be a Galactic mid-type star with absorption from Ca HK, H , and the calcium triplet. Owing to the configuration suggestive of a hilt and a blade, we have named this source ``Hercules' Sword''. The faint radio source NVSS J181731+272951 ( S_ 1.4 GHz = 3.2  mJy) is approximately 10 \ NE of the lens, but is unlikely associated.

gmw_00000516.name: NGC 1358
gmw_00000516.overview: NGC 1358 is an early-type barred spiral Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1001.2458, arXiv:1103.3796] hosting an active galactic nucleus with variable X-ray obscuration [arXiv:2207.06734]. Its complex nuclear structure features irregular dust [arXiv:1503.01836], a rotating emission-line disk [arXiv:1101.1337], and a high-excitation outflow [arXiv:1101.1337, arXiv:2107.07635].

sources.summary: The paper uses long-slit spectroscopy to model NGC 1358 as a Seyfert 2 barred Sa galaxy with an active nucleus, jet-like outflow, postshock cloud, and rotating nuclear emission-line disk.

quotes.quote: The aim of this paper is to demonstrate, with application to a long slit spectrum of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1358, how line intensities and velocities, together with modelling and knowledge of the point spread function, may be used to resolve the differing structures.

A preliminary solution for the nuclear structure of NGC 1358 is obtained, involving a nuclear point source and an emerging outflow of high excitation, ending with shock and postshock cloud as revealed by the velocities, as well as a nuclear emission line disk rotating in the potential of a stellar bulge and expressing a radial excitation gradient.

NGC 1358 (Fig. fig:ngc1358 ) is a barred Sa galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus (AGN) at a heliocentric velocity of 4028 \ (Theureau et al. 1998), which is included in the distance-limited sample of Ulvestad Wilson (1989) as a Seyfert 2. It attracted our attention because of its remarkable circumnuclear kinematics which also allows demonstrating the utility of the method introduced here. Assuming a Hubble constant of 71 \ (Spergel et al. 2003), the distance of NGC 1358 amounts to 57 Mpc. Then, 1 \ corresponds to 275 pc. The outer optical disk of NGC 1358, as seen in Fig. fig:ngc1358 , including the narrow spiral arms, has a radius of 1 , or 16.5 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper corrects detector artifacts in NGC 1358, finds its outer position angle differs from LEDA, and argues its deprojected measurements are affected by inclination and line-of-nodes uncertainties.

quotes.quote: In an effort to avoid spurious effects caused by the electronic cross-talk mentioned above, the J and Ks images of NGC 1358 and NGC 7743 (in this study, the only galaxies whose images are affected by this problem) underwent further processing.

All estimates agree within 10^ except for NGC 266, NGC 1358 and NGC 7080. For NGC 1358, the position angle from LEDA is 49 ^ , while the estimates both from our images and from the DSS2 images point to 15 ^ .

For NGC 1358, this parameter, as quoted in the literature, ranges from 37 ^ to 55 ^ (see results in NED and LEDA). Our choice is closer to the latter value and the ellipticity profile of the deprojected image argues favourably to it, as the ellipticity is close to zero in the outer parts (Fig. res1358 ). To be reassured of that, we created a deprojected image of NGC 1358 assuming that i=40^ and found that the disc remains significantly eccentric, again arguing that the true inclination angle of this galaxy is closer to 50 ^ than to 40 ^ .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1358 as a barred Seyfert S0/a galaxy whose spectroscopy reaches three scalelengths, with an inner gaseous spiral and an outer bar corotation radius of 5.1 kpc.

quotes.quote: The spectroscopic data extend to 2 scalelengths for most of the galaxies, except for NGC 1358, where 3 scalelengths are reached. Only for two of the four galaxies (NGC 1358 and NGC 1365) do our data extend beyond corotation.

NGC 1358: This galaxy is a Seyfert, S0/a galaxy with a bar. For this galaxy, long-slit spectroscopy along the bar was presented in P erez, S anchez-Bl azquez Zurita (2009). The galaxy has an inner gaseous spiral structure (with size < 1 kpc; Dumas, Emsellem Ferruit 2007 Dum07 ). The outer bar has a corotation radius of 5.1 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 1358 as an early-type barred galaxy, but notes its pattern speed is less secure because only one bar-side spectrum was available.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present an analysis of the bar pattern speed in four barred galaxies using the TW method. Three galaxies, NGC 271, NGC 1358 and ESO 281-31 are early type barred galaxies.

NGC 1358 Longslit spectra are available for the nucleus and on one side of the bar. Unfortunately, spectra on the opposite end of the bar could not be obtained. The pattern speed in this galaxy was derived using the individual exposures only. The low number of data points did not warrant an analysis on the combined spectra.

sources.summary: The paper monitors NGC 1358 as a changing-look Seyfert 2 AGN, confirming variable X-ray obscuration, a recent Compton-thin state, and torus-parameter constraints.

quotes.quote: We present the multi-epoch monitoring with \ and \ of NGC 1358, a nearby Seyfert 2 galaxy whose properties made it a promising candidate X-ray changing-look active galactic nucleus (AGN), i.e., a source whose column density could transition from its 2017 Compton-thick (CT--, having line-of-sight Hydrogen column density N _ H,los > 10 ^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) state to a Compton-thin (N _ H,los < 10 ^ 24 cm ^ 2 ) one. The multi-epoch X-ray monitoring confirmed the presence of significant N _ H,los variability over time-scales from weeks to years, and allowed us to confirm the ``changing-look'' nature of NGC 1358, which has most recently been observed in a Compton-thin status. Multi-epoch monitoring with \ and \ is demonstrated to be highly effective in simultaneously constraining three otherwise highly degenerate parameters: the torus average column density and covering factor, and the inclination angle between the torus axis and the observer. We find a tentative anti-correlation between column density and luminosity, which can be understood in the framework of Chaotic Cold Accretion clouds driving recursive AGN feedback. The monitoring campaign of NGC 1358 has proven the efficiency of our newly developed method to select candidate -variable, heavily obscured AGN, which we plan to soon extend to a larger sample to better characterize the properties of the obscuring material surrounding accreting supermassive black holes, as well as constrain AGN feeding models.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1358 as a Seyfert 2 from optical line ratios, reports [OIII] luminosity and SMBH mass, and infers an Eddington ratio.

quotes.quote: NGC 1358 : The log( [ OIII ] /H ) and log( [ NII ] /H ) line ratios, 1.0542 and 0.3032 respectively Ho1997a , place it in the Seyfert 2 region of the BPT diagram. Shu2007 report an [ OIII ] luminosity, log( L [ OIII ] ) = 40.86 erg s ^ 1 . The SMBH mass is determined to be 6.56 2.50 10 ^7 M_ Wu2001 . This implies an Eddington ratio of 0.029.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1358 as a Seyfert 2 barred Sa galaxy with complex nuclear structure, including a point source, high-excitation outflow, rotating emission-line disk, and bulge.

quotes.quote: The aim of this paper is to demonstrate, with application to a long slit spectrum of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 1358, how line intensities and velocities, together with modelling and knowledge of the point spread function, may be used to resolve the differing structures.

A preliminary solution for the nuclear structure of NGC 1358 is obtained, involving a nuclear point source and an emerging outflow of high excitation with a post shock cloud, as well as a nuclear emission line disk rotating in the potential of a stellar bulge and expressing a radial exitation gradient. The method results in a likely scenario for the nuclear structure of NGC 1358.
NGC 1358 is a barred Sa galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus at a heliocentric velocity of 4100 , included in the sample of Ulvestad Wilson (1989) as a Seyfert 2.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1358 as a special abundance-calibration case, with R23 near method limits and large central KD03 differences in its Seyfert region.

quotes.quote: An special case is that of NGC 1358: the R _ 23 values calculated for this galaxy are in the limit of applicability of the methods based on this parameter.
P05 values are systematically 0.3-0.5 dex lower than those of R _ 23 (M91) with the exception of NGC 1358.
For KD03 , the largest differences are found in the central part of the Seyfert galaxy NGC 1358, being much smaller beyond 0.08 R/R _ 25 , the activity zone in Fig.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1358 as a barred Seyfert with irregular nuclear dust, mostly old-to-intermediate stellar populations, and a sparse near-infrared emission-line spectrum.

quotes.quote: NGC 1358 (S2) The nucleus of this barred galaxy shows irregular dusty structures Malkan98 . Storchi-Bergmann98 find that 64 of the nuclear optical emission of NGC 1358 can be accounted for by a 10 Gyr template, with almost all of the remainder from a 1 Gyr template. On the other hand, Perez11 estimate the age of the central stellar population to be in the region of 6 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 1358's central ionized gas, describing its strong-emission region, velocity field, and signs of nuclear outflow, rotation, density structure, or inflow.

quotes.quote: This Sy 2 galaxy shows emission that peaks at the nucleus, with the SER, observed in [ N ii ] , showing an S-shape structure, being most extended approximately perpendicularly to the PMA. The kinematics show a distorted rotation pattern with the KMA approximately along the S-shape structure, almost perpendicular to the PMA. Two emission blobs at 2 arcsec ( 500 pc) from the nucleus at the end of the S are associated with an increase in W _ 80 and can be attributed to outflows from the nucleus (as previously discussed in SchnorrMuller2017a ).

gmw_00000517.name: PKS 1441+25
gmw_00000517.overview: PKS 1441+25 is a distant flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of approximately 0.94 [arXiv:1512.04435, arXiv:2001.06016]. It is classified as a blazar [arXiv:1606.04585] and has been detected at very high energies during periods of flaring activity [arXiv:1510.00635, arXiv:1706.01611].

sources.summary: PKS 1441+25 is presented as a z=0.939 FSRQ monitored by MAGIC, detected at very high energies with high significance, and used for extragalactic-background-light constraints.

quotes.quote: The two sources detected and analyzed, the blazar QSO B0218+357 and the FSRQ PKS 1441+25 are located at redshift z=0.944 and z=0.939 respectively.

PKS 1441+25 The MAGIC telescopes monitored PKS 1441+25 in April and May 2015 for a total of 30 h. The observations were performed in the standard wobble mode with a 0.4^ offset and four symmetric positions with respect to the camera center. The data were collected in the zenith angle range of 3^ <zd<38^ . The analysis of the data was performed using the standard MAGIC analysis framework MARS and, as in the case of QSO B0218+357, with Monte Carlo simulations matching the night-sky background levels. PKS 1441+25 was detected with a significance of 25.5 , see Fig. fig:th2 , panel b.

QSO B0218+357 ( z=0.944 ) and PKS 1441+25 ( z=0.939 ) were detected for the first time in the VHE range by MAGIC, extending the maximum redshift for VHE ray observations up to a distance z 1 , near the cosmic ray horizon.

sources.summary: The paper reports VERITAS follow-up of PKS 1441+25, a high-redshift FSRQ, detecting a soft-spectrum VHE excess after MAGIC and finding no later significant excess.

quotes.quote: PKS 1441+25 is an FSRQ located at a redshift of z = 0.939 . Immediately after the MAGIC discovery (ATel 7402) of VHE ray emission from this object in April 2015, VERITAS initiated a ToO observation campaign. A total of 15 h of good-quality observations was acquired during a 1-week period, resulting in the detection of a very soft spectrum excess of 400 events ( 8 ; see Figure Blazar_Maps ). The observed flux was steady at 5 Crab above 80 GeV. A further 4 h of data were taken in May 2015, after the MeV-GeV ( Fermi LAT) flare that initiated the MAGIC observations had subsided.

No significant excess was observed from the source, unlike the VERITAS detection ( 6 in 6 h over 10 nights) of steady emission ( 3 Crab) from the FSRQ PKS 1222+216 approximately two weeks after a similar MeV-GeV flare had subsided in February 2014.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1441+25 as a distant flat-spectrum radio quasar detected by MAGIC and VERITAS in VHE gamma rays, constraining the EBL.

quotes.quote: PKS 1441+25 is an flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) located at a red-shift of z = 0.939 . First detected in VHEs by MAGIC at a statistical significance of 25 after a MeV-GeV flare , it was quickly confirmed by VERITAS (at a statistical significance of 8 ). The source appeared to be steady at that time, at 5 of the Crab flux above 80 GeV with a very soft spectrum. In later observations in May 2015, after the end of the MeV-GeV flare, PKS 1441+25 could not be detected at the same level in VHEs.
Being one of the two most distant VHE blazar ever, the source is of particular interest. Detected up to 250 GeV , it implies important constraints on the extragalactic background light (EBL) .

sources.summary: The paper reports MAGIC detection of the z 0.940 flat-spectrum radio quasar PKS 1441+25, models its multiwavelength flaring jet, and uses its VHE spectrum to constrain EBL absorption.

quotes.quote: The flat-spectrum radio quasar PKS 1441+25 at a redshift of z=0.940 is detected between 40 and 250 GeV with a significance of 25.5 using the MAGIC telescopes. Together with the gravitationally lensed blazar QSO B0218+357 ( z = 0.944 ), PKS 1441+25 is the most distant very high energy (VHE) blazar detected to date. The observations were triggered by an outburst in 2015 April seen at GeV energies with the Large Area Telescope on board Fermi .

Multi-wavelength observations suggest a subdivision of the high state into two distinct flux states. In the band covered by MAGIC, the variability time scale is estimated to be 6.4 1.9 days. Modeling the broadband spectral energy distribution with an external Compton model, the location of the emitting region is understood as originating in the jet outside the broad line region (BLR) during the period of high activity, while being partially within the BLR during the period of low (typical) activity.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 2015 gamma-ray flares of PKS 1441+25, finding rapid variability, spectral hardening, and leptonic SED models requiring changed Lorentz factor or magnetic field.

quotes.quote: We present the ray observations of the flat-spectrum radio quasar PKS 1441+25 (z=0.939), using the Fermi large Area Telescope data accumulated during January - December 2015. A ray flare was observed in January 24, when the flux increased up to (2.22 0.38) 10^ 6 photon cm^ 2 s^ 1 with the flux-doubling time scale being as short as 1.44 days. The spectral analysis shows that from April 13 to April 28, 2015 the MeV-to-GeV photon index has hardened and changes in the range of =(1.73-1.79) for most of the time.
