It has a red-shift of z = 0.1396 , and the mass of its central supermassive black hole (SMBH) has been estimated to be 4.8 10 ^ 8 M _ 2005ApJ...631..762W . Based on its high X-ray-to-radio flux ratio, it was classified as an HBL 1995A&AS..109..267G .

In this paper, we have considered the LC of three sectors (42--44) of the TESS observations of the blazar 1ES 0229+200. The object shows almost no optical variations during that period except for a flare in the first segment of Sector 42. We find that the flare is highly symmetric, exhibiting identical e-folding timescales within errors.

Very high Doppler factors with a large range of 40--100 for 1ES 0229+200 have been estimated via MW SED modeling 2009MNRAS.399L..59T,2011A&A...534A.130K,2014ApJ...782...13A . Taking z=0.1396 and considering an average e-folding timescale ( 0.036 0.002 \ d, or 0.88 0.05 \ h) as the variability timescale, for this range of , we find the upper limit to the size of the emission region to be within (3.3 0.2 - 8.3 0.5) 10^ 15 \ cm, assuming that the uncertainty is propagated via variability timescale only.

sources.summary: The paper selects 1ES 0229+200 as a hard-spectrum BL Lac target for extended gamma-ray emission searches, analyzing 80 hours of H.E.S.S. data and reporting upper limits.

quotes.quote: Both extended pair halo (PH) and magnetically broadened cascade (MBC) emission from regions surrounding the blazars 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 0229+200 and PKS 2155-304 were searched for, using VHE ray data taken with the High Energy Stereoscopic System (H.E.S.S.), and high energy (HE; 100 MeV < E < 100 GeV) ray data with the Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT). By comparing the angular distributions of the reconstructed ray events to the angular profiles calculated from detailed theoretical models, the presence of PH and MBC was investigated. Upper limits on the extended emission around 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 0229+200 and PKS 2155-304 are found to be at a level of few percent of the Crab nebula flux above 1 TeV, depending on the assumed photon index of the cascade emission.

1ES 0229+200 This source was first observed by H.E.S.S. in late 2004 and detected with a significance of 6.6 2007A&A...475L...9A . This high-frequency peaked BL Lac is hosted in a elliptical galaxy and is located at a redshift of z = 0.140 2005ApJ...631..762W . A total of 80 hours of data taken between 2004 and 2009 were used for this analysis. 1ES 0229+200 is a prime source for such studies due to its hard intrinsic spectrum reaching beyond 10 TeV 2007A&A...475L...9A, 2012ApJ...747L..14V, 2010MNRAS.406L..70T, 2011ApJ...727L...4D .

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0229+200 as an ultra-high-frequency-peaked BL Lac with hard TeV emission, difficult SSC fits, observed soft-X-ray variability, and viable hadronic SED solutions.

quotes.quote: Out of the 44 HBLs detected so far in the TeV range, five sources --- 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 0347-121, RGB J0710+591, 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 1218+304 --- are characterized by particularly hard spectra with the high-energy peak above 1 TeV, which is consistent with their very hard and weak signal in the Fermi LAT band. Another common characteristic is the lack of observable variability on short time-scales of the very-high-energy flux with the exception of 1ES 1218+304, which showed VHE flaring activity on the scale of days, 1218_Veritas . Although the BL Lac object H 1426+428 belongs to this sub-class as well, since it was detected at VHE only during flaring activity without extensive multi-wavelength coverage 1426_hegra, Arache, 1426_whipple2, 1426_whipple, Leonardo09, wystan we decided to exclude it from our sample. The modelling of these ultra-high-frequency peaked BL Lacs UHBLs, also known as extreme BL Lacs, EHBLs, see e.g. for a more recent review Costamante01, Gunes13 with one-zone SSC scenarios, which usually yield good results for HBLs, is proving difficult in most cases, as can be seen from Table tab:uhbl , where some recent attempts at SSC modeling of the sources are summarized. A good representation of the complete SEDs requires either extremely high values of the minimum Lorentz factor of the electron distribution (1ES 1101-232, 1ES 0347-121), or an elevated Doppler factor (1ES 1218+304), or both (1ES 0229+200, RGB J0710+591).

For the five sources, we find two distinct sets of solutions, corresponding to the proton-synchrotron scenario and a lepto-hadronic scenario, dominated by emission from secondary particles from p- interactions. In the latter case, the SSC contribution is at a level comparable to the emission from hadronic processes, and justifies the '' lepto-hadronic '' classification.

Beside 1ES 1218+304, for 1ES 0229+200 0229_Veritas and RGB J0710+591 See http://www.swift.psu.edu/monitoring/source.php?source=RGBJ0710+591 a _ var of the order of ten days has been observed in soft X-rays.

sources.summary: 1ES 0229+200 is treated as an EHBL with hard TeV emission and weak variability; the UHECR-induced cascade model fits it but predicts the strongest anisotropy constraints of the trio.

quotes.quote: Very-high-energy (VHE; 100 GeV) ray observations have resulted in the detections of several extreme high-frequency-peaked BL Lac objects (EHBLs), most notably 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 1101-232, and 1ES 0347-121, which show very hard VHE spectra ( 3; dN / dE E^ ) extending to tens of TeV e.g., Aharonian2007AA470p475,Aharonian2007AA473L25,Aharonian2007AA475L9 . Moreover, these sources show weak variability, unlike most blazars. VHE rays can be attenuated by pair-creation interactions with EBL photons while propagating through intergalactic space Nikishov1962JETP14p393,Gould1966PRL16p252,Stecker1992ApJ390L49 . A possible scenario to overcome the severe absorption that would soften the TeV spectra of these sources is the UHECR-induced cascade model e.g., Essey2010APh33p81,Essey2011ApJ731p51,Murase2012ApJ749p63,Razzaque2012ApJ745p196 .

We have generally derived the constraints on the CR luminosity of a powerful source from the observed isotropy at 10^ 19 eV. In this section, we specifically focus on three known EHBLs as powerful UHECR emitters, that is, 1ES 0229+200 Aharonian2007AA475L9 , 1ES 0347-121 Aharonian2007AA473L25 , and 1ES 1101-232 Aharonian2007AA470p475 , all of which have been detected by the High Energy Spectroscopic System (H.E.S.S.). The spectra of these objects in the VHE range can be well reproduced by the UHECR-induced cascade model e.g., Essey2011ApJ731p51,Murase2012ApJ749p63 . The isotropic-equivalent CR luminosities to reproduce the observed flux are shown in Table tab:ehbls with their redshift and positions.
Based on the discussion in this section, 1ES 0229+200 produces the strongest anisotropy, and therefore provides the strongest constraints among the three.

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0229+200 as a TeV blazar for ALP constraints, using H.E.S.S. data, redshift-dependent optical depths, Fermi-LAT variability, and a cluster magnetic-field environment.

quotes.quote: The source 1ES 0229+200 was first discovered by H.E.S.S. in 2006. It has been observed by both VERITAS and H.E.S.S with observation times of 46 and 41.8 hours, respectively. The source has a redshift of z = 0.1396 , leading us to determine that observations by H.E.S.S extend to an optical depth of 4.6 while the observations by VERITAS extend to 3.4 . The HAWC observation at 7 TeV is at an optical depth of 3.15. Since the observations by both H.E.S.S. and VERITAS extend to energies beyond the HAWC observation and have similar exposure times, there is no obvious choice of which spectrum we should use.

The source has also been detected by the Fermi-LAT reaching a significance of 12.5 in the 4FGL catalog . Fermi-LAT observations also indicate that the variability index of this source is only 6.8 , significantly less than the critical value of 18.5 used by the 4FGL catalog to assign a source as ``variable".

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 0229+200 as an extreme HSP BL Lac blazar; IXPE detected strong X-ray polarization, far above optical polarization, making it highly chromatic.

quotes.quote: One of the most extreme blazars, at least from the standpoint of its panchromatic spectral energy distribution (SED) observed to date is ( RA=02h32m48.6s , Dec=+20 ^o 17 ^ 17.4 ^ , z=0.14 ). is a BL Lacertae object which belongs to the rare class of extreme high synchrotron peaked (HSP) objects Costamante2002, Biteau2020 with a synchrotron peak frequency of _ syn 10^ 19 Hz or 40 Ajello2020 . Due to its extreme spectrum, 1ES 0229+200 has been used to study the extragalactic background light Aharonian2007 , extragalactic magnetic fields Tavecchio2010,Acciari2023 , and Lorentz invariance violations Tavecchio2016 .

The IXPE observations of have detected X-ray polarization of _ X 17.9 along _ X 25^ . The polarization degree we measure for this blazar is significantly higher than that measured during the first IXPE observation of Mrk 501 10 Liodakis2022 and similar to that measured during the first IXPE observation of Mrk 421 15 ; DiGesu2022 . As for these two HSP blazars, the X-ray polarization degree of is significantly higher than the optical values of 2 . In this case, the ratio of X-ray to optical is >7 making it the most strongly chromatic source in polarization observed thus far.

sources.summary: The paper uses 1ES 0229+200 as a TeV blazar test case for intergalactic magnetic-field limits, emphasizing its redshift, observed TeV spectrum, monitoring history, and Fermi-LAT analysis.

quotes.quote: These limits, which depend on the assumed opening angle _ j of the TeV photon source, are summarized in Table 1 for the TeV blazar 1ES 0229+200 at redshift z = 0.1396 . Under the assumption of persistent TeV emission over long time scales, these studies find that B_ IGMF 10^ 15 G for magnetic coherence (or correlation) lengths _ coh 1 Mpc when _ j 0.1 .

The TeV blazar 1ES 0229+200, which provides some of the strongest constraints on the lower limit to the IGMF field strength, was observed with HESS aha07 in 2005 and 2006 and with VERITAS per10 in October 2009 -- January 2010. No evidence for variability of the TeV flux has been reported, so the observations give an average TeV flux from this source on timescales of 3 yr, though with poor sampling. The HESS and preliminary VERITAS data per10 are shown in Fig. \ 2 by the blue open circles and red squares, respectively.
Here we reanalyze the Fermi LAT data for 1ES 0229+200 collected from 2008 August 4 to 2010 September 5 in survey mode.

sources.summary: The paper reports Fermi/LAT GeV observations of the blazar 1ES 0229+200, using its spectrum with HESS data to constrain EBL density and extragalactic magnetic-field strength.

quotes.quote: We report the observation in the GeV band of the blazar 1ES 0229+200, which over recent years has become one of the primary sources used to put constraints on the Extragalactic Background Light (EBL) and Extragalactic Magnetic Field (EGMF).

In this Letter we report the observations of the blazar 1ES 0229+200 in the 1-300 GeV energy range using LAT's data with three years of exposure. The detection of the source below 100 GeV provides the information necessary for the correct analysis of the EGMF-dependent upper bound on the EBL density and of the EBL-dependent lower bound on the EGMF strength.

1ES 0229+200 is detected by LAT in the 1-300 GeV energy band with significance 7 . The test statistics (TS) value found in the likelihood analysis is TS=45 . Modelling the source spectrum as a powerlaw we find the slope of the spectrum =1.36 0.25 and normalization at 20 GeV (1.4 0.5) 10^ 15 (MeV cm ^2 sec) ^ 1 (at the 68 confidence level).

sources.summary: The paper finds 1ES 0229+200 can reject zero IGMF under standard assumptions, but that EBL uncertainty or VHE duty-cycle changes remove the lower-limit claim.

quotes.quote: Another avenue to make the HE-VHE observational data of 1ES 0229+200 compatible with H0 is to question whether or not the VHE flux of the source reported is representative of the average flux during the Fermi observations. The HESS measurements were accumulated in 2005 (6.8 hrs) - 2006 (35 hrs) and are not strictly contemporaneous with the Fermi HE spectrum. The significance of the detection in 2005 reported is 2.7 , while in 2006 it is 6.1 with average photon fluxes above 580 GeV of 6.8 10^ 13 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , and 10 10^ 13 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , respectively.

Further communication with the VERITAS Collaboration suggests that the flux level of 1ES 0229+200 has been steadily declining from 2009 - 2012 (private communication). To investigate the effect of a reduced duty cycle for 1ES 0229+200, the spectrum of this source was modified at the highest 5 energy points to half an order of magnitude of their reported values. It was found that the VHE - HE data set combined in this way does not rule out H0 at more than 95 confidence level.

sources.summary: The review emphasizes 1ES 0229+200 as a well-measured hard TeV blazar constraining the 2-10 micron EBL and the intergalactic magnetic field.

quotes.quote: A stronger confirmation came with the H.E.S.S. detection of 1ES 0229+200 ( z=0.140 ), whose spectrum was measured with good statistics up to 10-15 TeV. Taking advantage of the new EBL limits at 1-2 , the 1ES 0229+200 data constrains the EBL SED between 2 and 10 \ to be ^ with 1.1 0.25 , yielding an upper limit flux very close to the lower limits given by galaxy counts from Spitzer . This is possible because the Opt-NIR limit precludes the possibility of reducing the contrast in optical depth between 1 and 10 TeV by considering a higher EBL at 1 \ (see Sect. diagn ). The 1ES 0229+200 constraint is also confirmed by another analysis, based on the expected intensity of the flattening break in the VHE spectrum around 1-2 TeV produced by the EBL .

The recent detection of 1ES 0229+200 with Fermi-LAT allowed the exploration of the 3D parameter space given by IGMF strength vs EBL intensity (using template by Ref. franceschini08 ) vs intrinsic photon index _ int (varied within the range from 1.5--0 ). Taking into account the Fermi-LAT result in favour of a low EBL density, which means _ int 1.5 , the 1ES 0229+200 data at HE and VHE together constrain the IGMF to B 10^ 16 G.

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0229+200 as an extreme TeV blazar whose hard spectrum, variability hints, and synchrotron-pair signal constrain UHECR and magnetic-field scenarios.

quotes.quote: The HBL 1ES 0229+200 Aharonian07 is a relatively distant, hard spectrum source. The observed spectral index _ VHE 2.5 suggests a very hard intrinsic emission. Its high energy peak is at energy, E 10  TeV. Under the assumption that the intrinsic spectrum cannot be harder than 1.5 in leptonic models, it has been inferred that the EBL must be very low, close to the minimum inferred by resolved galaxy counts in order to be consistent with the observed spectrum of 1ES 0229+200 Aharonian07 .

fig:var_egmf shows the model prediction of the secondary synchrotron signal to the observed spectrum of 1ES 0229+200 for B in the range 6-316  nG. The goodness of the model fit to the spectrum of 1ES 0229+200 depends on the EBL assumed and the best fit is obtained with the lower limit model of Kneiske08 .

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0229+200 as an ultra-high-synchrotron-peaked blazar near a poor cluster, using its hard VHE spectrum and cluster magnetic field for ALP-sensitivity simulations.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200 This ultra high synchrotron peaked blazar at a redshift z = 0.1396 has been observed with H.E.S.S. for 41.8 hours and with VERITAS for 54.3 hours . The observations revealed a hard observed ray spectrum with _ obs = 2.50 0.19_ stat 0.10_ sys . No sign for variability on any time scale is present in H.E.S.S. data whereas evidence is found for flux variations on a yearly time scale in the VERITAS observations. The H.E.S.S. spectrum extends beyond 11 TeV or an optical depth of 4.4 .
We find that the blazar is within a distance of 0.72 Mpc < r_ 200 from the cluster WHL 22793 at a redshift difference of z = 8 10^ 4 . The cluster has 9 galaxies associated with it.

sources.summary: 1ES 0229+200 is described as a hard-spectrum BL Lac with extreme synchrotron and inverse-Compton properties whose highest-energy data are well explained by UHECR interactions.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200 : This BL Lac object at redshift 0.140 Woo_05 shows an extremely hard intrinsic TeV spectrum with the synchrotron spectrum peaking at exceptionally high energies near hard X-ray regime Costamante_18 . It has one of the highest inverse Compton (IC) peak frequency and the narrowest electron distribution among the extreme blazars known Kaufmann_11 . The source was first discovered by HESS in 2004 Aharonian_07a . The X-ray-to-radio flux ratio classifies it as an HBL Giommi_95 .

In our pure-leptonic modeling, there is no significant change in the SSC spectrum on increasing E_ e,cut beyond the value considered due to KN suppression. We show, the necessity of an additional component is inevitable and the spectrum arising from UHECR interactions well explains the highest energy data points.

sources.summary: 1ES 0229+200 is identified as an IceCube-track counterpart candidate and an attractive monitoring target, but the model finds only two flaring states and a small predicted neutrino total.

quotes.quote: Some of these sources (TXS 0506+056, 1ES 0229+200 /3HSP J023248.5+20171 and PKS 1502+106/5BZQ J1504+1029) have also been identified as possible counterparts of IceCube high-energy tracks e.g. 2016NatPh..12..807K, IceCube:2018cha,2019ApJ...880..103G, 2020ApJ...893..162F, 2020MNRAS.497..865G .

While 1ES 0229+200 has a moderate X-ray flare duty cycle ( 13 per cent), we identify only two flaring states with our method. Accounting for both, we predict N _ _ + _ =0.05 0.01 in IceCube's livetime. However, the number drops significantly (by a factor of 10 ), if we remove the flaring state with t = 143  d as being non-physical.

An ideal object for X-ray monitoring is 1ES 0229+200 (extreme synchrotron source) which is in spatial coincidence with an astrophysical muon neutrino track event detected by IceCube 2020MNRAS.497..865G . The declination of the source ( 20.3  deg) makes it also suitable for neutrino detection since there are no constraints in terms of the effective area of IceCube for this declination.

sources.summary: The paper finds that only 1ES 0229+200 in the hard-TeV BL Lac sample can be modeled by one-zone pp emission, requiring an extremely compact radiation region and continuous proton injection.

quotes.quote: We find that in a sample of hard-TeV BL Lacs collected by , only the hard-TeV spectrum of 1ES 0229+200 could be explained by ray from _0 decay produced in the pp interactions, but at the cost of setting a small radius of the radiation region that comparable to the Schwarzschild radius of the central black hole.

If the maximum that can be set is 30 as indicated by observation 2009A&A...494..527H , only 1ES 0229+200 has a certain parameter space which can be used to interpret the hard-TeV spectrum with the one-zone pp model.

Even though, the allowable maximum radius of the blob under the constraints of Eq.  m1 is very small, comparable to the Schwarzschild radius of the SMBH. This compact blob might be a relativistically moving plasmoid generated in the magnetic reconnection of the jet inside 2017ApJ...841...61A and imply a fast minute-scale variability, which is observed in TeV band of radio galaxies e.g., 2014Sci...346.1080A and blazars e.g., 2007ApJ...669..862A . However, no evidence of fast variability in TeV band of 1ES 0229+200 is found, therefore the injection of relativistic protons in such a compact blob must be continuous.

sources.summary: The paper includes 1ES 0229+200 as an extreme HSP blazar whose hard TeV spectrum can be explained by pp decay, though EBL absorption keeps predicted emission below LHAASO sensitivity.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200 1ES 0229+200 is the prototype of extreme HSP blazars ( _ S,peak 10^ 17   Hz ; 2001A&A...371..512C, 2018MNRAS.480.2165A ). The most remarkable feature of 1ES 0229+200 is the hard TeV spectrum, which poses a challenge to the conventional one-zone model 2009MNRAS.399L..59T, 2011A&A...534A.130K, 2019ApJ...871...81X . Analytical calculations suggest that pp interactions may have the potential to explain the hard TeV spectrum of 1ES 0229+200 2022A&A...659A.184L .

In this work, we include 1ES 0229+200 in our sample, and use numerical model to reproduce its multi-wavelength SED, especially the hard-TeV spectrum 2014ApJ...782...13A . The fitting result is given in Figure  0229 . It can be seen that the hard-TeV spectrum is well explained by the ray generated from ^0 decay, although a very compact emitting region is introduced, just as indicated by Ref.  2022A&A...659A.184L .

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0229+200 as an extreme HBL and favors it for X-ray ALP-polarization studies, especially at higher keV energies.

quotes.quote: In particular, by closely following the analysis developed in  , we investigate the photon-ALP beam propagation for photons emitted at the jet base of several BL Lacs: OJ 287, BL Lacertae, Markarian 501 and 1ES 0229+200 and we compute -- for each of them -- the photon survival probability P_ and the corresponding final photon degree of linear polarization _L and polarization angle . By using the state-of-the-art knowledge concerning the crossed regions (blazar jet, host galaxy, extragalactic space, Milky Way) and physically consistent bounds on the photon-ALP system parameters, we conclude that ALPs induce sizable effects on the final photon polarization which is observable by present and planned missions  . In particular, OJ 287 and BL Lacertae appear as good observational targets both in the X-ray and in the HE band, 1ES 0229+200 in the X-ray range only, while Markarian 501 does not represent a good candidate for studies of ALP effects on photon polarization.

1ES 0229+200 is the prototype of the so called extreme HBLs (EHBLs,  ) and has been observed at redshift z=0.1396 . Similarly to Markarian 501 it shows the synchrotron peak at X-ray energies, and it exhibits the valley between the synchrotron and the VHE peak in the HE range at about a few MeV. Therefore, 1ES 0229+200 represents an excellent observational target for polarization studies in the X-ray band but not in the HE range.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES0229+200 as a BL Lac blazar measured by HESS, derives a hard TeV intrinsic spectrum, and argues its particles are accelerated in relativistic shocks.

quotes.quote: The BL Lac object 1ES0229+200 was predicted to be an observable TeV source with a TeV flux of 10^ 12 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 by Stecker, de Jager Salamon (1996). It was detected by Aharonian et al. (2007) at a flux level of 0.94 10^ 12 above 0.58 TeV. Aharonian et al. (2007) have measured the spectrum of the blazar 1ES0229+200 at energies between 0.5 TeV and 15 TeV.

Our analysis thus presents evidence for relativistic shock acceleration in 1ES0229+200 that results in a very hard intrinsic spectrum with no evidence of a peak in the SED up to energies 10 TeV. Unfortunately, there are no simultaneous observations at other wavelengths that can be used to model the flare that occurred in 1ES0229+200 and, in any case, TeV orphan flares have been observed in other BL Lac objects.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 0229+200 as a redshift 0.1396 BL Lac with hard multi-TeV emission extending to 15 TeV and supporting low EBL flux.

quotes.quote: Recently, the HESS collaboration reported detection of multi-TeV gamma-rays from 1ES 0229+200, a BL Lac object located at a redshift z=0.1396 . It is remarkable that the detected hard gamma-ray spectrum of this source with a photon index _ obs 2.5 extends up to 15 TeV. This, to a certain extent surprising result can be explained by the shape of the energy flux of EBL which between the NIR and MIR bands is expected to be proportional to ^ 1 . Yet, the absolute EBL flux, derived from a rather conservative assumption that the photon index of the intrinsic spectrum of TeV gamma-rays does not exceed 1.5 , appears again close to the EBL lower limit, this time at MIR ( 2-3 \ nW/m^2 sr at 10 \ m ), derived from the Spitzer galaxy counts . Thus, the gamma-ray observations of 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 0229+200 can be interpreted as an argument that the galaxies resolved by the Hubble and Spitzer telescopes provide the bulk of the EBL flux from optical to mid infrared wavelengths.

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0229+200 as a hard-spectrum TeV blazar whose VHE and X-ray emission can be explained by internal absorption with proton synchrotron primary radiation.

quotes.quote: We study the applicability of the idea of internal absorption of rays produced through synchrotron radiation of ultrarelativistic protons in highly magnetized blobs to 1ES 0229+200 and 3C 66A, the two TeV blazars which show unusually hard intrinsic ray spectra after being corrected for the intergalactic absorption. We show that for certain combinations of reasonable model parameters, even with quite modest energy requirements, the scenario allows a self-consistent explanation of the non-thermal emission of these objects in the keV, GeV, and TeV energy bands.

In particular, 1ES 0229+200 shows VHE ray emission without significant flux or spectral changes between two HESS measurements separated by one year aharonian07 . Moreover, Fermi LAT was not able to detect GeV emission from the direction of 1ES 0229+200. In the SED plot, the upper limit of the GeV flux appears below the TeV flux corrected for intergalactic absorption.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 0229+200 as an extreme TeV blazar used for IGMF limits; under standard assumptions its data reject zero field above 99%, prompting tests of how the nonzero-field requirement could fail.

quotes.quote: We reveiw recent work in the literature which utilizes this idea to derive constraints on the IGMF for three TeV-detected blazars-1ES 0229+200, 1ES 1218+304, and RGB J0710+591, and we also investigate four other hard-spectrum TeV blazars in the same framework.

The most comprehensive work yet on constraining the IGMF was done by (hereafter referred to as TVN11), in which three sources were found (RGB J0710+591, 1ES 1218+304, and 1ES 0229+200) whose combined quasi-simultaneous IACT and Fermi-LAT dFED are not compatible with the B_ IGMF = 0 hypothesis at greater than 95 confidence level. It was suggested that B_ IGMF 10^ 17 G is required to explain observations.

It is confirmed that the data for 1ES 0229+200 are incompatible with the B_ IGMF = 0 hypothesis at > 99 confidence level, with the standard assumptions of 1) - 4) (listed in the previous paragraph). Since only this single source was found to be in conflict with a non-zero IGMF, each of these standard assumptions were varied to within their uncertainty limits to research all the possible ways in which the non-zero IGMF requirement can fail.

sources.summary: The paper presents VERITAS monitoring of 1ES 0229+200, detecting it with a measured VHE spectrum, variable flux, and synchrotron self-Compton model constraints.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200 was observed by VERITAS for 54.3 h from 2009-2012 as part of a MWL monitoring campaign including Swift , RXTE and Fermi LAT . The blazar was solidly detected by VERITAS ( 12 ) and the VHE spectrum well measured ( = 2.59 0.12 ). A variable VHE flux was observed presenting challenges to efforts to exploit the VHE spectrum of 1ES 0229+200 to constrain the strength of the intergalactic magnetic field. In addition, a synchrotron self-Compton (SSC) model was fit to the SED. Rather than presenting a single, degenerate solution the entire range of SSC parameters allowed by the data were presented.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 0229+200 as a hard, slowly varying TeV blazar, fitting absorption-only and ALP models and finding electromagnetic-cascade effects strengthen when K_V is below unity.

quotes.quote: Blazar 1ES 0229+200 is a frequent source in many discussions of extragalactic ray propagation due to its hard spectrum, slow variability, and comparatively high total flux. The fits for the case of the absorption-only model, which are similar in every respect to those presented in Fig. fig12 for 1ES 0347-121, are shown in top-left panel of Fig. fig14 . For this source we also present two fits for a model that includes the ALP oscillation process (top-right panel of Fig. fig14 ), but without account of any secondary (cascade) emission.

For the case of the source 1ES 0229+200 we also performed a fit in the framework of an exotic model with ALP oscillations and a calculation of the modification factor K_ B vs. voidiness K_ V dependence in the framework of the electromagnetic cascade model. We found that when K_ V is lower than 1, the value of K_ B does not fall at high energies, but, on the contrary, even grows in the energy range of 1-15 TeV , thus making the effects induced by electromagnetic cascades even more pronounced.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 0229+200 as an extreme HBL with hard TeV emission, using its observed SED to test conventional physics against photon-ALP oscillation models.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: RA: 02 h 32 m 48.6 s and DEC:+20 d 17 m 17 s

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200 - 1ES 0229+200 is a BL Lac observed in the sky at RA: 02 h 32 m 48.6 s and DEC:+20 d 17 m 17 s at a redshift z=0.1396 . 1ES 0229+200 is the prototype of the so-called `extreme HBL' (EHBL) btgs2015,cbtgtk2018 which shows a rather hard VHE observed spectrum up to at least 10 TeV. This fact is particularly interesting since the observed data points at such high energies allow to distinguish between different models (conventional physics versus photon-ALP oscillations). Future observations with the CTA that can eventually reach energies up to 100 TeV could give a definitive answer. In Figure 0229 we plot its observed SED both when only conventional physics is taken into account and in the case in which also a oscillations are present.

sources.summary: The paper uses 1ES 0229+200 as a TeV BL Lac example, deriving interaction efficiencies and noting that leptonic fits require unusually large Doppler factors.

quotes.quote: Here we take 1ES 0229+200 as an example to further interpret the relation between _ and f_ ph . The critical energy E_ for this source is 7.3 TeV. By adjusting the physical parameters to make _ (E_ ) equal to 1, we have f_ ph 10^ 3 at the proton energy E_ p, 3 10^5E_ 2.2 10^ 18 eV where p- interaction efficiency reaches the maximum.

For 1ES 0229+200, one of the important outcome of the SSC interpretation 2014ApJ...782...13A is that the minimum _ D required in the fitting of SSC model is _ D 53 , which is significantly higher than the commonly adopted value for blazar jets. In earlier studies, a large _ D has been commonly suggested for this object, e.g., adopt _ D = 50 and adopt _ D = 40.

sources.summary: The paper finds 1ES 0229+200 difficult for the recollimation-shock scenario, requiring a higher SSC peak, parsec-scale emission region, near-Eddington jet power, and large shock Lorentz factor.

quotes.quote: In the case of 1ES 0229+200, the SSC component of the model is not sufficiently steep to pass through the ray data points, as a result of the smaller value of _ e, min . The estimated SSC peak frequency, which is one of the input parameters for the modelling, needs to be increased significantly to be able to approach the VHE data points in 1ES 0229+200. This leads to an increased radius of the emission region, of the order of a parsec, and a required jet power close to the Eddington limit. The magnetic field strength of this solution would be quite small, of the order of 10^ 5 G.

To reproduce the measured VHE flux in 1ES 0229+200, a minimum value of _ j< n 20 is necessary, while in the case of 1ES 1101-232, _ j< n 10 leads to a sufficiently steep rise of the SED at low energies to respect the constraints from the optical/IR data.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 0229+200 as an extreme BL Lac at z=0.1396, emphasizing its hard very-high-energy spectrum and using its observed SED to test photon-ALP oscillation effects.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200 --This is a BL Lac at redshift z=0.1396 . This is the prototype of the so-called `extreme HBL' (EHBL)  , which exhibit a rather hard VHE observed spectrum up to at least 10 TeV. This fact is particularly interesting since the observed data points at such high energies allow to distinguish between the models based on conventional physics and those containing a oscillations. Future observations with the CTA that can eventually reach energies up to 100 TeV can provide a definitive answer. In Figure  0229 we plot its observed SED both when only conventional physics is taken into account and in the case in which also a oscillations are present.

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0229+200 as a hard-TeV blazar whose parameter space is tightly restricted; its high-energy hump is proton-synchrotron dominated while UV host-galaxy emission is excluded.

quotes.quote: Here we study two typical hard-TeV spectrum blazars, which are 1ES 0229+200 and 1ES 1101-232. In the _ D - B diagrams of Figs. 0229 and 1101 , under the observational constraints, it can be seen that only when setting R=1 10^ 17 cm the parameter space can be found. Especially for 1ES 0229+200, its parameter space is restricted strictly. In the modeling, the high-energy hump is dominated by the proton synchrotron radiation, and the SSC radiation of electrons can be ignored, since electrons mainly lose energy through the synchrotron radiation, as shown by the cooling timescales in Figs. 0229 and 1101 . Please note that, the UV data points of 1ES 0229+200 are suggested as the emission from the host galaxy , therefore we do not fit them with the jet model.

sources.summary: The paper uses 1ES 0229+200 as one of three distant TeV blazars constraining EBL attenuation, finding an AHA0.65-corrected intrinsic spectral index of 2.40.

quotes.quote: Imran 1 1 Department of Physics and Astronomy, Iowa State University, Ames, IA, 5011-3160, USA, e-mail: krennrich@iastate.edu 2 Observational Cosmology Laboratory, Code 665, NASA Goddard Space Flight Center, Greenbelt, MD 20771, e-mail: eli.dwek@nasa.gov We combine the recent estimate of the contribution of galaxies to the 3.6 \ intensity of the extragalactic background light (EBL) with optical and near-infrared (IR) galaxy counts to set new limits on intrinsic spectra of some of the most distant TeV blazars 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 1218+30.4, and 1ES 1101-232, located at redshifts 0.1396, 0.182, and 0.186, respectively. The new lower limit on the 3.6 \ EBL intensity is significantly higher than the previous one set by the cumulative emission from resolved \ galaxies. Correcting for attenuation by the revised EBL, we show that the differential spectral index of the intrinsic spectrum of the three blazars is 1.28 0.20 or harder. These results present blazar emission models with the challenge of producing extremely hard intrinsic spectra in the sub-TeV to multi-TeV regime.

By searching for the combination of intrinsic spectra of these three sources that yield the softest spectral indices, one finds that the AHA0.65 scenario gives a spectral index _ i = 1.28 0.20 for 1ES 1101-232, _ i = 1.30 0.38 for 1ES 1218+30.4, while 1ES 0229+200 gives _ i = 2.40 0.13 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 0229+200 as a distant VHE blazar for testing EBL attenuation, reporting that a modified EBL density can leave its inferred intrinsic spectrum fitted with a power-law index of at least 1.5.

quotes.quote: Ref. krennrich:2008a (KDI08) argued that for the VHE source 1ES 0229+200 ( z = 0.1396 ), 1ES 1218+304 ( z = 0.182 ), 1ES 1101-232 ( z = 0.186 ) such a high EBL density should always lead to intrinsic spectra harder than =1.28 0.20 .

We will, therefore, only consider the spectra of the blazars 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 0229+200 . In addition, we will also show results for 1ES 1218+304 to facilitate the comparison with the results from KDI08.
The resulting intrinsic spectra are shown in Fig. fig:spectra and the fit results are given in Tab. ta1 . All intrinsic spectra all well fitted with power laws with 1.5 or larger.

sources.summary: The paper uses simultaneous GeV-TeV observations of 1ES 0229+200 to constrain EGMF, finding GeV flux below zero-field cascade expectations and inferring magnetic-field bounds from suppression mechanisms.

quotes.quote: Constraints on EGMF could be derived from the simultaneous GeV-TeV data on the blazars RGB J0710+591, 1ES 0229+200, and 1ES 1218+304. The measured source flux level in the GeV band is lower than the flux of the expected cascade component calculated under the assumption of zero EGMF. Assuming that the reason for the suppression of the cascade component is the extended nature of the cascade emission, we find that B 10^ 15 G (assuming EGMF correlation length of 1 Mpc) is consistent with the data. Alternatively, the assumption that the suppression of the cascade emission is caused by the time delay of the cascade photons the data are consistent with B 10^ 17 G for the same correlation length.

sources.summary: The paper discusses 1ES 0229+200 as an HBL TeV source with HESS detection, hard observed spectrum, Swift data, Fermi/LAT upper limit, and no detected variability.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200 A HBL object was observed by HESS in 2005/2006, and confirmed to be a TeV source (Aharonian et al. 2007c). The integral flux above 580 GeV is 9.4 10^ 13 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , corresponding to 1.8 of Crab flux, and the observed spectrum is characterized by a hard power law with 2.5 from 500 GeV to 15 TeV. During the observation, no significant variability on any scale was detected. Except for the data of the HESS observation, the broadband SED considered in this work also includes the data of Swift observation in August 2008 and an upper limit of Fermi /LAT observations (Tavecchio et al. 2010b).

sources.summary: The paper assumes a Fermi-LAT spectral index for 1ES 0229+200, applies BDT processing because standard detection was weak, and says it gives the strongest cascade-fraction constraints.

quotes.quote: Note that in the absence of a Fermi LAT-measured spectral index for 1ES 0229+200, an intrinsic spectral index =1.5 is assumed.

The hadron separation was achieved using selection on mean-scaled Hillas parameters for all sources other than H 1426+428, 1ES 0229+200, and VER J0521+211. For these three sources, which were not detected to a high significance with standard hadron separation, a boosted decision tree (BDT) analysis was used.
Upper limits were derived for PG 1553+113 and VER J0521+211 as well, however 1ES 0229+200 allows the strongest constraints on f_ compared to the prediction.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 0229+200 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200 : The faintest source in our optical sample, resulting in failing calculation of the modulation index for the source. The source is also one of the weakest sources in the radio band in our sample. Still the radio light curve shows significant increasing trend, while in the optical light curve the increasing trend is significant only at 99.9 significance level. Our polynomial subtraction method decreases the rms of the optical light curve very little, which is probably due to large uncertainty in the optical data.

sources.summary: The paper uses 1ES 0229+200 as the prototype hard-TeV EHBL, emphasizing its redshift, characterized synchrotron emission, hard VHE spectrum, and difficult Fermi detection.

quotes.quote: The prototypical hard-TeV EHBL is 1ES 0229+200, located at a moderate redshift of 0.14 aharonian07,tavecchio09 . The synchrotron peak of 1ES 0229+200 was sampled in great detail in a multi-wavelength campaign carried out in 2010 including optical, UV and X-ray data which firmly characterized the synchrotron emission of this object kaufmann11,aliu14 .

Due to the extreme hardness of the intrinsic spectrum which does not show any curvature at VHE up to 10 TeV, 1ES 0229+200 yields the necessary TeV photons to study a wider range of the EBL spectrum up to the, yet less constrained, far infrared band aharonian07 .

For example, the Fermi LAT reports a significant detection of 1ES 0229+200 only after 4 years of exposure time Acero15,vovk12 and despite the hard VHE spectrum it is not present in the the Second Catalog of Hard Fermi LAT Sources, 2FHL Ackermann16 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 0229+200 as the prototype EHBL, modeling its hard TeV spectrum with hadron-beam, photon-ALP, and LIV effects.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200 1ES 0229+200 represents the prototype of EHBLs and possesses a hard TeV observed spectrum extending above 10 TeV hess0229 in spite of the fact that it is located at a redshift z=0.1396 , where EBL absorption in the TeV energy range is huge (optical depth 2 , e.g. EBLfranc ). EHBLs have peculiar and not yet completely understood features which differentiate them from other BL Lacs. As far as the VHE spectrum is concerned, its extreme hardness challenges standard one-zone leptonic models (see e.g. tavExtreme09 ) and its weak variability is untypical for the BL Lac population (see e.g. variabExtreme ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1ES 0229+200 as an extreme TeV and highly peaked BL Lac object, comparing its measured SED with telescope sensitivities and hard-spectrum detectability.

quotes.quote: Indeed, some blazars such as 1ES 0229+200 Aharonian2007a,Aliu2014 , 1ES 1101-232 Aharonian2006,Aharonian2007b and 1ES 0347-121 Aharonian2007c may be classified as both ETBs (see Dzhatdoev2017 , Fig. 12, 14, 17) and EHBLs. Compared to well-known nearby blazars such as Mkn 501 and Mkn 421, ETBs, as a rule, reveal weak and slow variability in the high energy (HE, E > 100 MeV) and the very high energy (VHE, E > 100 GeV) spectral bands.

In Fig. Fig11 we show the SEDs of extreme TeV blazars 1ES 1101-232 , 1ES 0347-121 , and 1ES 0229+200 ( z_ s = 0.14 ) measured with H.E.S.S. Aharonian2007a,Aharonian2007c , VERITAS Aliu2014 , and Fermi-LAT Biteau2018 in comparison with the differential sensitivity for point-like sources of various ray telescopes. The sensitivity of Fermi-LAT is barely sufficient to measure the spectra of these sources; however, IACTs can readily detect extreme TeV blazars due to their very hard intrinsic spectra.

sources.summary: The paper uses 1ES 0229+200 as a prototypical extreme blazar for CTA simulations, emphasizing its hard TeV spectrum, weak variability, and modeled conical jet.

quotes.quote: We perform simulations of the prototypical extreme blazar 1ES 0229+200, which, owing to its hard intrinsic ray spectrum extending to 10 TeV, and the lack of strong ray variability, is perhaps one of the best-suited sources for cascade signatures searches.

We employ the simplifying assumption, for 1ES 0229+200, of a conical jet with a 10^ opening angle, tilted by an angle of 5^ . The source intrinsic spectrum is taken to be a power law with an exponential cut-off at E_ cut ' = 10 TeV.
A flaring state of Mrk 501 is simulated with a 10 hour long CTA exposure, whereas a long-term observation of 1ES 0229+200 is simulated with a 50 hour one.

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 0229+200 as an extreme TeV BL Lac with a well-characterized SED and uses it as the representative case for particle and turbulence timescales.

quotes.quote: We apply the procedure described above to four Extreme TeV BL Lacs: 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 0347-121, RGB J0710+591, and 1ES 1101-232 taken from . All these EHBLs have a well-characterized SED with a synchrotron peak well-tracked by Swift and NuSTAR and a high-energy peak covered by Fermi and Cherenkov telescopes.

As a representative case, we display in electron0229 and turbulence0229 the results for 1ES 0229+200. Finally, we report the median and the credible interval of the final timescales associated with electrons (i.e. acceleration, cooling, and escape time) and turbulence (cascading and damping times) for 1ES 0229+200, respectively in electron_times_0229 and turbulence_times_0229 .

sources.summary: The paper studies GeV-TeV emission from blazar 1ES0229+200, noting that weak GeV emission informs IGMF limits and that cosmogenic-ray emission can match observations without an IGMF.

quotes.quote: As an example we have studied the GeV-TeV emission of the blazar 1ES0229+200. The absence of strong GeV emission of this source has been used to derive lower limits on the IGMF strength. In contrast, the cosmogenic ray emission of this source is consistent with the observation without the presence of an IGMF.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 0229+200 as one of four TeV blazars with adequate data, deriving lower UHECR power limits and small Auger South detection prospects.

quotes.quote: While for a 2.2 injection spectrum, the lower limits on the powers emitted in UHECRs by 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 0347-121 are lower than their respective synchrotron luminosities ( 10^ 46 erg s ^ 1 ); in the case of 1ES 1426+428 it exceeds the corresponding synchrotron luminosity by up to an order of magnitude. Currently there are four such TeV blazars with adequate data, namely 1ES 1426+428 hess1426 , 1ES 0229+200 hess0229,veritas0229 , 1ES 1101-232 hess1101 and 1ES 0347-121 hess0347 . The detection prospect for UHECRs with = 2.2 from the other 3 blazars within the field-of-view of the Auger South is much smaller, N_ evt = 0.04 , 0.03 and 0.02 respectively for 1ES 0229+200 ( = +20.27^ ), 1ES 1101-232 ( = -23.50^ ) and 1ES 0347-121 ( = -11.98^ ); for L_p = 10^ 45 erg/s and E = 9,000 km ^2 yr sr.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1ES 0229 + 200 as an extreme blazar whose SED and possible very-high-energy detection could test gamma-ray emission mechanisms above tens of TeV.

quotes.quote: Prominent sources such as extreme blazars (1ES 0229 + 200), nearby well-known BL Lac objects (MKN 501) and radio-galaxies, galactic pulsar wind nebulae (Crab Nebula, Vela-X), supernovae remnants (Vela-junior, RX J1713.7 3946) and microquasars (LS 5039), as well as the Galactic Center can be observed in a previously unexplored energy range, in order to investigate the electron acceleration and cooling, relativistic and non relativistic shocks, the search for cosmic-ray (CR) Pevatrons, the study of the CR propagation, and the impact of the extragalactic background light on the spectra of the sources.

The ASTRI mini-array will be extremely important to investigate the VHE emission from extragalactic sources as well. Figure fig:022 shows the SED of the extreme blazar 1ES 0229 + 200 ( ). A clear detection of VHE emission above a few tens of TeV from such a blazar could provide fundamental information on the long-standing debate on the emission mechanisms in this energy band.

sources.summary: ATOM observations of 1ES 0229+200 found no clear color-magnitude correlation, only slight brightness variation, and a very steep optical continuum.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200: The source was observed during 184 nights in 2007--2012. No clear CM correlation found, either in the entire dataset or in shorter (e.g., one-year) intervals, but the source brightness varied only slightly within the 0.2 mag range. The optical continuum was very steep, with _ BR > 3.7 .

sources.summary: The paper uses 1ES 0229+200 as a prototypical extreme BL Lac, comparing candidate SEDs with its TeV spectrum and hard SSC component.

quotes.quote: For comparison, we report in background (gray) the data corresponding to 1ES 0229+200 (for simplicity only the observed TeV spectrum is shown). It is clear that the structure of the synchrotron part of the SED of all the sources closely resembles that of 1ES 0229+200.
As for 1ES 0229+200, this also causes a very hard SSC component, consistent with the extremely low flux in the LAT band.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2WHSP J023248.5+201717 as an extreme HSP blazar with VHE data, a synchrotron peak near 10^18-10^19 Hz, and an inverse-Compton peak above 1 TeV after EBL correction.

quotes.quote: 2WHSP J023248.5+201717 (1ES0229+200) . This is an extreme source with VHE data available the ebl-deabsorbed VHE data shown as black filled circles are from Finke2015 . The synchrotron peak is at 10^ 18 10^ 19 Hz and the peak flux is one of the highest among the 2WHSP sources. In the VHE band, once one corrects the VHE fluxes for EBL absorption, the inverse Compton peak will be at energies >1 TeV.

sources.summary: The paper reports 2010-2011 outbursts for 1ES 0229+200, with high peak luminosity and energy, and lower 2008/2015/2016 states with reduced luminosity, energy, and curvature.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0229+200 had outbursts from 2010 to 2011, during which its peak luminosity reached 10 ^ 45.1 \ erg \ s ^ 1 , peak energy reached 9.4 keV, and curvature maintained nearly at 1. The five observations in 2008, 2015 and 2016 represented the low-flux states of this source, whose peak luminosity decreased to 10 ^ 44.3 \ erg \ s ^ 1 , peak energy decreased to 2 keV, and curvature decreased to about 0.5. Our result is consistent with that of .

sources.summary: The paper uses 1ES 0229+200 as a hard-spectrum TeV BL Lac whose derived EBL SED depends on fixed intrinsic spectral-index assumptions.

quotes.quote: Comparing the EBL density given by galaxy count and Spitzer observations, we then obtain that 1ES 1101-232 has _i 1.0 and 1ES 0229+200 should have _i not harder than 1.0.

Besides, at least some distant TeV BL Lacs, e.g., 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 0229+200, exhibit a much harder spectral index. point out that their spectral index _i is 1.28 or harder even under the weakest EBL model.

From Table Table:1 Table:2 and Figure Fig:6 , we can find that, for the 1ES 0229+200, the EBL SED is slightly larger than that obtained by the Spitzer mid-IR and galaxy count when _i =1.5 is fixed, while the upper limit of the derived EBL SED is close to the direct measurements if _i =1.0 is given.

gmw_00000523.name: 1ES 0647+250
gmw_00000523.overview: The active galactic nucleus 1ES 0647+250 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2211.13268] with a detectable near-infrared host galaxy [arXiv:1109.0627]. It exhibits strong multi-band variability [arXiv:2211.13268], including optical outbursts [arXiv:1606.06431] and X-ray flux increases [arXiv:1909.06801], alongside a radio jet that displays a large projected bend [arXiv:1801.00817].

sources.summary: The paper finds that 0647+251 has jet components differing by over 90 degrees in position angle, uses 15 GHz imaging to identify the core, and interprets the structure as a projected bend.

quotes.quote: In addition to the 8.4 GHz images described above, we also obtained images at 15.3 GHz during experiment S7017 of the two sources 0033+595 and 0647+251. These two sources displayed apparent jet bends exceeding 90 in their images in Paper I, and images at a higher frequency were obtained in order to attempt identification of the core in these sources from its spectral properties.

We also noted in Paper I that two sources (0033+595 and 0647+251) showed jet components that differed by more than 90 in their position angles. Because of the likely at least modest Doppler factor of the radio emission in these sources, such structure is unlikely to represent a jet and counter-jet. Images of these two sources were obtained at 15 GHz to compare with the 8 GHz images, and these are also shown in Figure 1; in each case the spectral index of the presumed core and of component 2 are similar within the estimated errors (see Table mfittab ). However, we are confident in the core identification in each source due to the measured brightness temperatures, which are or order 10^ 10 K for the presumed core at every epoch, but only of order 10^ 8 K for the closest jet component at every epoch (for both sources); these can be compared with typical core brightness temperatures from Figure 4 of Paper I. We expect then that these two sources are cases where the jet has a large apparent bend due to projection effects; deep images at lower frequencies would also be useful to confirm this.

sources.summary: The paper presents a long-term multiwavelength study of 1ES 0647+250, finding VHE detections, strong variability, redshift constraints, and leptonic SED models with an extra radio region.

quotes.quote: The BL Lac object 1ES 0647+250 is one of the few distant ray emitting blazars detected at very high energies (VHEs; 100 GeV) during a non-flaring state. It was detected with the MAGIC telescopes during a period of low activity in the years 2009-2011 as well as during three flaring activities in the years 2014, 2019, and 2020, with the highest VHE flux in the last epoch. An extensive multi-instrument data set was collected as part of several coordinated observing campaigns over these years. We aim to characterise the long-term multi-band flux variability of 1ES 0647+250, as well as its broadband spectral energy distribution (SED) during four distinct activity states selected in four different epochs, in order to constrain the physical parameters of the blazar emission region under certain assumptions.

The indirect estimation of the redshift yielded a value of z=0.45 0.05 , which is compatible with some of the values reported in the literature. The SEDs related to the low-activity state and the three flaring states of 1ES 0647+250 can be described reasonably well with the both one-component and two-component leptonic scenarios. However, the long-term correlations indicate the need for an additional radio-producing region located about 3.6 pc downstream from the gamma-ray producing region.

sources.summary: The paper detects and characterizes the near-infrared host galaxy of the BL Lac object 1ES 0647+250, deriving its magnitude, effective radius, and imaging redshift.

quotes.quote: We obtained a deep, high-resolution near-infrared H band image of the BL Lacertae object 1ES 0647+250. We are able to detect the underlying host galaxy in the near--infrared. The host galaxy has an H band magnitude of 16.9 0.2 and an effective radius of 1 6 0 3. Using the imaging redshift method by Sbarufatti et al. (2005), we estimate a redshift z = 0.41 0.06.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 0647+250 as a VHE BL Lac source in its monitoring sample and reports its optical/radio flux level, variability trends, and correlation behavior.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0647+250 : The optical light curve shows clear outbursts and significant increasing trend. In radio the source is rather weak, but the increasing trend is significant also in this band. As discussed in Sect. 4.3., the positive correlation found in DCF analysis, with time lag of 1030 days, is due to this common trend rather than correlated flares. The polynomial subtraction suggests that at least 10 of the optical flux would originate from common optical-radio component (see Fig. Fig:lc10 ).

sources.summary: The paper confirms the featureless nature of 1ES 0647+250 with a higher-SNR GTC spectrum and derives a redshift lower limit from nondetected features.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0647+250 : An optical spectrum of this source, with modest SNR was found featureless by schachter1993 , a result later confirmed by a better spectrum obtained with the Keck telescope by shaw2013a . A relatively high redshift can be supported by the absence of detection of the host galaxy from high quality image by kotilainen2011 . Our GTC higher SNR ( 200) spectrum confirms this featureless behaviour with absorptions at around 4400 and 4880 due to Diffusing Interstellar bands (DIBs) and at 6500 due to water vapor. On the basis of our spectrum we set a lower limit of z > 0.29.

sources.summary: The paper finds that 1ES 0647+250 brightened in X-rays from 2010 to 2012 while its peak energy and curvature varied, but large errors prevented a plotted best-fit relation.

quotes.quote: 1ES 0647+250 showed a gradually increasing trend of X-ray flux from 2010 to 2012. During this period, its peak luminosity increased from 10 ^ 45.8 to 10 ^ 46.5 \ erg \ s ^ 1 , while its peak energy ranged between 0.5 and 2 keV, and curvature around the peak ranged between 0.25 and 1. Given the large error bars of the fitting result of \ relation (see Table table:table1 ), we do not show the best-fit linear model of this source in Figure fig:eplp .

gmw_00000524.name: 1ES 1101-232
gmw_00000524.overview: The active galactic nucleus 1ES 1101-232 is an extreme high-frequency-peaked BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1412.4716, arXiv:2311.12695] hosted by an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1801.04423, arXiv:1911.06011]. It features a one-sided, kiloparsec-scale radio jet [arXiv:1801.04423, arXiv:0808.0184] and exhibits a hard, weakly variable very-high-energy gamma-ray spectrum [arXiv:1412.4716, arXiv:1205.6149].

sources.summary: 1ES 1101-232 is analyzed as an EHBL UHECR emitter whose hard TeV spectrum can be fit by the cascade model, with significant anisotropy in hard-spectrum magnetic-field cases.

quotes.quote: Very-high-energy (VHE; 100 GeV) ray observations have resulted in the detections of several extreme high-frequency-peaked BL Lac objects (EHBLs), most notably 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 1101-232, and 1ES 0347-121, which show very hard VHE spectra ( 3; dN / dE E^ ) extending to tens of TeV e.g., Aharonian2007AA470p475,Aharonian2007AA473L25,Aharonian2007AA475L9 . Moreover, these sources show weak variability, unlike most blazars. VHE rays can be attenuated by pair-creation interactions with EBL photons while propagating through intergalactic space Nikishov1962JETP14p393,Gould1966PRL16p252,Stecker1992ApJ390L49 . A possible scenario to overcome the severe absorption that would soften the TeV spectra of these sources is the UHECR-induced cascade model e.g., Essey2010APh33p81,Essey2011ApJ731p51,Murase2012ApJ749p63,Razzaque2012ApJ745p196 .

We have generally derived the constraints on the CR luminosity of a powerful source from the observed isotropy at 10^ 19 eV. In this section, we specifically focus on three known EHBLs as powerful UHECR emitters, that is, 1ES 0229+200 Aharonian2007AA475L9 , 1ES 0347-121 Aharonian2007AA473L25 , and 1ES 1101-232 Aharonian2007AA470p475 , all of which have been detected by the High Energy Spectroscopic System (H.E.S.S.). The spectra of these objects in the VHE range can be well reproduced by the UHECR-induced cascade model e.g., Essey2011ApJ731p51,Murase2012ApJ749p63 . The isotropic-equivalent CR luminosities to reproduce the observed flux are shown in Table tab:ehbls with their redshift and positions.

Figure fig:1es1101 shows the same as Figure fig:1es0229E200 , but for 1ES 1101-232. Significant anisotropy appears at 3 level under the LEGMF with B_ LEG a few nG as for 1ES 0229+200 in the hard-spectrum cases.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 1101-232 as a BL Lac in an elliptical host at z=0.186, with a one-sided kpc jet and multiwavelength HESS-era observations used to model its SED.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1101-232 The HBL 1ES 1101-232 resides in an elliptical host galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.186 . The radio maps of this HBL show an one-sided, not well-collimated jet structure at a few kpc distance from the core . In 2004 and 2005, 1ES 1101-232 was observed by the HESS telescopes and following the detection of a weak signal in its observations, an extended multifrequency campaign was organized for 11 nights in March 2005 to study the multiwavelength emission and to look for possible correlated variability in different wavebands .

But, the analysis of 3.5 years data collected from August 2008 to February 2012 by Fermi-LAT reported the observation of GeV emission from this object . From the simultaneous observations in optical, X-rays and VHE rays, Aharonian et al. constructed a truly simultaneous SED of 1ES 1101-232. In 2006 May and July, Suzaku observed this HBL in X-rays which was also quasi-simultaneously observed with HESS and MAGIC telescopes and no significant variability was observed neither in X-rays nor in rays . In fact during this observation period it was found in a quiescent state with the lowest X-ray flux ever measured. The multiwavelength observation of the blazar 1ES 1101-232 during the flaring in 2004-2005 is used to constructed the synchrotron and SSC SED using one zone leptonic model and the parameters for the best fit are given in Table- tab:tab1 .

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 1101-232 among six extreme BL Lacs, explaining its broadband SED with a two-zone lepto-hadronic scenario involving inner-blob photohadronic and outer-blob SSC/EC emission.

quotes.quote: Finally, using the parameters listed in Table tab_model_parameters we model successfully the broadband SED of six known extreme BL Lac 1ES 0229+200, RGB J0710+591, 1ES 0347-121, 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 1218-304, 1ES 0414+009. The result of our model is shown in Figure ( fig_SED_result ). It is important to mention that in this work, we have not included the contribution observed in the optical band produced by the host galaxy Costamante2018 . The extreme BL Lac behavior presents challenges for a pure leptonic or hadronic model with one-zone emission. These objects demand atypical parameters for explaining the hard spectrum shape, especially in a VHE gamma-ray band.

In particular cases, we described the six best-known extreme BL Lacs using our proposed model, and we found a feasible explanation that relaxes the atypical values required by the one-zone models. We showed that the annihilation line radiation produced in a mildly relativistic outflow pair-plasma and the DT play essential roles in forming the hard spectrum observed in the VHE gamma-ray bands. In our model, this gamma-ray bump is produced mainly in two regions: i) the inner blob, which is responsible for the highest energy contribution above TeV-energies via photo-hadronic interactions with the annihilation line photons from the pair-plasma, and ii) the outer blob, where the very-high-energy gamma rays are created by EC with seed photons from the DT, and by the SSC mechanism.

sources.summary: The paper reports the first H.E.S.S. VHE gamma-ray detection of the blazar 1ES 1101-232, finding a hard, nonvariable spectrum and building simultaneous optical-to-VHE SEDs.

quotes.quote: The blazar 1ES 1101-232 was observed with the High Energy Stereoscopic System (H.E.S.S.) of Atmospheric Cherenkov Telescopes (ACT) in 2004 and 2005, for a live time of 43 hours. VHE ( E > 10^ 11 eV) rays were detected for the first time from this object. VHE observations of blazars are used to investigate the inner parts of the blazar jets, and also to study the extragalactic background light (EBL) in the near-infrared band.

1ES 1101-232 was detected with H.E.S.S. with an excess of 649 photons, at a significance of 10 . The measured VHE ray flux amounts to d N/ d E = (5.63 0.89) 10^ 13 (E/ TeV )^ (2.94 0.20) cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 TeV ^ 1 , above a spectral energy threshold of 225 GeV. No significant variation of the VHE ray flux on any time scale was found. 1ES 1101-232 exhibits a very hard spectrum, and at a redshift of z=0.186 , is the blazar with the highest confirmed redshift detected in VHE rays so far.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 1101-232 as a distant TeV blazar whose H.E.S.S. spectrum and redshift make it useful for probing cosmic infrared background attenuation and Hubble-constant fits.

quotes.quote: Two blazars Mrk 501 and 1ES 1101-232, which have relatively good spectra measurements by the atmospheric Cerenkov telescope, are studied to measure H_0 .

The other source investigated here, 1ES 1101-232, is a distant blazar with redshift z=0.186 . TeV observation of 1ES 1101-232 was performed by H.E.S.S. in 2005 2006Natur.440.1018A . It is shown that even though the measured maximum energy only reaches 3 TeV, 1ES 1101-232 is still very effective to probe the intensity of CIB in the propagation path VHE rays go through due to its large distance from us 2006Natur.440.1018A . The observational spectrum of 1ES 1101-232 is shown in the right panel of Fig. spectrum .
The best-fitting intrinsic spectrum is _ int =2.62 for Mrk 501 and 0.93 for 1ES 1101-232. It seems that the intrinsic spectrum for 1ES 1101-232 is still too hard.

sources.summary: The paper models 1ES 1101-232 with inner- and extended-jet components, finding its hard, non-variable TeV emission is dominated by IC/CMB radiation from a moderately or highly beamed extended jet.

quotes.quote: We apply the model to explain the non-variable hard TeV emission of 1ES 1101-232. No very significant signal from 1ES 1101-232 is detected by Fermi LAT in its first year scientific operation abdoagn , and only the flux upper limits derived from the first year Fermi LAT observations are available (e.g., ). The two years scientific operation of Fermi LAT reports a significant detection of 1ES 1101-232 at a significance 5.2 with the photon spectral index =1.80 0.31 Ackermann . More significant signals ( 10 ) from 1ES 1101-232 have been detected in Fermi LAT's 3.5 years observations finke12 .

It can be seen that our model can well reproduce the SED of 1ES 1101-232, and its hard TeV emission is dominated by the IC/CMB component from the extended jet. We can well reproduce the TeV emission of 1ES 1101-232 using each one of them. Furthermore, for a moderate or a large value of Doppler factor ( _ D =10 or 15), the physical parameters can achieve equipartition (or quasi-equipartition) between the relativistic electrons and the magnetic field.

sources.summary: The paper discusses 1ES 1101-232 as a UHBL whose previous hadronic TeV interpretation required extreme proton power, then fits it in the authors’ global lepto-hadronic framework.

quotes.quote: Out of the 44 HBLs detected so far in the TeV range, five sources --- 1ES 0229+200, 1ES 0347-121, RGB J0710+591, 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 1218+304 --- are characterized by particularly hard spectra with the high-energy peak above 1 TeV, which is consistent with their very hard and weak signal in the Fermi LAT band. Another common characteristic is the lack of observable variability on short time-scales of the very-high-energy flux with the exception of 1ES 1218+304, which showed VHE flaring activity on the scale of days, 1218_Veritas . Although the BL Lac object H 1426+428 belongs to this sub-class as well, since it was detected at VHE only during flaring activity without extensive multi-wavelength coverage 1426_hegra, Arache, 1426_whipple2, 1426_whipple, Leonardo09, wystan we decided to exclude it from our sample. The modelling of these ultra-high-frequency peaked BL Lacs UHBLs, also known as extreme BL Lacs, EHBLs, see e.g. for a more recent review Costamante01, Gunes13 with one-zone SSC scenarios, which usually yield good results for HBLs, is proving difficult in most cases, as can be seen from Table tab:uhbl , where some recent attempts at SSC modeling of the sources are summarized. A good representation of the complete SEDs requires either extremely high values of the minimum Lorentz factor of the electron distribution (1ES 1101-232, 1ES 0347-121), or an elevated Doppler factor (1ES 1218+304), or both (1ES 0229+200, RGB J0710+591).

A hadronic origin of the TeV spectrum of 1ES 1101-232 has recently been proposed by Cao14 . They ascribe the spectrum measured with the H.E.S.S. Cherenkov telescopes from this source to secondary ray emission from neutral pion decay following interactions of sub-PeV protons with synchrotron radiation inside the source. This interpretation, however, requires an extremely large power in protons of a few 10 ^ 53 erg s ^ 1 , around six orders of magnitudes higher than the Eddington luminosity of the super-massive black hole (SMBH) powering the AGN.

sources.summary: The appendix describes 1ES 1101-232 as a distant VHE blazar in an elliptical host, with H.E.S.S. emission, steady VHE, X-ray, and optical behavior, and a broken-power-law X-ray spectrum.

quotes.quote: The blazar 1ES 1101-232 was discovered as a distant VHE -ray source in an elliptical host galaxy at redshift z = 0.186 . The discovery of VHE -ray emission from this source was reported in 2007 using H.E.S.S. observations performed during 2004--2005 in the energy range above 250 GeV . The observed VHE spectrum was described as a power law function with a spectral index of 2.94 0.20 in the energy range of 200 GeV to 4 TeV. No evidence of variability at any timescale in the VHE light curve of the source was detected. The X-ray emission of the source in the energy range 3--15 keV was found to be compatible with a broken power law of spectral indices 2.49 0.02 and 2.78 0.16 before and after the break energy ( 8 keV) respectively.

The peak energy in the first hump of the SED was found to be located between 0.5 keV and 3.5 keV. The X-ray and optical emissions from the source were also observed to be constant like their VHE counterpart.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 1101-232 as one of three selected VHE blazars, using H.E.S.S. and Fermi data to search for extended pair-halo and cascade emission.

quotes.quote: Both extended pair halo (PH) and magnetically broadened cascade (MBC) emission from regions surrounding the blazars 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 0229+200 and PKS 2155-304 were searched for, using VHE ray data taken with the High Energy Stereoscopic System (H.E.S.S.), and high energy (HE; 100 MeV < E < 100 GeV) ray data with the Fermi Large Area Telescope (LAT). By comparing the angular distributions of the reconstructed ray events to the angular profiles calculated from detailed theoretical models, the presence of PH and MBC was investigated. Upper limits on the extended emission around 1ES 1101-232, 1ES 0229+200 and PKS 2155-304 are found to be at a level of few percent of the Crab nebula flux above 1 TeV, depending on the assumed photon index of the cascade emission.

1ES 1101-232 The blazar 1ES 1101-232 was first discovered with H.E.S.S. in 2004 at VHE ray energies 2007A&A...470..475A . It resides in an elliptical host galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.186 1994ApJS...93..125F . A total of 66 hours of good quality data, taken between 2004 and 2008, have been analysed, resulting in a detection significance exceeding 10 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 1ES 1101--232 as an HBL in an elliptical host, correctly identified as a BL Lac, with hard TeV data improved by a hadronic UHECR component.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1101--232 : This HBL resides in a elliptical host galaxy at redshift z=0.186 . It was first detected by Ariel-5 X-ray satellite and was misidentified with the Abell 1146 galaxy cluster at redshift z=0.139 Maccagni_78, McHardy_81 . The optical and radio data led to the correct identification as a BL Lac object Buckley_85, Remillard_89 . The highest energy data points exhibit very hard TeV spectra, which has been explained earlier using hadronic origin inside the jet emission region Cerruti_15 .

It can be seen that that the highest energy data points are not well-covered by the SSC spectrum alone in a pure-leptonic fit. The fit improves considerably in the entire VHE range with the addition of the hadronic component originating in UHECRs.

sources.summary: The paper uses 1ES 1101-232 as a hard-spectrum TeV BL Lac whose EBL limits and spectral-index behavior challenge simple transparency conclusions.

quotes.quote: Besides, we take one distant source, 1ES0229-200, and two sources with the hard spectra, 1ES1101-232 and 1ES1218+304, because their TeV emissions are more subject to EBL absorption, and their hard spectra are natural candidates to study the lower limit on EBL.

Besides, at least some distant TeV BL Lacs, e.g., 1ES 1101-232 and 1ES 0229+200, exhibit a much harder spectral index. point out that their spectral index _i is 1.28 or harder even under the weakest EBL model.

We find that this faint value is also higher than the derived EBL density by 1ES 1101-232 under the case of _i=1 , implying this TeV BL Lacs may have much harder spectra. We summarize the results as following: (1) Four TeV BL Lacs of MKn421, MKn501, 1ES1101-232, and 1ES0229+200 can well constrain the upper limits of EBL SED. However, for 1ES0229+200 with lower , the EBL SED is higher than that obtained by another source with large scale factor, such as 1ES1101-232, because the fitting optical depths have large uncertain (the error to be 1.6) and the EBL templates are only given by ; (2) The upper limits of EBL density is around 50 nW m^ 2 sr^ 1 . at 1 eV, and the lower density could be at ones between given by spitzer fazio2004 and galaxy counts mad2000 ; (3) 1ES1218+304 favours the scenario that the EBL is more transparent to VHE rays. However, 1ES1101-232 with a similar redshift does not favour this collusion; (4) Using the fixed spectral index to limit the EBL, for the 1ES1101-232, we doubt that its spectral index could be harder than that we considered before and below 1.0.

sources.summary: HESS observations of 1ES 1101-232 produced unexpectedly hard TeV spectra, challenging high-EBL models; modest intrinsic-slope changes only slightly alter the derived EBL limit.

quotes.quote: In 2005, however, the HESS observations of the BL Lac objects 1ES 1101-232 ( z =0.186) and H 2356-309 ( z =0.165) provided a fundamental breakthrough ( ). The observed ray spectra (detected between 0.2 and 1-3 TeV) were much harder than expected for their redshift, implying extremely hard intrinsic spectra ( _ int 0 ) in case of high EBL densities (Fig. fig2 ). Such hard spectra were never seen in the closer, less absorbed objects and were at odds with all the known blazar physics and phenomenology. They were also not supported by same-epoch multiwavelength observations of their SED ( ).
For example, in the case of 1ES 1101-232, a shift of =0.3 (e.g. by assuming _ int 1.2 ) affects the EBL limit by no more than 10 ( ).

sources.summary: The review identifies 1ES 1101-232 as a key H.E.S.S. blazar whose hard VHE spectrum at z=0.186 tightly constrained the optical-NIR EBL.

quotes.quote: The breakthrough was achieved in 2005 , with the H.E.S.S. detections of 1ES 1101-232 ( z =0.186) and H 2356-309 ( z =0.165). The observed ray spectra (measured between 0.2 and 1-3 TeV) were much harder than expected for their redshift, implying extremely hard intrinsic spectra ( _ int 0 ) in case of high EBL densities (Fig. nature ). To accomodate _ int 1.5 , the upper limit on the EBL becomes (14 4) \ around 1--2 , which is very close to the lower limits given by the integrated light of galaxies , and even closer to the most recent NIR estimates . This means that more than 2/3 of the EBL in the Opt-NIR band is resolved into single sources.
In the case of 1ES 1101-232, even assuming _ int 1.2 affects the EBL limit by no more than 10 .

sources.summary: The paper uses the 1ES 1101-232 blazar spectrum as an example where a cosmic-ray beam model needs an added secondary-photon component to fit the data.

quotes.quote: As an example, let us consider the 1ES 1101-232 blazar spectrum (redshift z =0.186). Figure 2 shows a spectral energy distribution (SED) of the source measured by the H. E. S. S. Cherenkov telescope (red circles) together with uncertainties (dashed red lines); the assumed intrinsic spectrum is shown by black curve, the fit to the observed spectrum is shown by green line (for detals of calculations, that were performed with the publicly-available code ELMAG 2.02 and the EBL model of , see ). The intrinsic spectrum curve here is normalized to the absorbed one at E = 200 GeV , where absorption effects are already small. The fit here doesn't include any secondary photon component. To explain the data in the framework of the CR beam model, an additional component from secondary photons is needed (the corresponding region of the spectrum is denoted by thick blue dashed lines).

sources.summary: For 1ES 1101-232, the paper uses HESS spectra and their reanalysis, fitting absorption-only, electromagnetic-cascade, and basic hadronic models to its spectral energy distribution.

quotes.quote: 1ES 1101-232 was observed by the HESS Collaboration in 2004-2005, and the result of spectral measurements was published in Aharonian et al. . One year later, a reanalysis of these observations was performed, and the new result on the spectrum was presented in Aharonian et al. . In our work we mainly use the latter results, but we also present a fit for the case of the electromagnetic cascade model for the former analysis. Fig. fig17 , top-left panel contains a fit to the SED of 1ES 1101-232 in the absorption-only model; top-right and middle-left panels of this figure contain the fits for the case of the electromagnetic cascade model for reanalysis and 2006 analysis, respectively. A basic hadronic model fit is presented in Fig. fig17 , middle-right panel.

sources.summary: The paper finds 1ES 1101-232 cannot be satisfactorily described under some model constraints because Fermi-LAT and infrared data require extreme Doppler or shock parameters.

quotes.quote: The final source in the sample, 1ES 1101-232, cannot be satisfactorily described either given the model constraints. In addition to a poor representation of the Fermi LAT points, the infrared flux is overproduced by the model. If _ e, min remains fixed at its default value, the only way around this problem is to increase to a value well above 100, in order to shift the low-energy break to sufficiently high frequencies.

To reproduce the measured VHE flux in 1ES 0229+200, a minimum value of _ j< n 20 is necessary, while in the case of 1ES 1101-232, _ j< n 10 leads to a sufficiently steep rise of the SED at low energies to respect the constraints from the optical/IR data. Given these solutions, if acceleration were to take place at a mildly relativistic recollimation shock, this would lead to an initial bulk Lorentz factor of the jet of _ j< s 10^ 3 for 1ES 0229+200, while for 1ES 1101-232, it would result in \ _ j< s 500 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 1101 232 as an elliptical-host source with one-sided kpc structure, a faint northwest VLBI/TANAMI jet, VHE detection, and extended-jet TeV modeling.

quotes.quote: 1101 232: The source is hosted by an elliptical host galaxy. On kiloparsec scales it shows a one-sided structure with a diffuse extension to the north laurent93 . 5 and 8 GHz observations with the VLBA tiet12 as well as our 2.3 GHz TANAMI observations show a core with a faint, slightly extended jet to the northwest. aharonian07 has detected the source at VHE with H.E.S.S. SED modeling yan12 can recover the observed TeV emission by using slightly beamed high-energy jets emission, but only the models with moderately high Doppler factors result in a jet in equipartition. yan12 suggests that inverse Compton scattering of the CMB photons in the extended jet is the main source of the TeV emission.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1ES 1101-232 as an extreme TeV blazar with hard intrinsic spectra, applying shock co-acceleration modeling and finding low photon indices that place its high-energy peak above 1 TeV.

quotes.quote: We further model two of the extreme VHE sources, RGB J0710 and 1ES 1101-232 under a scenario of co-acceleration of protons and electrons on internal or recollimation shocks. The model provides a satisfactory description to the extreme TeV spectra which demands particle re-acceleration in multiple shocks to reproduce the same.
