In a low-flux state it exhibits the expected multiwavelength double-peaked spectrum in the radio -- ray band. The high-flux state shows extreme flux variations in the X-ray band with a hardening as well as a peculiar curved feature in the spectrum. Thus far, is the only source to exhibit such a feature.

is a BL Lac type blazar at coordinates =302 3558, =-48 8316 gaia_dr2 , and redshift z=0.07067 keeny2018 . Its host galaxy is a giant elliptical galaxy Scarpa2000 . was detected by Parkes in the 2.7 GHz survey in 1972 parkes . EGRET (20 MeV -- 30 GeV band) was the first telescope to make a ray detection of the source egret , which was later confirmed in the 30 GeV -- 100 TeV VHE band by the H.E.S.S. telescopes in Namibia hess10 .

sources.summary: The paper reports H.E.S.S. detection of PKS 2005 489 as a nearby high-frequency peaked BL Lac, measuring low VHE flux, a soft spectrum, and no short-timescale variability.

quotes.quote: The high-frequency peaked BL Lac PKS 2005 489 ( z = 0.071 ) was observed in 2003 and 2004 with the H.E.S.S. stereoscopic array of imaging atmospheric-Cherenkov telescopes in Namibia. A signal was detected at the 6.7 level in the 2004 observations (24.2 hrs live time), but not in the 2003 data set (27.3 hrs live time). PKS 2005 489 is the first blazar independently discovered by H.E.S.S. to be an emitter of VHE photons, and only the second such blazar in the Southern Hemisphere.

PKS 2005 489 was initially discovered as a bright ( > 0.5 Jy) radio source at 2.7 GHz and later identified as a very bright BL Lac object . It is classified as a high-frequency peaked BL Lac (HBL) due to its X-ray-to-radio flux ratio and because its broadband spectral energy distribution (SED) peaks in the UV. It has been the target of several multi-wavelength observation campaigns and is well studied from the radio to the X-ray regime.

PKS 2005 489 has been detected by H.E.S.S. at energies greater than 200 GeV in 2004. It is the first AGN independently discovered by H.E.S.S. as an emitter of VHE photons and only the second such AGN known in the Southern Hemisphere.

sources.summary: The paper observes PKS 2005--489 in a very high X-ray state, finds a steepening synchrotron spectrum, and models its synchrotron peak with synchrotron self-Compton interpretations.

quotes.quote: The bright BL Lac object PKS 2005--489 was observed by Beppo SAX on November 1--2, 1998, following an active X--ray state detected by Rossi XTE. The source, detected between 0.1 and 200 keV, was in a very high state with a continuum well fitted by a steepening spectrum due to synchrotron emission only. Our X--ray spectrum is the flattest ever observed for this source. The different X--ray spectral slopes and fluxes, as measured by various satellites, are consistent with relatively little changes of the peak frequency of the synchrotron emission, always located below 10^ 17 Hz. We discuss these results in the framework of synchrotron self--Compton models.

PKS 2005--489 ( z=0.071 ) is a bright BL Lac object that belongs to the 1 Jy radio catalog (Stickel et al. 1991). Its broad band spectrum peaks in the UV-- soft X--ray bands making it an HBL type of source.

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 2005 489 as a z=0.071 TeV BL Lac with weak Ly emission, apparently narrow line width, high black-hole mass, and a still-standard low-luminosity disc.

quotes.quote: Stocke et al. (2011) recently reported the detection of very weak (EW <1 ) Ly emission lines from three well-known TeV BL Lac Objects: Mkn 421 ( z=0.030 ), PKS 2005 489 ( z=0.071 ) and Mkn 501 ( z=0.0337 ). Since Ly are thought to be produced in the BLR, FWHM of several thousands of km/s are expected (masses of BL Lacs are 10^ 8-9 M_ ). Instead, Stocke et al. (2011) found values for the Ly FWHM of 300, 1050, and 820 km/s, for the three blazars respectively.

Elitzur Ho (2009) suggested that the BLR vanishes (which in turn occurs when the disc is radiatively inefficient) when the disc power is below a critical value: L_ crit = 5 10^ 39 ( M 10^ 7 M_ )^ 2/3 erg/s By considering masses for Mkn 421, Mkn 501, and PKS 2005 489 with values of 4 10^ 8 , 10^ 9 , and 4 10^ 8 M_ , respectively (from Wagner 2008), then the critical disc luminosities for the disappearance of the BLR are 6 10^ 40 , 10^ 41 , and 6 10^ 40 erg/s, respectively. These values are comparable with the luminosities of the Ly lines observed by Stocke et al. (2011), which are: 2.4 10^ 40 , 5.2 10^ 40 , and 2.5 10^ 41 erg/s for Mkn 421, Mkn 501, and PKS 2005 489, respectively. Therefore, since the disc must have a greater luminosity -- say at least one order of magnitude greater taking into account the covering factor (e.g. Netzer 1990) -- to generate such lines, it results that the discs of these three BL Lac Objects are still powerful enough to be considered in the standard regime.

sources.summary: The review describes PKS 2005--489's 2009 high synchrotron state, strong H.E.S.S. detection, TeV variability, and dramatic X-ray spectral changes with only small VHE flux changes.

quotes.quote: PKS 2005--489 ( z=0.071 ): The 2009 observations of this HBL, presented in , were triggered by the particularly high level (historical maximum) of its synchrotron emission at optical and X-ray frequencies. The source was detected at the significance level of 16 after 13 h exposure, with the integrated photon flux F( _ >0.3 TeV ) = 6.7 10^ 12 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and the VHE spectrum best described as a flat power-law continuum _ VHE = 1.3 0.6_ stat 0.2_ sys moderated by the exponential cutoff at _ = 1.3 0.5 TeV. The variability was firmly established in the TeV range on the timescales from days to years. The uniqueness of the source has been revealed by the comparison of the gathered 2009 broad-band dataset with the historical one corresponding to the previous MWL observations in 2004/07. In particular, as shown in Figure f-2005 , the X-ray emission of PKS 2005--489 has changed dramatically during those two epochs, both in spectral shape and in the normalization, while only small-amplitude flux changes have been noted in the VHE range.

sources.summary: The paper divides PKS 2005-489 observations into flux bins, finds no clear spectral variability, and constrains its SED peak in the GeV range.

quotes.quote: All the observations taken on PKS 2005-489 have been divided into two flux bins to ensure sufficient statistics.
PKS 2005-489 does not seem to show spectral variability but here again, statistics are lacking for a firm conclusion.

Three sources have their \ SED peak constrained by the \ and data : Mrk 421, PKS 2005-489 (full data set) and PKS 2155-304 (2008 data set). The peak energy is in the GeV range (Table table:max ). For Mrk 421, this result is obtained during a flaring episode which is not the case of the 2 other sources. Nevertheless, the flux of all the sources is higher than the flux of the other blazars of the sample (Fig Emaxplot ).

sources.summary: The paper reports a large 1998 outburst in PKS 2005-489, a lower 2014 state with similar curvature to 2009, constrained peak energy, and uncertain fitted relation.

quotes.quote: PKS 2005-489 was in a large outburst in 1998, whose peak luminosity reached 10 ^ 45.3 erg \ s ^ 1 . In 2014, it was in a relatively low-flux state with peak luminosity decreasing to 10 ^ 44 erg \ s ^ 1 and the curvature value similar to that of the high-flux state in 2009. Its peak energy ranged between 0.5 and 2.5 keV. Our result based on the 1998 \ data is consistent with that of \ based on the 1998 \ data. Given the large error bars of the fitting result of \ relation, we do not show the best-fit linear model in Figure fig:epb .

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 2005-489 as a nearby BL Lac gamma-ray source whose HESS spectrum shows a power-law component below 2 TeV and excess emission above it.

quotes.quote: PKS 2005-489 HESS observed gamma ray events above 100 GeV from BL Lac object PKS 2005-489 (z = 0.071) in 2004-2007. A summary of their observation reads 1) Below 2 TeV, they obtained the spectrum of a power law fit with a photon index of 3.2. 2) Above 2 TeV (upto 10 TeV), they observed a gamma ray excess relative to the above power law spectrum. It would be interesting to see whether this excess would result in a sharp peak in the multi TeV range upon statistical improvement in the future.

sources.summary: The paper models PKS 2005-489 as an HBL with HESS, XMM-Newton, RXTE, Fermi/LAT, and BeppoSAX data, separating low and high X-ray states.

quotes.quote: PKS 2005-489. A HBL object was detected by HESS in 2003 and 2004 (Aharonian et al. 2005). A significant signal of VHE emission was detected in 2004 with the integral flux above 200 GeV of 6.9 10^ 12 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , corresponding to 2.5 Crab flux. The flux level in 2003 was lower than that measured in 2004, indicating that the activity in 2004 increased, but no significant variability on timescale less than a year was found. The multiwavelength observation by HESS, XMM-Newton and RXTE satellites from 2004 to 2007 was performed (HESS Collaboration 2010a), indicating that the large flux variations at the X-ray band are coupled with weak or no variations in the VHE band.

sources.summary: The paper presents a simultaneous multi-wavelength campaign on the BL Lac object PKS 2005-489, covering TeV, GeV, X-ray, UV, and optical observations and variability studies.

quotes.quote: The high-frequency peaked BL Lac object PKS 2005-489 was the target of a multi-wavelength campaign with simultaneous observations in the TeV ray (H.E.S.S.), GeV ray ( Fermi /LAT), X-ray ( RXTE , Swift ), UV ( Swift ) and optical (ATOM, Swift ) bands. PKS 2005-489 is one of the brightest high-frequency peaked BL Lac objects (HBL) in the southern hemisphere. PKS 2005-489 was detected through the Parkes 2.7 GHz survey Wall1975 and is part of the 1 Jy catalog Kuehr1981 of the brightest extragalactic radio sources.
Hence, for the first time, simultaneous observations have been taken on PKS 2005-489 in the VHE, HE ray, X-ray, UV and optical bands, that can be used for variability and spectral studies.

sources.summary: The paper selects PKS 2005--489 for detailed RXTE/PCA blazar analysis, extracts one flare, finds peak energies below 3 keV, and reports harder-when-brighter X-ray spectra.

quotes.quote: As such, among 32 TeV blazars, we singled out 5 objects (i.e., Mrk 421, Mrk 501, PKS 2155--304, PKS 2005--489, and 1ES 1959+650; hereafter ``the five sources''; see Table table:allta ) and for which RXTE /PCA data allow us to obtain high-quality spectra (detailed analysis of the other 27 TeV blazars will be presented in a future study). Furthermore, the flares were selected with the following criteria: 1) individual flares lasted for several days and were covered by at least two observations in both the rise and decay periods; and 2) the minimum total count rate (summed over available PCUs) in 3--25 keV is above 30 /s. In addition, adjacent outbursts following or followed by those flares were also included. Finally, we picked out 20.5 flares for Mrk 421 (an outburst with observations only in the rise or decay period was considered as 0.5 flare), 7 flares for Mrk 501, 4 flares for PKS 2155--304, and only one flare for both PKS 2005--489 and 1ES 1959+650 from the 16-year data (see Figures fig:spectrum and fig:pfevo for the typical flares of each source; also see the observations annotated with `` '' in Table table:allta .

Combining the 3--25 keV spectral shape information and the synchrotron peak energy obtained by fitting all PCA spectra with the log-parabolic model detailed in Section sec:peakes , we found that: for Mrk 421, the peak energy of all the spectra is below 6 keV, which is consistent with the results in 2004A&A...413..489M , 2004ApJ...601..759T and 2007A&A...466..521T (but 2009A&A...501..879T shows that its peak energy could be up to 30 keV); for Mrk 501, the peak energy of most spectra is above 3 keV, and 2008A&A...478..395M shows that its peak energy could be up to 100 keV; for PKS 2005--489 and PKS 2155--304, the peak energy of spectra is below 3 keV; for 1ES 1959+650, the peak energy of most spectra is below 30 keV.

The X-ray spectra, characterized by , display a harder-when-brighter trend during a number of flares of the five brightest sources (i.e., Mrk 421, Mrk 501, PKS 2155--304, PKS 2005--489, and 1ES 1959+650), which is consistent with previous studies.

sources.summary: The paper gives PKS 2005 489 an individual blazar variability note, summarizing TeV-band detections, GeV or hard-X-ray behavior, and variability classification.

quotes.quote: PKS 2005 489 : This blazar was in an elevated activity state during the first year of the LAT operation as revealed by its TeV, GeV, and hard X-ray light curves. The source was also detected with the H.E.S.S. observatory in this time period Figure fig:1 ; [ 2011A&A...533A.110H . After that, the object went into quiescence for the rest of the observing period, exhibiting no significant variability and was sporadically detected at TeV energies. Overall, it is non-variable in the TeV band based on the estimated value.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 2005-489 as a H.E.S.S.-detected HBL with steady hard Fermi emission; its GeV extrapolation overpredicts TeV flux, implying intrinsic spectral curvature.

quotes.quote: PKS 2005-489: A H.E.S.S.-detected HBL with no evidence of TeV variability. Fermi detects hard steady emission from this source (Figure FIG::pks2005 ). An extrapolation of the GeV spectrum to TeV energies over- predicts the TeV spectrum, suggesting the presence of intrinsic curvature in the spectrum of PKS 2005-489.

sources.summary: PKS 2005--489 showed significant positive color-magnitude correlations overall and in shorter intervals, with distinct spectral states and about 1.5 magnitude flux variation.

quotes.quote: PKS 2005--489: The source was observed during 697 nights in 2007--2012. Significant positive CM correlations are seen in the entire dataset as well as in shorter time intervals, which in addition seem to correspond to distinct spectral states of the blazar (each following trend, though with different regression slopes). About 1.5 mag flux variations corresponded to a relatively narrow, _ BR 3.0-3.5 range covered.

sources.summary: The paper describes 2005 489 as an early H.E.S.S. blazar detection with soft TeV spectrum, elongated TANAMI core, and diffuse southwest jet emission.

quotes.quote: 2005 489: The source was the first blazar independently detected by H.E.S.S. at very high energies, and the second source noted as a VHE emitter in the southern hemisphere 2005A&A...436L..17A . The integral flux is 0.025 of that of the Crab Nebula and has a very soft spectrum with photon index of =4.0 0.4 . X-band TANAMI observations of ojha10 , as well as our new 2.3 GHz image shows an elongated core in the northeast-southwest direction and a diffuse jet emission to the southwest.

gmw_00000531.name: NGC 3938
gmw_00000531.overview: NGC 3938 is an isolated, unbarred Sc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1606.05713, arXiv:1812.03076] located nearly face-on in the Ursa Major Cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/9811391]. It features a multi-armed spiral structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0509708, arXiv:astro-ph/0211538] rich in H II regions [arXiv:astro-ph/9811391] and has hosted multiple observed supernovae [arXiv:1812.03076].

sources.summary: The paper focuses on NGC 3938, a nearly face-on Sc spiral, deriving H-alpha velocity maps, rotation curve, dispersion behavior, distance, arms, and cluster environment.

quotes.quote: The nearly face-on spiral galaxy NGC 3938 has been observed in the H_ line with the TAURUS II Fabry-Perot interferometer at the William Herschel Telescope in order to study the kinematics of the ionized gas. We are able to construct intensity, velocity and velocity dispersion maps for this galaxy. The rotation curve of the galaxy is calculated up to 4.5 radial scale lengths from the galactic centre. The residual velocity field shows very small values with no systematic pattern.

NGC 3938 is a nearly face-on (inclination 14^ ) late type (Sc) spiral galaxy (see figure image1 Based on photographic data of the National Geographic Society -- Palomar Observatory Sky Survey (NGS-POSS) obtained using the Oschin Telescope on Palomar Mountain. It has quite well defined arms and is rich in HII regions. Its distance derived from its corrected radial velocity (848 km/s according to the RC3 catalog) is 11.3 Mpc (using a Hubble constant of 75 km/s/Mpc). The galaxy belongs to the Ursa Major Cluster (see Tully et al. 1996) but has no close companions within 100 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC3938 as a face-on grand-design spiral; its bulge is less star-forming than the arms, and its average SFR is enhanced, possibly environmentally driven.

quotes.quote: The final sample includes 8 objects: NGC0628, NGC3184, NGC3938, NGC4254, NGC4321, NGC4535, NGC5194, NGC5457. Distances, sizes, inclinations and morphologies are those adopted by the DustPedia collaboration, based on the HyperLEDA database The HyperLEDA database is available at http://leda.univ-lyon1.fr/ 2014A&A...570A..13M , and are available on their website (see Tab. tab:sample ). The sample stellar masses are in the range between 10^ 10 10^ 11 M_ , and star formation rates between 1 and 4 M_ yr ^ 1 These sources represent the evolved descendants of high-redshift normal star-forming galaxies, that are rotationally supported systems, at least by z=2 e.g. 2015ApJ...799..209W, 2009ApJ...706.1364F, 2018ApJS..238...21F . Moreover, their regular dynamical properties suggest that they have quiescently assembled their stellar mass through cosmic times.

For example, NGC3938 and NGC4254 show an average enhancement of the SFR, that we interpret as a possible effect of the environment, as these two sources are located in the Ursa Major group and the Virgo cluster, respectively. Other galaxies, in particular NGC0628, show a prominent bulge feature appearing as a narrow distribution at the highest stellar mass densities. Indeed, we already mentioned that at the lowest stellar mass densities (i.e. at large galactocentric distances) in few sources we have identified a cloud of cells deviating from the main relation toward lower SFR, at fixed stellar mass (NGC4321 is the cleanest example). Such distributions are circularly distributed in the outer parts of the galaxies, out of the regions spanned by the dynamical interaction of the spiral arms. It is still to be understood if this is associated to an older population migrated out of the disk, or if it is the remnant of external accretion through, for example, minor merging. Finally, only NGC5457 reveals strong mini-starbursts inside the spiral arms (i.e. regions well elevated above the MS, by a factor larger then 10-100 times at fixed mass density).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3938 as the nearby face-on Sc spiral host of SN 2017ein, notes previous supernovae there, and derives distance estimates from SN II standardization methods.

quotes.quote: SN 2017ein was discovered in NGC 3938 by Ron Arbour on 2017 May 25.97 (UT dates are used throughout this paper), with a magnitude of 17.6 mag in a clear filter. NGC 3938 is a very nearby type Sc, face-on spiral galaxy at a distance of 17 Mpc, which has produced SN 1961U (type II), SN 1964L (type Ic), and SN 2005ay (type II) before SN 2017ein .

The distance to the host galaxy of NGC 3938 (and hence SN 2017ein ) was estimated by three methods . One is based on the standardization of SNe II through an empirical relation between photospheric magnitude and color 2014AJ....148..107R , which is calibrated using SNe II with Cepheid distances. With the observed parameters of the SN IIP exploded in NGC 3938 , SN 2005ay , a distance of 31.75 0.24 mag and 31.70 0.23 mag can be derived for this galaxy, by using the V and I band data respectively. While a smaller value of 31.27 0.13 mag was obtained when using correlation between luminosity and photospheric velocity of SNe IIP 2002ApJ...566L..63H, 2009ApJ...694.1067P .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3938 as a nearly face-on Sc galaxy with prominent outer blue/NUV emission, little radial SFH variation, and symmetric H I kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 3938 NGC 3938 is a nearly face-on and symmetric Sc galaxy. This galaxy has also been observed as part of the SINGS sample and its multiwavelength surface brightness profiles have been documented munozmateos2009 . Similar to NGC 3344, NGC 3938 has prominent B+NUV emission at its edges in the colour image of fig:ngc3938_4panel . The SED-fitting results in Figure fig:ngc3938_sfh show that NGC 3938 does not have much radial variation in SFH.

Prior analysis of neutral gas distribution and kinematics is presented in vanderkruit1982 . The HI (Figure fig:ngc3938_4panel ) does not extend much past the stellar disk and is rather symmetric in its velocity.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 3938 as a nearly face-on Sc galaxy whose measured rotation and velocity dispersion support a multi-armed spiral instability picture.

quotes.quote: NGC 3938 is an Sc galaxy that has an inclination of about 14 , and distance of 11.3 Mpc (Jim enez-Vicente et al. 1999). The left panel of Figure 14 shows the I-band image of NGC 3938 obtained with the Mauna Kea 2.2m telescope (Tully et al. 1996), taken from the NED archive. As can be seen from the Figure, NGC 3938 is a typical multi-armed spiral with the spiral arms branching from its center. The rotation curve and the velocity dispersion of NGC 3938 have been measured, allowing us to build an equilibrium model for this galaxy.

The linear stability analysis shows that the models are the most unstable towards the m=3 and m=4 global modes, which grow with the comparable rates. The superposition of the three-armed and the four-armed spirals of approximately equal amplitudes determines the multi-armed appearance of NGC 3938.

sources.summary: The paper finds moderately faint outer-disk polarized emission in NGC 3938, enhanced interarm regions, an atypical minimum near the approaching major axis, and 10-25 percent polarization.

quotes.quote: NGC 3938 Moderately faint polarized emission is detected in this almost face-on spiral. The polarized emission is concentrated to the outer disk and is enhanced in inter-arm regions. In contrast to other galaxies in our sample, the lowest brightness of polarized emission seem to occur near the approaching rather than the receding major axis (i.e., opposite to PA = 204 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). However, as apparent in the Stokes I imaging braun_etal_2007 , there is also a minimum in the total intensity on the approaching major axis, so that this object does not represent a counter-example to the trend we see in the azimuthal modulation of P . The polarized fraction (where detected) is about 10-25 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3938 as a nearly face-on late-type spiral with weak radio and CO emission, making the RC-CO correlation hard to measure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3938 . For this galaxy the RC contours in the bottom-right panel of Fig. 2 (g) start from 3 , since the RC emission at high resolution is extremely weak, with steps of a factor of 2 . It is a nearly face-on late-type spiral galaxy with well defined arms. It produces very faint radio emission, mostly distributed along the spiral arms (Fig. figura1a ). The CO emission is also faint, so studying the correlation is hard, given the low dynamic range of both emissions, nevertheless the mean value is not far from the mean of the overall sample.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 3938 as an isolated unbarred spiral with shallow metallicity gradients and no clear radial-profile breaks beyond the inner regions.

quotes.quote: The final division of our sample yields two barred isolated galaxies (NGC 2903 5713), three unbarred isolated galaxies (NGC 0628, 3938, 4254), one barred weakly interacting galaxy (NGC 0337 There is some evidence that NGC 0337 might be undergoing an interaction or minor merger. See notes for column 8 in Table tab:venga-subsample . ), and one unbarred weakly interacting galaxy (NGC 5194).
The three isolated unbarred galaxies (NGC 0628, 3938, 4254) also show similarly shallow gradients of -0.44 0.13 in units of dex ( R / ) ^ 1 and -0.034 0.008 in units of dex kpc ^ 1 .
None of our unbarred isolated galaxies (NGC 0628, 3938, 4254) or unbarred interacting galaxy (NGC 5194) show any clear breaks in their radial profiles beyond the inner few kpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3938 as an Sc galaxy with a multi-armed spiral structure arising from unstable m=3 and m=4 disk modes.

quotes.quote: NGC 3938. This Sc galaxy has a multi-armed structure. A linear modal analysis shows that the disk of this galaxy is most unstable toward m = 3 and m = 4 modes. This results in a multi-armed spiral pattern, which is confirmed by our nonlinear simulations.

sources.summary: The atlas notes previous stellar-kinematic work on NGC 3938 and reports a diffuse disk plus spiral-arm radio emission, while its H i does not extend far beyond the stellar disk.

quotes.quote: The stellar kinematics of this galaxy was studied by Bottema ( ) along with that of NGC 3198. We detect a diffuse disk and emission from spiral arms in the radio continuum which are also prominent in H (see the image in Jim enez-Vicente et al ( ). The H i traced in our observations does not extend much beyond the stellar disk.

gmw_00000532.name: NGC 4570
gmw_00000532.overview: NGC 4570 is an edge-on lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1004.2190] that hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1902.10175] and exhibits non-cylindrical bulge rotation [arXiv:1212.2317]. Its central region features a nuclear disk with old, metal-rich stars [arXiv:1004.2190] and two edge-on rings likely formed via bar-driven secular evolution [arXiv:astro-ph/0409625].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4570 as an edge-on S0 whose nuclear disk lies inside the main disk cutoff, has a large mass, old metal-rich stars, and nuclear edge-on rings.

quotes.quote: NGC 4570 is a edge-on disk galaxy classified as S0 by RSA and RC3. It has M_B^0 19 (RC3) at a distance of 12.6 Mpc tully88 . Its nuclear disk was first studied in detail by vdbetal98 . It resides within the inner cutoff of the main stellar disk, whose light contribution to the galaxy surface brightness is negligible within the inner 2'' .

The NSD of NGC 4570 has a mass of 8 10^7 \ (at the adopted distance) as derived from dynamical modelling by vdbems98 . It has a different stellar population than the rest of the bulge, consisting of relatively old ( t 8 Gyr) and metal-rich ( [ Fe / H ] 0.35 ) stars vdbetal98 . These results were derived by comparing the colors measured in WFPC2 images and the line-strength indices measured in Faint Object Spectrograph spectra with the models of single stellar populations by Worth94 . In addiction, NGC 4570 exhibits two nuclear edge-on rings.

sources.summary: The paper presents STIS spectroscopy of NGC 4570, finding regular nuclear-disk rotation, central velocity-dispersion peak, metallicity gradients, and evidence consistent with bar-driven evolution.

quotes.quote: NGC 4570 NGC 4570 is a well-studied galaxy with HST. The main result from previous studies is that the inner region of the galaxy was shaped under the influence of a weak bar 1998MNRAS.298..267V .

The probed region corresponds to the nuclear disk and inner 2 . The velocity curve shows regular rotation peaking at 0 15 from the nucleus. The velocity dispersion steeply rises and peaks in the center.

As can be seen from the Fig. f:ngc4570 , both measured indices show flattening in the central 0 5 ( 60 pc). At larger radii the metallicity drops. Also, the slits positioned on both side of the center measure the smallest metallicity (Table t:lsi ) in the sample and the largest drop in values with respect to the center.

sources.summary: The paper applies its cylindrical-rotation measure to NGC4570, using SAURON kinematics and concluding that the galaxy shows non-cylindrical bulge rotation.

quotes.quote: In order to test the above formulae, we applied it to two real galaxies, NGC7332 and NGC4570. NGC7332 is a well studied galaxy in the literature and shows prominent cylindrical rotation Falconetal2004 . We have used SAURON integral-field kinematics data Emsellemetal2004 for the above two galaxies. The bulge effective radii ( R_ b, 1/2 ) for NGC4570 and NGC7332 were taken to be 14 and 6 arcsec respectively Emsellemetal2004, Fisheretal1994 .

Our analysis indicates that NGC7332 has a high degree of cylindrical rotation( _ CL ^n 0.8 ) within the bulge effective radius. On the other hand, NGC4570 shows non-cylindrical rotation within its bulge effective radius (see Fig. fig:xdeltaCL ).
Using the same formulae, we confirm that NGC4570 is not cylindrically rotating.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4570 as one of six nearby fast-rotating early-type galaxies with new SINFONI-based dynamical modeling and reports a Schwarzschild black-hole mass of (6.8 2.0) 10^7 M_.

quotes.quote: We add six new black hole mass (M _ BH ) measurements of nearby fast rotating early-type galaxies to the known black hole mass sample, namely NGC 584, NGC 2784, NGC 3640, NGC 4570, NGC 4281 and NGC 7049. Our target galaxies have effective velocity dispersions ( _ e ) between 170 and 245 km s ^ 1 , and thus this work provides additional insight into the black hole properties of intermediate-mass early-type galaxies. We combine high-resolution adaptive-optics SINFONI data with large-scale MUSE, VIMOS and SAURON data from ATLAS ^ 3D to derive two-dimensional stellar kinematics maps. We then build both Jeans Anisotropic Models and axisymmetric Schwarzschild models to measure the central black hole masses. Our Schwarzschild models provide black hole masses of (1.3 0.5) 10^8 M_ for NGC 584, (1.0 0.6) 10^8 M_ for NGC 2784, (7.7 5) 10^7 M_ for NGC 3640, (5.4 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 4281, (6.8 2.0) 10^7 M_ for NGC 4570 and (3.2 0.8) 10^8 M_ for NGC 7049 at 3 confidence level, which are consistent with recent M _ BH _ e scaling relations.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4570 as a detailed comparison S0, emphasizing its double-disk and ring structures, smaller bar-related features, and more evolved state relative to NGC 7332.

quotes.quote: A comparison with the edge-on S0 galaxy NGC 4570 appears appropriate here, since it may be at a different stage of a similar (bar-driven) process. NGC 4570 was studied in detail by vdbemsellem98 , who identified similar morphological features (i.e. \ double disk and intermediate boxy structures) to those in NGC 7332. Their study also revealed the same trends in ellipticity and boxiness ( c_4 ). The case for a bar was made there almost exclusively based on the identification of two edge-on rings, exceptionally well characterized and consistent with the locations of bar resonances. The structures observed in NGC 4570 are however one order of magnitude smaller than in NGC 7332: the 'nuclear' disk is less than 1 in radius and the inner ring is at a radius of 1 7 .

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4570 as a strong case for bar-driven secular evolution, linking its ring-like structures, inner disk, colour gradients, and metal enrichment to bar resonances and accretion-fed star formation.

quotes.quote: Although the inner disk of e.g., the Sombrero galaxy qualifies as a secularly evolved structure (Emsellem 1995), one of the best case to date remains the photometric features exhibited by the nearly edge-on S0 galaxy NGC 4570: there is strong evidence that the two ring-like structures and the 100 pc inner disk of that galaxy are the result of bar-driven secular evolution (van den Bosch Emsellem 1998). Numerical simulations by Friedli, Benz Kennicutt (1994) predicted that a radially decreasing initial abundance gradient should evolve due to the presence of a bar, with a strong flattening of the gradient outside cororation, a weakened one inside the bar region and a possible starburst at the very centre. This seems consistent with the observed colour gradients in NGC 4570 along the minor-axis, which retains the original vertical gradient, and along the major-axis which shows a clear correlation with the presumed location of the bars and its resonances (Fig. fig:n4570_1 ).

However, we recently obtained line-strength maps of NGC 4570 within the course of the SAURON survey (Bacon et al. 2001, de Zeeuw et al. 2002, Emsellem et al. 2004), in which we see a direct correspondence between the previously observed structures and the metal enrichment (as probed here by e.g. the Mgb index, Fig. fig:n4570c ). Inner disks with sizes ranging from 100 to 500 pc, such as the one observed in NGC 4570, are quite common in early-type galaxies and could indeed be the result of bar-driven accretion followed by star formation (e.g. see the case of NGC 3115).

gmw_00000533.name: NGC 7332
gmw_00000533.overview: NGC 7332 is an edge-on S0 galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0311078, arXiv:astro-ph/0402225] featuring a boxy bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0311078, arXiv:astro-ph/0407564], a bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0402225, arXiv:astro-ph/0409625], and young stellar populations [arXiv:astro-ph/0311078, arXiv:astro-ph/0402225]. It exhibits complex kinematics with counter-rotating stellar and gaseous components [arXiv:astro-ph/0402225, arXiv:astro-ph/0409625] and is undergoing a tidal interaction with its neighbor, NGC 7339 [arXiv:1009.4438, arXiv:1202.4790].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7332 as an S0 galaxy in a close pair whose debated H i detections, ionized counter-rotating gas, and nearby H i cloud indicate recent accretion or interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 7332 and NGC 7339 form a close pair (5.2 arc minutes separation) with velocities of 1172 and 1313 km s ^ 1 respectively. They lie at a distance of 23 Mpc (Tonry et al. 2001, from surface-brightness fluctuations), giving a projected separation of 35 kpc. NGC 7332 itself is an S0 galaxy; whether or not it contains neutral hydrogen has been debated in the literature for many years. Ionized hydrogen has been detected in NGC 7332 (Plana Boulesteix 1996) which extends over the velocity range of the Knapp et al. neutral hydrogen detection. This ionized gas is organised into two dynamically separate discs, one of which (the more massive) is counter-rotating with respect to the stars.

Morganti et al. (2006) observed NGC 7332 with the Westerbork Synthesis Radio Telescope (WSRT). They did not detect any gas at the position of NGC 7332 but did detect the gas in NGC 7339 and also found a cloud with an H i mass of 6 10^6 M_ around 3 arc minutes from NGC 7332, in the direction of NGC 7339, which they attribute to an interaction between the two galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7332 as an edge-on S0 with barred-disk signatures, counter-rotating gas, complex kinematics, young stellar populations, and evidence for bar-driven processes plus interactions.

quotes.quote: NGC 7332 is an ordinary looking edge-on S0 galaxy (Fig. Fig:sample ) that has been extensively studied in the past. Photometric analysis reveals a boxy bulge, a central disk and evidence for the presence of a `weak` bar (Seifert Scorza 1996; L u tticke et al. 2000). However, the galaxy is mainly known for a bright counter-rotating and a faint co-rotating \ gas component with respect to the stars (Bertola, Buson Zeilinger 1992; Fisher, Illingworth Franx 1994).

NGC 7332 colours are somewhat bluer than those of elliptical galaxies of the same luminosity. Spectral analysis of the central regions reveals a luminosity-weighted age of about 6 Gyr (Vazdekis Arimoto 1999). The \ stellar kinematics (see Fig. Fig:N7332 ) displays a rather smooth velocity field with rotation along the major-axis and a weak dependence of rotation on galactic height.

The analysis of the absorption line-strengths reveal that NGC 7332's stellar populations are generally young ( 5 2 Gyr), not only in the disk but also in the bulge, in agreement with previous studies (Balcells Peletier 1994; Vazdekis et al. 1996; Terlevich Forbes 2002).

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7332 as an evolving edge-on S0 with a bar, inner disk, counter-rotating stellar and gas components, young stellar populations, and likely interaction-driven evolution.

quotes.quote: We present \ integral-field observations of the S0 galaxy NGC 7332. Existing broad-band ground-based and HST photometry reveals a double disk structure and a boxy bulge interpreted as a bar viewed close to edge-on. The \ two-dimensional stellar kinematic maps confirm the existence of the bar and inner disk but also uncover the presence of a cold counter-rotating stellar component within the central 250 pc. The \ and \ emission line maps show that the ionised gas has a complex morphology and kinematics, including both a component counter-rotating with respect to the stars and a fainter co-rotating one.

Analysis of the absorption line-strength maps show that NGC 7332 is young everywhere. The presence of a large-scale bar can explain most of those properties, but the fact that we see a significant amount of unsettled gas, together with a few peculiar features in the maps, suggest that NGC 7332 is still evolving. Interactions as well as bar-driven processes must thus have played an important role in the formation and evolution of NGC 7332, and presumably of S0 galaxies in general.

sources.summary: NGC 7332 is used as a directly discussed bulge-spectrum example, with fitted central spectra, detected [O iii] emission, outer-bulge line emission, and Mg2 agreement with most prior measurements.

quotes.quote: Figure fitemplate illustrates the result of this procedure for two galaxies (NGC 7332 and UGC 10043), showing, in the upper panels, the fits between the observed central spectra of the galaxies and the corresponding optimal templates corrected with the derived dynamical parameters. The lower panels show the residual of the fits. Note that emission lines, although almost hidden in the original spectrum (like the 5007 line for NGC 7332) are clearly detected after subtracting the template (once an emission line is detected, the corresponding wavelength region is not used for the minimization of the residuals).

However, in some special cases (e.g., NGC 1886 and NGC 7332), line emission can be detected even in the outer part of the bulge, where any contamination by disk light is negligible. For instance, in NGC 7332, the line emission increases to EW( [ O iii ] 5007) 4 \ at a distance of 16 arcsec from the centre.

However, the fact that, when there are several published values for a galaxy (NGC 7332), our measurements agree with the majority of them gives us confidence in our measured values. For instance, for the 5 previous measurements for the central Mg _2 index in NGC 7332, our value agrees within the errors with 4 out of them.

sources.summary: The paper uses SAURON data for NGC7332 to test a cylindrical-rotation criterion, finding a high degree of cylindrical rotation within its bulge effective radius.

quotes.quote: In order to test the above formulae, we applied it to two real galaxies, NGC7332 and NGC4570. NGC7332 is a well studied galaxy in the literature and shows prominent cylindrical rotation Falconetal2004 . We have used SAURON integral-field kinematics data Emsellemetal2004 for the above two galaxies. The bulge effective radii ( R_ b, 1/2 ) for NGC4570 and NGC7332 were taken to be 14 and 6 arcsec respectively Emsellemetal2004, Fisheretal1994 . Our analysis indicates that NGC7332 has a high degree of cylindrical rotation( _ CL ^n 0.8 ) within the bulge effective radius.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7332 as showing externally acquired counter-rotating gas, strong bar evidence, centrally fueled gas, and a compact counter-rotating stellar disk from a past accretion episode.

quotes.quote: This is now clearly witnessed in NGC 7332, for which two-dimensional SAURON spectrography has been obtained (Falc on-Barroso et al. 2004). The ionized gas distribution and kinematics does not leave any doubt on the external origin of part of the dissipative component which is counter-rotating with respect to the stars. Evidences for the presence of a strong bar are also very strong: e.g. a boxy bulge, a cylindrical stellar velocity field. An hybrid scenario emerges in this case where the interaction with a companion provides the gas which is then fueled to the central inner 50 pc with the help of a bar (the formation of which could have been triggered by the interaction). It is finally important to note that the central stellar kinematics shows the presence of a counter-rotating stellar disk, less than 100 pc in diameter, a possible remnant of a past accretion episode.

sources.summary: The survey identifies NGC 7332 as a Fundamental Plane outlier with young populations, rich kinematic substructure, and likely recent interaction with NGC 7339.

quotes.quote: NGC 7332 was already recognised as an outlier of the FP in the optical and near-IR bands by . Although it shows signs of widespread young populations, it is not at the level of other early-type galaxies. Nevertheless, this object is peculiar in that it is rich in kinematic substructure fb04 and might have suffered from a recent interaction with NGC 7339. Moreover, the presence of two gaseous counter-rotating discs pb96 suggests that this galaxy might not be in dynamical equilibrium.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 7332 as a barred S0 counter-rotator lacking direct VLA H I, but likely interacting through a common H I envelope with NGC 7339.

quotes.quote: In NGC 7332, we do not find any directly-associated H i .
NGC 7332 also appears to be in the merging process with its neighbour through the common H i envelope.
These facts strongly suggest a tidal interaction between NGC 7332 and NGC 7339.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC7332 as an S0 galaxy with a boxy bulge, bar-like stellar kinematics, and mostly homogeneous stellar populations across disk and bar/bulge components.

quotes.quote: The best examples are NGC7332 (Falc o n-Barroso et al. \ 2004) and NGC4526 (Fig. fig:sauron ; Emsellem et al. \ 2004; Sarzi et al. \ 2005; Kuntschner et al. \ 2005). Both S0 galaxies have a boxy bulge and clearly show the stellar kinematic signatures of a bar. They also possess homogeneous stellar populations (age and metallicity) across the disk and bar/bulge components, except in the central parts, as expected from simple bar-driven evolution models (e.g. \ Friedli Benz 1995).

gmw_00000534.name: KISSR 1219
gmw_00000534.overview: KISSR 1219 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy [arXiv:1708.00618, arXiv:1807.00979] that exhibits double-peaked optical emission lines [arXiv:1708.00618]. This spectral feature is driven by a one-sided parsec-scale radio jet [arXiv:1508.07429, arXiv:1807.00979] that pushes and shocks surrounding emission-line clouds [arXiv:1708.00618, arXiv:1807.00979].

sources.summary: The paper presents KISSR 1219 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy whose double-peaked lines are best explained by a one-sided radio outflow pushing emission-line clouds rather than by dual radio cores.

quotes.quote: We present the results from 1.5 and 5 GHz phase-referenced VLBA and 1.5 GHz Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) observations of the Seyfert 2 galaxy KISSR 1219, which exhibits double peaked emission lines in its optical spectrum. The VLA and VLBA data reveal a one-sided core-jet structure at roughly the same position angles, providing evidence of an AGN outflow. The absence of dual parsec-scale radio cores puts the binary black hole picture in doubt for the case of KISSR 1219. The high brightness temperatures of the parsec-scale core and jet components ( >10^6 K) are consistent with this interpretation. Doppler boosting with jet speeds of 0.55c to 0.25c , going from parsec- to kpc-scales, at a jet inclination 50 can explain the jet one-sidedness in this Seyfert 2 galaxy.

A blue-shifted broad emission line component in [ O iii ] is also indicative of an outflow in the emission line gas at a velocity of 350 km s ^ 1 , while the [ O i ] doublet lines suggest the presence of shock-heated gas. A detailed line ratio study using the MAPPINGS III code further suggests that a shock+precursor model can explain the line ionization data well. Overall, our data suggest that the radio outflow in KISSR 1219 is pushing the emission line clouds, both ahead of the jet and in a lateral direction, giving rise to the double peak emission line spectra.

sources.summary: The paper finds a one-sided parsec-scale jet in KISSR 1219 and argues its double-peaked emission lines likely arise from AGN outflow interaction.

quotes.quote: KISSR 1219: VLBI observations of the second Seyfert galaxy, KISSR 1219 ( z=0.0375 ), took place in February 2015. A clear 100 milliarcsecond ( 70 parsec) one-sided core-jet structure is revealed in our preliminary 1.6 GHz image (Kharb et al 2016, in preparation). The core but not the jet is detected at 5 GHz, indicating that the jet has a steep radio spectrum.

Interestingly, this parsec-scale jet is pointing directly towards some lobe-like radio emission about 50 kiloparsec away, as seen in its VLA FIRST image (see Figure 2). If the parsec-scale and the kiloparsec-scale emission are connected, KISSR 1219 will be the fourth only Seyfert galaxy with a large-scale jet ejected from a spiral galaxy see Bagchi14 . New Expanded VLA observations of KISSR 1219 are currently being analysed to search for this connection. Like in KISSR 1494, there is an indication that the double peaked emission lines in KISSR 1219 are a result of an AGN outflow impacting the emission line clouds.

sources.summary: KISSR 1219 is described as a Seyfert double-peaked AGN with a one-sided steep-spectrum 70 pc radio jet that decelerates from parsec to kiloparsec scales while driving NLR clouds.

quotes.quote: Similar dual-frequency phase-referenced VLBA observations of the Seyfert DPAGN KISSR 1219, have revealed a one-sided 70 parsec radio jet with a steep radio spectrum 1.0 0.2 ; Kharb17a . Using the jet-to-counterjet surface brightness ratio in KISSR 1219 and an orientation angle consistent with its Seyfert type 2 classification ( 50^ based on the typical dusty torus half opening angle), we concluded that the jet speed had decreased from 0.55c on parsec-scales to 0.25c on kpc-scales. The radio jet was likely pushing the NLR clouds in opposite directions, giving rise to the double-peaked emission lines. This may have resulted in jet deceleration and eventual dissipation.

gmw_00000535.name: J0006+1215
gmw_00000535.overview: J0006+1215 is a radio-quiet, heavily reddened quasar at a redshift of approximately 2.32 [arXiv:1701.04501, arXiv:2203.06203]. It features a 100-kiloparsec Lyman-alpha halo [arXiv:2303.12835], spatially extended continuum and [O III] emissions [arXiv:2312.03917], and emission-line outflows driven by its active nucleus [arXiv:1701.04501, arXiv:2203.06203].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes a heavily reddened quasar whose dusty torus reveals blueshifted emission-line outflows, constraining their physical state, distance, and likely scattering origin.

quotes.quote: We present detailed analysis of a heavily reddened quasar, SDSS J000610.67+121501.2, whose normal ultraviolet (UV) broad emission lines (BELs) are heavily suppressed by the Dusty Torus as a natural ``Coronagraph'', thus the blueshifted BELs (BBELs) can be reliably measured. The physical properties of the emission-line outflows are derived as follows: ionization parameter U 10^ 0.5 , column density N_ H 10^ 22.0 cm ^ 2 , covering fraction of 0.1 and upper limit density of n_ H 10^ 5.8 cm ^ 3 . The outflow gases are located at least 41 pc away from the central engine, which suggests that they have expanded to the scale of the dust torus or beyond.

In this work, we report such a infrared-luminous quasar J000610.67+121501.2 (hereafter 0006), at z=2.3174 with outflows revealed in , , and \ BBELs. 0006 \ is an infrared-luminous quasar with faint ultraviolet (UV) emission. It was first imaged by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS; York et al. 2000) at the five optical wide-bands.

The multiwavelength SED analysis indicates that the continuum of 0006 \ is heavily suppressed by dust reddening with E(B-V)=0.7512 0.0049 , reducing the high luminosity of L_ bol =2.63 10^ 47   erg s^ 1 to M_i=24.17 , and the detected UV radiation comes from scattering. In the optical and NIR spectra, the normal BELR-originated emission lines, and the continuum, are obscured, and, thus, the emission-line outflows are significantly presented, even the emission profiles of , \ and \ are totally dominated by the blueshifted components, suggestive of the AGN outflows.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J000610.67+121501.2 as the reddest known ERQ at z=2.3184, with extreme [O III] kinematics, strong UV suppression, high luminosity, and radio-quiet compact emission.

quotes.quote: In this paper we report results on the reddest of all known ERQs, SDSS J000610.67+121501.2, hereafter J0006+1215. It has a rest-frame UV to mid-IR colour of i-W3=8.01 AB mag, an emission redshift of z_ em =2.3184 , and is radio quiet.

J0006+1215 Selection and Basic Properties For this first wide-field integral-field study of an ERQ, we choose the reddest among all known ERQs, with i-W3=8.01 , which also has among the most blueshifted and broadest [ O III ] lines among ERQs. As measured by in a one-dimensional spectrum, the velocity shift that encompasses 98 per cent of its [ O III ] 5007 emission-line flux integrating from the red side v_ 98, OIII =-6092 km s ^ 1 , and the velocity width encompassing 90 per cent of the [ O III ] line power w_ 90, OIII =6206 km s ^ 1 .

J0006+1215 is suppressed by about six magnitudes in the rest-frame UV, and we estimate its intrinsic bolometric luminosity from mid-IR photometry assuming a standard Type I spectral energy distribution. We apply a bolometric correction factor of 8 to the rest-frame 5 m luminosity extrapolated from the W 3 band photometry, with details in . J0006+1215 has an intrinsic bolometric luminosity of L_ bol =10^ 47.9 erg s ^ 1 , and is near the high end of the ERQ population. The radio luminosity of J0006+1215 is in the quiet regime, and is unresolved on 10 kpc scale Hwang+18 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J0006+1215 as a strongly detected extremely red quasar whose XMM spectrum is fitted by an absorbed power law with significant intrinsic column density.

quotes.quote: Specifically, SDSS J0006+1215 and SDSS J2129 0018 are the two most strongly detected objects with sufficient counts to enable spectral analysis.

In Figure pic:xray we show the X-ray spectra of two sources, SDSS J0006+1215 and SDSS J2129 0018, which have > 100 detected counts in their XMM data, sufficient to perform a spectral fit. For both sources, we fit a power-law model combined with foreground Galactic absorption determined from star92 and a rest-frame absorber which is intrinsic to the source.

For SDSS J0006+1215 and SDSS J2129 0018, we find that an absorbed power-law produces a reasonable fit with C-statistic 181.5 and 134.9 for 208 and 159 degrees of freedom, respectively. Hence, based on the available data there is no statistical evidence for requiring a more complex model. The best-fit model parameters for SDSS J0006+1215 are 1.74 0.07 and N_ H (5.8 0.7) 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 . This is consistent with the value of N_ H of (6 2) 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 predicted from using HR _1 .

sources.summary: The paper finds J0006+1215 has very extended continuum and [O III] emission, spatially resolved continuum detectable to the field edge, and narrow-dominated residual emission after PSF subtraction.

quotes.quote: For J0006+1215, the OSIRIS narrowband images in Fig. fig:nbimgsintspec reveal very extended continuum and [ O III ] emissions.

The continuum emissions of J0006+1215, J1232+0912, J1217+0234, and J1604+5633 are spatially resolved, although we caution the suboptimal weather of the J1604+5633 observations. In particular the continuum emission of J0006+1215 remains detectable out to edge of the field at 1.4 arcsec.

With the PSF subtraction method we reveal spatially resolved [ O III ] emission in J0006+1215, J0209+3122, J1031+2903, J1217+0234, J1550+0806, J1652+1728, and J2323 0100, totalling seven out of 10 ERQs. In J0006+1215, J0209+3122, J1031+2903, and J1217+0234, the residual emission is dominated by a narrow component.

sources.summary: J0006+1215 is the reddest ERQ, with a fast [O III] outflow, 100 kpc quiet Ly halo, inner-halo emission spike, and unusual He II/Ly conditions.

quotes.quote: Our team already analyzed one Ly halo from our sample that is the reddest known ERQ (J0006+1215, in Lau+22 ), which displays an extremely fast [ O III ] 5007 outflow at 6000 km s ^ 1 Perrotta+19 . The Ly halo spans 100 kpc, and the narrow Ly emission spike in the quasar spectrum originates from the inner halo. It is kinematically quiet, with velocity dispersion of 300 km s ^ 1 and no broadening above the dark matter circular velocity down to the spatially resolved limit 6 kpc from the quasar.

Lau+22 , hereafter L22, proposed that the He II 1640/Ly ratio of the inner halo and the asymmetry level of the overall halo in J0006+1215 are dissimilar to Type-II quasars, suggesting unique physical conditions for J0006+1215 that are beyond orientation differences from other quasar populations.

gmw_00000536.name: J1217+0234
gmw_00000536.overview: J1217+0234 is a galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2312.03917]. Observations reveal spatially resolved, clumpy, and disturbed continuum and [O III] emissions, with the residual [O III] emission dominated by a narrow component [arXiv:2312.03917].

sources.summary: The paper reports extended continuum around J1217+0234, clumpy disturbed OSIRIS emission, spatially resolved continuum, and narrow-dominated residual [O III] emission after PSF subtraction.

quotes.quote: The HST image reveals extended continuum emission around J1217+0234 detectable out to 3 arcsec. In our OSIRIS images images we likewise find spatially-resolved, clumpy, disturbed continuum and [ O III ] emissions, although the detectable extent is smaller due to lower surface brightness sensitivity.
The continuum emissions of J0006+1215, J1232+0912, J1217+0234, and J1604+5633 are spatially resolved, although we caution the suboptimal weather of the J1604+5633 observations.

With the PSF subtraction method we reveal spatially resolved [ O III ] emission in J0006+1215, J0209+3122, J1031+2903, J1217+0234, J1550+0806, J1652+1728, and J2323 0100, totalling seven out of 10 ERQs. In J0006+1215, J0209+3122, J1031+2903, and J1217+0234, the residual emission is dominated by a narrow component.

gmw_00000537.name: J1550+0806
gmw_00000537.overview: J1550+0806 is classified as an extremely red quasar-like object due to its extreme emission-line properties [arXiv:2312.03917]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus wind with a spatially resolved, compact [O III] outflow component [arXiv:2312.03917].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1550+0806 as ERQ-like and finds one of its [O III] outflow components is spatially resolved, with residual emission dominated by the lowest-dispersion outflow component.

quotes.quote: All 10 sources are classified as ERQs with i-W3>4.6 except J1550+0806, which is listed as ``ERQ-like'' by based on extreme emission-line properties like ERQs but with a less extreme red i-W3 colour.

Specifically, among all [ O III ] components of the sample that meet the AGN-outflow cut, only five out of 16 of them are spatially resolved, namely the J1550+0806 component 2, the J1652+1728 components 1 and 2, and the J2323 0100 components 1 and 2. These five components are spatially compact with maximum radii 4 kpc.
In J1550+0806, the residual emission is dominated by a component that has the lowest velocity dispersion of all ERQ fitted components that meet the AGN-outflow cut.

gmw_00000538.name: J1652+1728
gmw_00000538.overview: J1652+1728 is an extremely red, highly obscured quasar at redshift z=2.94 [arXiv:2306.06303, arXiv:2307.13751] hosted by an ultramassive galaxy [arXiv:2404.14475]. The system is undergoing a major merger with multiple companion galaxies [arXiv:2303.06970], exhibiting a western tidal tail [arXiv:1907.10617, arXiv:2101.04688], clumpy starburst activity [arXiv:2303.06970, arXiv:2404.14475], and galactic-scale outflows [arXiv:2306.06303, arXiv:2307.13751].

sources.summary: The paper flags SDSS J1652+1728 as an ERQ major-merger candidate after excluding a foreground star and identifying multiple faint companions plus a long tidal tail.

quotes.quote: In addition to the major merger candidates selected due to the presence of a companion, we find an additional candidate -- ERQ SDSS J1652+1728. The analysis of this target is complicated by the presence of a bright compact source just North of our object (Figure pic:tidal ); from the combination of optical colours from the SDSS, our derived HST near-infrared colours and Keck spectroscopy alex18 which shows a featureless spectrum, we conclude that this object is most likely a foreground star. Excluding this chance superposition from consideration, we find that SDSS J1652+1728 has multiple faint companions within 2 \ - which could well be giant clumps of star formation characteristic of high-redshift star-forming galaxies elme07, fors09 - and a pronounced tidal tail extending over tens of kpc, marked with an arrow in Figure pic:tidal . Therefore, we flag this object as a major merger candidate even though it does not meet our formal companion-based definition.

Three of these four -- SDSS J1232+0912, SDSS J1515+1757 and SDSS J1652+1728 -- show high continuum polarization in the optical (rest-frame UV), between 10 and 15 , while the remaining source SDSS J2215 0056 is too weak in the continuum to reliably measure polarization. While these values are not at the theoretical maximum for polarization of scattered light, they are at the high end of polarization for the obscured quasar population hine93, smit02a, zaka05, gree12 . Therefore, if these three sources are representative of the entire HST sample, then in principle all of the optical (rest-frame UV) emission can be due to scattered light. As the wavelength increases, the scattered light contribution decreases while the host galaxy becomes more important, so it is not clear what fraction of the flux and on what scales the scattered light contributes to our WFC3 images.

sources.summary: SDSS J1652+1728 is a Compton-thick luminous ERQ with an extended host and tidal feature; IFS observations detect narrow and broad emission, with the broader component associated with an outflow.

quotes.quote: X-ray observations goul18a,Ishikawa21 show that SDSS 1652+1728 is a Compton thick, intrinsically luminous quasar with a column density of 1 10^ 24 cm^ 2 and X-ray luminosity L_ 2-10 keV =1.27 10^ 45 . HST observations zaka19 in the rest-frame B band show an extended host galaxy with multiple clumps or companions and an extended low surface brightness feature directed to the West, which may be a tidal tail. The host luminosity is well above the median for the ERQs and is 10 L^* for galaxies at a similar redshift.

After performing PSF-subtraction on the IFS observations, we detected extended emission towards the south-west, consisting of both narrow and a broad emission line (Figure fig:1652 ). The spectra along with the fits are shown in Figure fig:spec_SDSS1652 . We compare the distribution and morphology of the extended narrow and broad emission in Figure fig:SDSS1652_broad_n_narrow by constructing \ emission line maps for each component that we fit in Figure fig:spec_SDSS1652 . The broader emission is associated with the outflow and is more extended towards the south, while the narrow emission extending towards the west is either gas within the host galaxy or a tidal tail feature associated with the extended emission seen in the HST observations.

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J1652 as a high-redshift obscured extremely red quasar with strong polarization, heavy X-ray obscuration, and galaxy-scale outflows.

quotes.quote: Our prototypical target is SDSS J165202.64+172852.3, hereafter SDSS J1652, an ERQ ross15, hama17 at z=2.9 originally selected by its high infrared-to-optical ratio. When classified based on its UV and optical emission-line properties, it is a high-redshift type 1.8 2 (obscured) quasar candidate, with a high equivalent width of CIV 1550 \ and a high [ OIII ] 5008 /H ratio alex18 . The width of its CIV emission line (full width at half maximum =2400 km s ^ 1 ) places it just above the standard cutoff ( <2000 km s ^ 1 ) for type 2 quasar selection alex13 , and there is a weak broad component in H alex18 and a stronger broad component in H vayn23c . X-ray observations confirm that the source is highly obscured, with a column density between the observer and the X-ray emitting corona of N_H 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 goul18a, ishi21 .

As other ERQs, SDSS J1652 shows a blueshifted and broad (velocity width containing 80 of line power w_ 80 =1760 km s ^ 1 ; alex18 ) [ OIII ] 5008 \ emission line indicative of outflow activity on scales 100 pc where this line cannot get collisionally de-excited. The object was therefore observed by James Webb Space Telescope in its first weeks of science operations as part of an Early Release Science program (``Q-3D'', PI: Wylezalek) to investigate quasars with strong galaxy-scale outflows. High-velocity (several hundred km s ^ 1 ) outflows in this source have now been detected over the entire extent of the galaxy vayn21b, wyle22, vayn23b, vayn23c .

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSSJ1652+1728/J1652 as a z=2.94 extremely red quasar, using JWST/NIRSpec to map a fast ionized outflow across its host galaxy.

quotes.quote: We report observations of a powerful galactic-scale outflow in a z=3 extremely red, intrinsically luminous ( L_ bol 5 10^ 47 ) quasar SDSSJ1652+1728 with the Near Infrared Spectrograph (NIRSpec) on board JWST.
This paper presents a detailed emission-line study of the warm ionized outflow in SDSSJ1652+1728 (hereafter J1652).

In this paper, we present JWST NIRSpec IFU observations of J1652, a powerful extremely red quasar at redshift at z=2.94 . J1652 belongs to the population of extremely red quasars, which are selected from optical and infrared surveys based on their colors and rest-frame ultraviolet emission line properties ross15, hama17 . Extremely red quasars contain some of the fastest moving outflows of any quasar sample to date perr19 among the obscured quasar population. In this paper, we map the location of the fast-moving outflow across the quasar host galaxy using optical emission lines redshifted into the near-infrared at kpc scale resolution and we find: We detect the ionized outflow out to 10 kpc away from the quasar with outflow velocities ranging from 500-1400 \ with a generally decreasing trend as a function of projected radial distance from the quasar. We measure average instantaneous outflow rates of 0.1-100 \ per spaxel and a total integrated outflow rate of 2300 .

sources.summary: JWST observations show this z=2.94 extremely red quasar host undergoing merger activity, clumpy star formation, powerful outflows, shocks, and high-density low-metallicity star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: Here we present an emission-line study of an extremely red quasar SDSSJ165202.64+172852.3 host galaxy at z=2.94 , based on observations with the Near Infrared Spectrograph (NIRSpec) integral field unit (IFU) on board JWST. We use standard emission-line diagnostic ratios to map the sources of gas ionization across the host and a swarm of companion galaxies. Finally, the kinematically narrow emission associated with the host galaxy presents as a collection of 1 kpc-scale clumps forming stars at a rate of at least 200 M_ yr ^ 1 .

SDSS J165202.64+172852.3 (J1652 hereafter) is one such ERQ at z = 2.94 with i - W3=5.4 AB mag. Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ) observations with Wide Field Camera 3 (WFC3), corresponding to the rest-frame B band, suggest that the quasar is hosted by a massive galaxy with M_*/M_ 11.4 - 12.4 which is undergoing a major merger and exhibits a tidal feature in the western direction zaka19 . We are catching the SDSSJ1652 system at a unique time, with ongoing merger activity and quasar-driven outflows that drive shocks and turbulence in the ISM with ongoing vigorous star formation in clumpy star-forming regions across the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports J1652+1728 has extended HST continuum, three fitted [O III] components, spatially resolved outflow components, and a resolved narrow component below the outflow threshold.

quotes.quote: The HST image finds extended continuum emission around J1652+1728 out to 6 arcsec. In the NIFS data extended continuum emission is not detected.
All ERQs but J1652+1728 are fitted with two [ O III ] components while J1652+1728 is fitted with three.

Specifically, among all [ O III ] components of the sample that meet the AGN-outflow cut, only five out of 16 of them are spatially resolved, namely the J1550+0806 component 2, the J1652+1728 components 1 and 2, and the J2323 0100 components 1 and 2. These five components are spatially compact with maximum radii 4 kpc. On the contrary, all five components of the sample that are below the AGN-outflow cut are spatially resolved, namely the component 2 of J0006+1215, J0209+3122, J1031+2903, J1217+0234 and the component 3 of J1652+1728.

sources.summary: The paper studies J1652 as an extremely red z=2.94 quasar with outflows, a clumpy starburst, an ultramassive host, and black-hole mass interpretation sensitive to assumptions.

quotes.quote: Pasadena, CA 91125, USA Max-Planck-Institut für Extraterrestrische Physik, Giessenbachstrasse 1, D-85748 Garching, Germany CAS Key Laboratory for Research in Galaxies and Cosmology, Department of Astronomy, University of Science and Technology of China, Hefei, Anhui 230026, China School of Astronomy and Space Sciences, University of Science and Technology of China, Hefei 230026, China Received XXX; accepted YYY We present JWST/MIRI and JWST/NIRSpec observations of the extremely red quasar SDSS J165202.64+172852.3 at z = 2.94 (dubbed J1652), which is one of the most luminous quasars known to date, driving powerful outflows and hosting a clumpy starburst, in the midst of several interacting companions.

We broadly constrain the stellar mass of J1652 via SED fitting, which suggests the host to be extremely massive at 10^ 12.8 0.5 M_ falling about 2 dex above the characteristic mass of the Schechter fit to the z=3 stellar mass function. Notably, J1652's central black hole might be interpreted as being either undermassive, overmassive, or in line with the BH mass - stellar mass relation, depending on the choice of assumptions.

Based on UV-to-IR SED fitting with the CIGALE code Boquien2019 , we retrieve a stellar mass of 10^ 12.8 0.5 \ M_ , which places J1652 at the extreme end of the z=3 galaxy stellar mass function. The uncertainties are large when fitting a strongly quasar-contaminated SED at high redshift. In the context of the black hole mass - stellar mass relation, we caution that the choice of line, kinematic component(s) and other assumptions as well as the uncertainties in the stellar mass can lead to assessing the black hole in J1652 as overmassive, undermassive or typical for its host galaxy stellar mass.

sources.summary: J1652+1728 shows a blueshifted central Ly halo relative to its outer halo, uncertain quasar subtraction, possible two-component halo structure, and kpc-scale outflows.

quotes.quote: For J1652+1728, the Gaussian fit captures the line width for approximating dispersion, but the narrow Ly halo emission in the central region is blueshifted relative the outer halo, blueshifting the total spatially-integrated spectrum. This centrally concentrated blueshifting, which is seen in J1652+1728's 1st moment velocity map, is also seen in the Ly emission profile from the central arcsec aperture in Figure fig:fig_psf_template fig:fig_annular_spec . Blueshifted central emission causes uncertainties in what to clip as halo emission when subtracting the quasar.
This boundary could be evidence of a 2-component halo structure of compact and extended gas (e.g., J1145+5742 and J1652+1728).
This is further supported in multi-component JWST observations of J1652+1728, which found complex gas kinematics and outflows on kpc scales Vayner+23b .

gmw_00000539.name: J2323 0100
gmw_00000539.overview: J2323 0100 is an extremely red quasar at a redshift of 2.3695 [arXiv:2101.04688]. It is a radio-quiet source [arXiv:2101.04688] undergoing a face-on major merger with a second galactic nucleus [arXiv:2101.04688, arXiv:2312.03917], which exhibits spatially resolved active galactic nucleus outflows [arXiv:2312.03917].

sources.summary: SDSS J2323 0100 is an ERQ in a face-on major merger; OSIRIS observations reveal marginally resolved host-galaxy emission and broad/intermediate line components.

quotes.quote: SDSS J2323 0100 (z=2.3695) Like SDSS J1652+1728, SDSS J2323 0100 was selected as an ERQ ross15, hama17 . A noteworthy feature of its rest-frame UV spectrum from the SDSS zaka16b is a high apparent \ 1240 /Ly ratio, even higher than that seen in SDSS J1652+1728 alex18 ; this feature may be due to outflows in the broad-line region wherein the high-velocity \ troughs may be blocking Ly hama17 . SDSS J2323 0100 was among the first objects we observed with NIR (rest-frame optical) spectroscopy, revealing some of the most extreme kinematics of the \ emission known, with FWHM=2625 \ zaka16b, perr19 . HST observations of this object reveal a face-on major merger between two galaxies, with nuclei separated by 1.3 =11 kpc and the SDSS position of the quasar consistent with the south-western of the two galactic nuclei. Due to the lack of previous integral-field or spatially resolved spectroscopy, it was unknown whether the second nucleus hosts any activity. The source is not detected in the radio down to 0.03 mJy and is classified as radio-quiet hwan18 .

PSF-subtracted OSIRIS observations reveal a marginally spatially resolved emission in \ and \ associated with the galaxy hosting the luminous quasar. The \ emission line profile is consistent with a single broad component, while the \ emission line has both a broad and and an intermediate velocity (1267 54 ) component. The difference between the \ and \ profiles within our observations likely due to insufficient wavelength coverage near the \ emission lines to perform accurate emission-line and continuum fitting.

sources.summary: The paper describes J2323-0100, with J1232+0912, as an extreme core-ERQ whose strong NV and large CIV/HeII ratios imply dense and/or high-metallicity gas.

quotes.quote: J1232+0912 and J2323-0100. Their line ratios are rather extreme. NV is very strong compared with QSO2: NV/CIV 2 and NV/HeII 10, while QSO2 show in general NV/CIV < 1 and NV/HeII < 10. CIV/CIII ] 5 is also at the high end of the range of values spanned by QSO2. There are no optimum models within the parameter space explored by that can explain all line ratios simultaneously. On the other hand, the high NV ratios and large CIV/HeII strongly suggest high densities n> 10 ^6 cm ^ 3 and/or high metallicities Z 4 Z_ .

sources.summary: The paper describes J2323 0100 as an ERQ in an ongoing major merger, with a second nucleus, spatially resolved outflow components, and residual [O III] from both outflow components.

quotes.quote: The HST image reveals that the ERQ J2323 0100 is in an ongoing major merger with a second galactic nucleus 1.3 arcsec away in the north-northeast direction. The second nucleus is discernible in the OSIRIS continuum image at the expected position.

Specifically, among all [ O III ] components of the sample that meet the AGN-outflow cut, only five out of 16 of them are spatially resolved, namely the J1550+0806 component 2, the J1652+1728 components 1 and 2, and the J2323 0100 components 1 and 2. These five components are spatially compact with maximum radii 4 kpc.

In J2323 0100, where we detect residual emission contributed by both components 1 and 2 which both meet the AGN-outflow cut, we have the best corrected angular resolution and the deepest observations.

gmw_00000540.name: J1031+2903
gmw_00000540.overview: J1031+2903 is an extremely red quasar that exhibits spatially resolved [O III] emission [arXiv:2312.03917]. This residual emission is dominated by a narrow component whose surface brightness radial profile suggests a clumpy interstellar medium [arXiv:2312.03917].

sources.summary: The paper finds J1031+2903 has a spatially resolved narrow component whose surface-brightness profile suggests clumpy interstellar media, and narrow-dominated residual [O III] emission after PSF subtraction.

quotes.quote: On the contrary, all five components of the sample that are below the AGN-outflow cut are spatially resolved, namely the component 2 of J0006+1215, J0209+3122, J1031+2903, J1217+0234 and the component 3 of J1652+1728. The narrow component 2 of J1031+2903 is not centrally concentrated but displays a local dip in its surface brightness radial profile at (0.1 0.2) arcsec, implying clumpiness in the interstellar media.

With the PSF subtraction method we reveal spatially resolved [ O III ] emission in J0006+1215, J0209+3122, J1031+2903, J1217+0234, J1550+0806, J1652+1728, and J2323 0100, totalling seven out of 10 ERQs. In J0006+1215, J0209+3122, J1031+2903, and J1217+0234, the residual emission is dominated by a narrow component.

gmw_00000541.name: J0209+3122
gmw_00000541.overview: J0209+3122 is an extremely red quasar hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2312.03917]. Point spread function subtraction reveals spatially resolved [O III] emission in the galaxy, which is dominated by a narrow component below the active galactic nucleus outflow threshold [arXiv:2312.03917].

sources.summary: The paper reports J0209+3122 has a spatially resolved narrow component below the outflow threshold and narrow-dominated residual [O III] emission revealed by PSF subtraction.

quotes.quote: On the contrary, all five components of the sample that are below the AGN-outflow cut are spatially resolved, namely the component 2 of J0006+1215, J0209+3122, J1031+2903, J1217+0234 and the component 3 of J1652+1728.

With the PSF subtraction method we reveal spatially resolved [ O III ] emission in J0006+1215, J0209+3122, J1031+2903, J1217+0234, J1550+0806, J1652+1728, and J2323 0100, totalling seven out of 10 ERQs. In J0006+1215, J0209+3122, J1031+2903, and J1217+0234, the residual emission is dominated by a narrow component.

gmw_00000542.name: HE1104-1805
gmw_00000542.overview: HE1104-1805 is an early-type lensing galaxy [arXiv:0709.3189, arXiv:1503.02074] at a redshift of 0.729 [arXiv:1503.02074, arXiv:2401.10071] that gravitationally lenses a background quasar [arXiv:0709.3189, arXiv:2005.13550]. The lensed system exhibits intrinsic variability [arXiv:2107.06227], variable continuum fluxes [arXiv:astro-ph/0606309], and a time delay of approximately 152 to 161 days between its two images [arXiv:1503.02074, arXiv:2401.10071].

sources.summary: The paper uses HE1104-1805 as its worked strong-lensing example, deriving the lensing galaxy mass from image positions and comparing MOND and standard lensing estimates.

quotes.quote: HE1104-1805 is extracted from the CfA-Arizona Space Telescope Survey (CASTLES ) sample. It consists of a galaxy at redshift =0.73 with a background QSO at =2.32 . A grey-scale map of the HST/NICMOS F160W image is shown on the right panel, retrieved from the CASTLES web-page http://cfa-www.harvard.edu/castles/. . This is a double system with the image positions located at 2.09 and 1.10 arcsec on either side of the lensing galaxy. The left panel of Fig. fig:HE1104 shows the correlation between the actual position of the QSO, and the total mass of the lensing galaxy, assuming a Hernquist profile with the projected 2-D half-mass radius being equal to the observed half-light radius of the lensing galaxy.

The compatible solution corresponds to the crossing of the lines, shown in the figure with a star symbol. This gives the true position of the source and the mass of the galaxy. For comparison, the values from Refs. (for conventional lensing theory) and (for MOND) are given as a shaded region and an arrow, respectively.

sources.summary: HE1104 is described as a doubly imaged quasar with detected lensed arcs; its host-light inference is consistent with earlier NICMOS modeling.

quotes.quote: HE1104 HE1104 is a typical doubly imaged quasar. We have selected in total of 5 initial PSFs to perform the fit. There is an object in the northeast. However, since we do not know its redshift and considering it is further away from the lens we do not model it explicitly but just mask it out in the fitting.

The inference is presented in Table  tab:host_measure and Figure  fig:image_inference (e). the lensed arcs can be clearly seen from the bottom-left panel, indicating that the host galaxy is well detected. It is worth noting that the HE1104 has been modelled by  Peng2006 based on the /NICMOS H-band (F160W) imaging data. Their inferred host light is 20.14 0.30 mag in Vega system, which is also consistent to our inference ( 20.00 0.15 mag in Vega).

sources.summary: The paper describes HE1104-1805 as a distinct high-separation lens requiring external shear, with low stellar-mass fraction comparable to RXJ0911 in the high-mass lens regime.

quotes.quote: HE1104 features the second highest image separation of 3.19'' and a distinct lens galaxy (the median separation is 1.5'' ). Furthermore the lens appears to be near the bright image which is rather unusual and implies the presence of a group or cluster enhancing the separation LFK00 . Parametric as well as free-form mass models also suggest that an external shear is mandatory to reproduce the image configuration e.g. wwl98 . The lens galaxy is unaffected by quasar light allowing for a good fit.

In terms of stellar-mass fraction HE1104 exhibits small values of f_s 0.07 and RXJ0911 of f_s 0.06 . In the high mass regime ( >15 10^ 10 M_ ) the range of possible stellar-mass fractions appears to be small compared to low and intermediate masses, and always close to 0.05. Those two lenses are thus representative for low f_s lenses.

sources.summary: The paper tests HE 1104-1805 data from SMARTS, Wise, and OGLE, but finds the delay sensitive to data selection and microlensing treatment, preventing improved constraints.

quotes.quote: HE 1104-1805 We used the data published by 2007ApJ...660..146P , which combine their own SMARTS R-band data with Wise R-band data from 2003ApJ...594..101O and OGLE V-band data from 2003AcA....53..229W . The three data sets are shown in Fig. fig:HE1104 . Table tab:TD_HE1104 lists the four time-delay values published for HE1104-1805, where B is the leading image. We performed tests with both methods on different combinations of the data using all telescopes or only one or two of them. Unfortunately, the results seem to be sensitive to this choice, as they are to the way in which microlensing is treated: both the OGLE and Wise data sets were analysed to find a time delay t_ BA 157 days, whereas SMARTS data converge to a higher value of t_ BA 161 days or more as is shown in Fig. HE1104_OS_AB-2_histo_sum .

In addition, 2007ApJ...660..146P 's smaller value is recovered with the MD method when including OGLE and Wise data but only for some ways of modelling microlensing.
